name: The Flyover
subject: Winter Storms, Bipartisan Love, and McRib Returns
body:
Millions of of Americans woke up Tuesday to the first real blast of winter [as a powerful Arctic front](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0M1qxCAUBeCnMTuD96r5WbgolOymm1K6DCbezDgYHVQmnbcv6St0feAcvvN9mS_WBx-vH3YnU7c0RzpKoFopz4ePlXKpKe9l8Q-bqy82hvScUaDmABxwdtaH16waZzYiJNmQgb7DUWmtx-ZmpB6URo29EKOVi3DCaUm96zs9OFy3xpuzDQAQQGg9tINbFkkbWjWodSFgSpDzNeX2nnysN9rCKz0pt2vam2ButT4Kk28MJ4aTXdZT0F7TGTOcvj4ZTlAq3-09ZV5iOnghW1Lk9FMz7cTXFBzD6YS-mJy8Y_IdsFcAo-yabNzj8CH40vVMietuffibrv_662nwNwAA__-spn5y) swept across the eastern two-thirds of the country, bringing snow, deep freezes, and biting wind from the Midwest to the Gulf Coast.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbuq3DAQhp9G7rTovt7CRYjj7gQCgVMaWR6tJ-iySFqbffvgXEiaVJly-OabGf73t_nNYsB0_2wjDM3nOcFRA7QGZT4wNSi15RLrgg9bGlabQt5nwYSmnFMu5tVieM2qWwcPIEB2MPCrETeltb512wDgOWjZm8Urvnitr8wwvzimrPG29x0Op41zLjhnWveXfl0WCV5Y1Su3ACeKwYotl8u3jKlt4MMr71AuLscuDFtrj0rkByImIqbjOC4FGhaIkNqKO5SKHp1tmBOFaDGcc0RMfyiKqT6LTQ5oyOlOOZNM6NMnJ5eIHHutqblpqm6CEWFCJXJkjNNPp45-3YBOP08iwlTvcCVyvDL-ZWNnwfvHqSfCPFucnY0Pi_f0TyDCis9I5PjD_atZ87M4IHL8a1VXhvVxYAhYzZUodv_9Wtf-K8d9EN8DAAD__1Y4sEQ) | |
# Wednesday, November 12, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day 1966, astronaut Buzz Aldrin made history on a spacewalk during the Gemini 12 mission by taking the first-ever space selfie. [You can see it here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGSpCAQgOGnwUzLbtGRgOASs7n0QgukmekrBAvYsZyn35p9hc3_-oL_3329Gw4cH3_NTrr6tEY6S6BaKa8nx0q51JT3YvkwuXIxMaTXij2OLUALuDrD4Vpl47QnQhoa0nCbUMlxHFXz1JtS4EHNytGghpuUBmyPciM7mU3ZqWH90QAAAfpxnLvZWTuQRyNnuVkCIXtyXFPu_ieO9Uk-XOlFudvS3gT9rPUoYvgjcBG4nOfZ2euRIoXrEwhc7Nf73ZrgMsfWcy61LYfZqC0UPJPApcnaHSeHwGW6Cdk_dsPhR6-_WvLS-B0AAP__hG93aw). Today’s _Flyover_ spotlights couples who’ve learned to navigate [love across the partisan divide](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGupDAMxvHThM4jEhLeTEGxDd3bdkvkEDN4FRKUeEDcfsVe4TVuLP306f_ne_pGjpzev3GjQZY8JTprJBEq08lJqFTJZauedyzCFVPMx2Ra40Br0GYKyPGabBOGhchQ19Cgv3rzss65V7MOOnRth23_ROt6tL7rqbXOd87rF1mHDQ-3prU2WrfOPR_P4H1Hi0H7tLMnrWxLgSWXx9_MSVZa4pUPKo85b00cVpG9qu6XMqMyY7r2XOV-KTPesDKj1vdplRkjL1TliqTMyBsgBNryXFCAE8R8EJwsKyAU2j8-8owJNiIBWQnm_NkjVTjXDFgI7iCQl1u5OL3BF6KgzNiUIewnx8i1_1K2fW_I8f9Y-VHhYzD_AgAA___uf5CR). Maybe there’s a lesson there for the rest of the country. Are you a part of a bipartisan couple? Reply to this email to let us know. Before the next economic shock hits, today's sponsor, [GoldCo](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbuq3DAQhp9G7rTovt7CRYjj7gQCgVMaWR6tJ-iySFqbffvgXEiaVJly-OabGf73t_nNYsB0_2wjDM3nOcFRA7QGZT4wNSi15RLrgg9bGlabQt5nwYSmnFMu5tVieM2qWwcPIEB2MPCrETeltb512wDgOWjZm8Urvnitr8wwvzimrPG29x0Op41zLjhnWveXfl0WCV5Y1Su3ACeKwYotl8u3jKlt4MMr71AuLscuDFtrj0rkByImIqbjOC4FGhaIkNqKO5SKHp1tmBOFaDGcc0RMfyiKqT6LTQ5oyOlOOZNM6NMnJ5eIHHutqblpqm6CEWFCJXJkjNNPp45-3YBOP08iwlTvcCVyvDL-ZWNnwfvHqSfCPFucnY0Pi_f0TyDCis9I5PjD_atZ87M4IHL8a1VXhvVxYAhYzZUodv_9Wtf-K8d9EN8DAAD__1Y4sEQ), wants Americans to be aware of a little-known strategy that can serve as a form of “retirement insurance” and help protect their savings when it matters most.
|
---
## First Major Winter Storm Hits US
Millions of Americans woke up Tuesday to the first real blast of winter [as a powerful Arctic front](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0M1qxCAUBeCnMTuD96r5WbgolOymm1K6DCbezDgYHVQmnbcv6St0feAcvvN9mS_WBx-vH3YnU7c0RzpKoFopz4ePlXKpKe9l8Q-bqy82hvScUaDmABxwdtaH16waZzYiJNmQgb7DUWmtx-ZmpB6URo29EKOVi3DCaUm96zs9OFy3xpuzDQAQQGg9tINbFkkbWjWodSFgSpDzNeX2nnysN9rCKz0pt2vam2ButT4Kk28MJ4aTXdZT0F7TGTOcvj4ZTlAq3-09ZV5iOnghW1Lk9FMz7cTXFBzD6YS-mJy8Y_IdsFcAo-yabNzj8CH40vVMietuffibrv_662nwNwAA__-spn5y) swept across the eastern two-thirds of the country, bringing snow, deep freezes, and biting wind from the Midwest to the Gulf Coast. Over a foot of lake-effect snow buried parts of Indiana, Illinois, Wisconsin, and Michigan, while flakes flew as far south as Nashville, Myrtle Beach, and even the Florida Panhandle. The cold snap even triggered [falling-iguana alerts in Florida](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO6yAQRb_G7og8GBxcULwmXV67pTWGIZ4VBgtIrPz9KvsLW1_pHp3zdV_uyJHT4z_uZFvIS6KzRmqNynJyalRqy2WvKx9YGldMMb8WOUgtAATIxSPH96J6bwORpLEnC9dJzkprPfebHSi4yTs1zmYdcdIOtYd5VGGYJmMc9Gw_bwAgAQatzcX4dR0pSFRGuZWgUwN5brlcvjOntlGI7_yicnF576PdWjtqN_7r5K2Tt4PyEekzdfIWMH7cBD-emFCc2NwmuNYnecFJhJgLexS4sxcuRy9CyakJAKOuSqm-WH-cHCPX6dqp4bEjx19q-1Oql5U_AQAA__-1Pn73), where the invasive reptiles can go limp and tumble from trees when temperatures drop below 40. More than [1,000 flights were canceled](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL3O2zAMRZ9G3hhY8k-cwUMXb1_XjgZtUTELRjIkOqn79IUL9Am6EAQu7gHO_fE1fyELx-d3fNGoIc2RPkVIlfL84aiUi6b8KgvvmJULRknv2dWuA2vButkjyzm3lR8DkaOmotHee_dou657VNv4aKwj8mRD3y8PTwPavul6v3RDfV_RVjxeNGuts7buuuE2-GVpKDhsh3ZdyJq2Js-a8u1n4qgbBTnTm_JtTa9Kxk11L6b5Ztxk3BTPPRW9IuOmC2zcZO2_cxS4_IybgvBzU1gxriSonGIB-rXTquRBE2QuBHpQ8XjCET1lCIiQ8vVhgZCJfnN8wodQN8qwpkM8oJQEKx5XOeObBDYSMW6q8uj3D4tw6e-mrZ8vZPlroP81-3t0fwIAAP__xpaaJg) and 600 were delayed by Tuesday morning, as winter weather collided with problems caused by the ongoing government shutdown.
## Trump Seeks Name On New Stadium
President Trump is reportedly seeking to have the new $3.7 billion stadium for the NFL's Washington Commanders [named after him](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuOpDAQAL8GMo_oxuYROLhksr30QmRwe-iTH8juHbR_f9r7hY1LKpXqz8f24Thyfv12iayEsmW6WyQRqtvNWag2KTW1nS9XhZvLsbw3HNAoAAW4ecfxa9O9t4EIaezJwjzhqo0xa39aF_SK6z4CzngcYOYwGtA4jOj1tIapZ_ttAwAEGIxZHovf95ECOr3oYyfo9ECepdTH38JZTgrxq7ypPo6S-mhPkat1468Onx0-LypXpG_U4VPqZ7rU7bI0JSep27WT80tKVkdJyWVPtalMt2riPH8mlV0ir1wQqurkpCpdpYoCWPQ8LHNfrb9ujpHbNHd6eCXH8X-H_Gje2-K_AAAA__-mWYW7). The White House Press Secretary [Karoline Leavitt said](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuO8yAUhJ_G7ojgAL4UFH-TLn-7pXVsDjErLhaQWHn7lfcVthyNNJrv-3osD_TBp-d_jGSay0uiswZqjcpy-tSo1JZLrKs_sDRfMYX8XoCDZkIwAYtFHz6L6q1xRECyJyPGAWaltZ773Uip-TTOI9oBOK6jQD5KQD4QzBZJ9t5ca0IIEIJrPd0mu66SHKCa1LaS6BQn61sut-_sU9vJhU9-U7ltOfbB7K0dtZP_Orh3cMfjAriqK5Tmt0Ad3Ft5xYOdWHefni0ntuUYMVkqldWG1r8iSxiJJReYBZhXQidBoFMOLTiNJId1Ahg3qfpi7HH6EHwdxk7xZ0Qfft-0Pyl8G_gJAAD__1tChpE) that naming it after Trump “would surely be a beautiful name, as it was President Trump who made the rebuilding of the new stadium possible.” Reports say Trump has made this [request via back-channel communications](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2O6yAURleDO1twDY5dULyGLq-d0sLmOr4jfiwgQdn9KLOFqT_p0znn677eLXmKj_82oK5HWiO24rFWzGujWDGXmnIoG102Vyo2-vRagYPqhegFrM6Sf6-yc_pABBw71OI2wSKVUkt3ainkjbt9BsWdU-PENwUc7SitW45tnDvSnzchBAjBlZqH2W3biAdYOct9Q8EkR0c15eE7UawnHv6dXpiHPYXO67PWq7DxHwPDwLTWBixX_IwMTDw8A_NReDMwKwNDjoGR07yAEIqBqfkZrr7ZWEu_pxBsdJhLX6p19Ax9tAFdf1LosnZXI--pTDcm-SNY8r8I9U_dXhp-AgAA___84oI3) with the team’s owner, Josh Harris. The Commanders currently hold the naming rights for the stadium, and could sell it to a corporate sponsor or offer it to the president.
## China Bridge Collapses in Landslide
A 2,500-foot highway bridge in China’s Sichuan province collapsed Tuesday after a landslide slammed the mountainside where it stood. [See the video here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFy6yAQgOHToM4e7QJCKiheo86vTalBsLI3g4QHNnZ8-4xzhbR_8RX_x2W5BM58XP-HnbxsZTno2TKJUF2efAjVJqXubeV7qMItHLk8FuzRngBOgEsKnF-L6ZLfiJB0Rx7cgJOx1k7dzaNzOkIkZ6JB66KNSSecXL_REMwwdOzfGgAgQG_teB7TumraMJjRxJVAmZ4SS6nnz8KH3GjLr_Kgeo5l77K_idyb0v8Uzgrn73dVOLPCuUmQr6ZwhmkcEcFp58BpMzjXd9Wn-5Nz5jY4ZfrrHjj_ivKnDQ-PPwEAAP__C_Fuog). The Hongqi Bridge had just [opened earlier this year](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1y5CAQRk8jZaig9TNSQLDJZLPphioELdFrBDK0h5rbu8ZXcP7VV--9f4_1YShQPP6aEzXvaY1YS0BmzGulyJgLp3yWjS6TmYqJIT1XkDAKpYSC1RkKr3Vond4RAfsWtbpNsAzjOC6t17NdrIWbNYCml9Nil36zm-wlLgNOc9-Sfr8ppUApOY5zN7tt63EHM8yD3VA1g0RHnHL3P1Fkj3t4pSfmzqazDdozX6Xp_zRwb-Bea-3eChXx4z1o4O5TPD5JbJncgYKisJ6iETaFYK6CRZwpsi_C7IxZ2BQL5y_LlKLYKVLx6IRSEuZllG3W7qoUApXp1gzyOA2FHxD-Vb2nhu8AAAD__0R3hzA) to link China’s interior with the Tibetan Plateau. Police shut down traffic Monday after spotting slope cracks. The next day, part of the bridge [gave way and tumbled](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGupDAQRE9jMo_oBg9M4GATstl0Q2RwA70yNmu3QHP7FV_6J_hJJSU9Vb0_7_HtOHBcf7udrCxpjHSVQCKUx4ujUC6S8l4mPlwWLi6GdI5Yo9EAGnD0jsNnbCtvFyKkpiIL3RNfrTHmVW22AW-gA-yJ5hag9rhQb16wNM-ugQkqtjcNABCgNqZ_9H6aGlrQtX07TwSqrcmzpPz4mzjKRkv4pJPyY057FewmchTV_FI4KBzi50hF7krhcIMVDgDfcaUcvL4vKhzmjaMrektx_cd6yuxX0nMKwR2FiuaoM5-U9Z6ibEW7RSjrTOmgyHHVJ3tKCocqW39cHAKXZ6faet0dh69t8iOhp8X_AQAA___JE4sx) into the river below. No injuries were reported. The incident comes just months after another high-profile failure in August, when a railway bridge under construction in Qinghai province collapsed, killing at least 12 workers and leaving four others missing.
* * *

_Flying together with our sponsor: Logical Health Alternatives_ **➤ In 1918, this “miracle medicine”** was found in nearly every American household—then it quietly disappeared. clean_body: Millions of of Americans woke up Tuesday to the first real blast of winter swept across the eastern two-thirds of the country, bringing snow, deep freezes, and biting wind from the Midwest to the Gulf Coast. | | | | | | Wednesday, November 12, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day 1966, astronaut Buzz Aldrin made history on a spacewalk during the Gemini 12 mission by taking the first-ever space selfie. . Today’s Flyover spotlights couples who’ve learned to navigate . Maybe there’s a lesson there for the rest of the country. Are you a part of a bipartisan couple? Reply to this email to let us know. Before the next economic shock hits, today's sponsor, , wants Americans to be aware of a little-known strategy that can serve as a form of “retirement insurance” and help protect their savings when it matters most. | First Major Winter Storm Hits US Millions of Americans woke up Tuesday to the first real blast of winter swept across the eastern two-thirds of the country, bringing snow, deep freezes, and biting wind from the Midwest to the Gulf Coast. Over a foot of lake-effect snow buried parts of Indiana, Illinois, Wisconsin, and Michigan, while flakes flew as far south as Nashville, Myrtle Beach, and even the Florida Panhandle. The cold snap even triggered , where the invasive reptiles can go limp and tumble from trees when temperatures drop below 40. More than and 600 were delayed by Tuesday morning, as winter weather collided with problems caused by the ongoing government shutdown. Trump Seeks Name On New Stadium President Trump is reportedly seeking to have the new $3.7 billion stadium for the NFL's Washington Commanders . The White House Press Secretary that naming it after Trump “would surely be a beautiful name, as it was President Trump who made the rebuilding of the new stadium possible.” Reports say Trump has made this with the team’s owner, Josh Harris. The Commanders currently hold the naming rights for the stadium, and could sell it to a corporate sponsor or offer it to the president. China Bridge Collapses in Landslide A 2,500-foot highway bridge in China’s Sichuan province collapsed Tuesday after a landslide slammed the mountainside where it stood. . The Hongqi Bridge had just to link China’s interior with the Tibetan Plateau. Police shut down traffic Monday after spotting slope cracks. The next day, part of the bridge into the river below. No injuries were reported. The incident comes just months after another high-profile failure in August, when a railway bridge under construction in Qinghai province collapsed, killing at least 12 workers and leaving four others missing. ## Camp Mystic Families File Lawsuits
Families of the 25 campers and two counselors who died at Camp Mystic during the Fourth of July flash floods have filed at least four lawsuits against the camp and its owners, [alleging](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuy4yAMhp8Gd2RAdnwpKLZxd7bd0iOMiJkjQgZIHL_9jrOzT3AaNbp8-v4_X8sXBg7322-MZOrbp-VOe2GqlfKyYnzEo9Swlie5iK9yiz5kcgsouEqtpYbFYeBj6RpnrHKTbsjooYepG8exbzZz7XvfWVStXVsclMIeRz8MvbNX1_Z2aoI5j2mtQYPScL1MqrPT2MJKnbdaWdEpcqGmfKkbVXpj8XykF-XLmmLDZqv1UUT7S8AsYN73_fJdsJ5NAfNpI2DmtCILmE-UgFnr_8VjDByoyPIkefrKf8LyBEhH6PiQW2CWa3reaz6k55RckXXDKr8DMzkJw2eVcvlMFeKUT2qTjXvsgTmUfhCdukUM_Pm6_izrl4G_AQAA__83oJSl) "gross negligence and reckless disregard for safety." The largest lawsuit, filed by seven of the victims' families, [claims](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGuozAQxvHTQGdkD06CCxfb0GXbLZHBYzLSGCPsQLj9irwjvHqkv-b3_XsOT0dMy_zXRbTlE9Kw4JEZS8FtmFxc45kLTfmNPro9zzHQhn4ACTehlFAweEd8Drr2dpTeqBqtetzB6K7r7vXL-kkGGN3opWrBGY1GOt1JbaBFkOhrsldMKQUKpIJbY6QeTdfChDqMSo6VluippK0pLyz4cTnwmXbcminFmu2rlDVX7Z8K-gr64ziakD6X4rpX0L9zBX1wkZgwi_xGcbnED0xcIeHReT7FNy4Cp-SFY8YZxYIz04zLhGLdUqAiYiq0Y71Zvx7ETPn-qLScoyP-PlR-N-Nu4X8AAAD__99biuA) the camp "put profit over safety" in dismissing concerns about placing its cabins in a flood zone, as well as failing to adopt a legally required evacuation plan and ignoring warnings about the impending disaster. The families [called](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L3upCAUBfCn0Q4Dd-TDgmIbu9l2S3OFi5KAToTRmbffuPsI__7knPzOn-f0xJjitvzGTLZ-wj5tdJVEtdIxOcyv_C01uvImn_EsSw7xID8BB8mEYAImjzF9p771duZ-EC1ZoRUMvTFGtasdlApaBuQaSUsFTnojgxpQoXHGyDbau0wIAQK4ANkNvJ8H8wBHfZgFn5uek491P7q6UqUPlpC--0lH5_bcJrvW-irN41cDYwPjdV1d2D8a36XG7Y40MN6mBsaV8KStMNAs4VXesbKbyP4b2XLspbCNlhQX2hy1h_WvK6YUi9JNz5eMMf0brT-76rTwNwAA__-3yX-3) the deaths of 27 campers and counselors an "entirely preventable tragedy." They are seeking jury trials and millions in damages. "_The camp is ready to move on, but these girls and their parents first deserve transparency and justice_ ," the same lawsuit states. ## Texas Southern University Under Investigation
Gov. Greg Abbott has directed the Texas Rangers to investigate Texas Southern University for [potential misuse of public funds](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbGynDAMRb_GdN7BWligoEhD99KmZASWQfOMzdgC3v59hs0npFElXZ0z98_X-IXsOSy_caNeflwcA13ZkwilccZt395ZeM4H2Q3PvGyOE9kRSqi1MdrAaJH9e6wK20-l7UxBvWle0FVt276KtW_r59Q56qhF6MhMWNfoXFU3rpksVk3B_R1mjAEDpYH60ZXV1LVPmKlykyknVZVkWWJ6yEpCP5idf8eT0mOOW-H7VWTP6vlLwaBguK7rMXuev2GNR5YY7i0Fw62lYPBxRq9guH8qGIy5R6lg-ARrL3qJp7YY9I6SeP7WM3qftYtJczgpCy8oHIPmIFH_u8rxkJVS0Efgk1JmeesbUO9RKAij1xvnI5OOTmdBIe2OYG-eIvV2v9h7zq9GVeWyIfuPmPxfHWcPfwMAAP__TZ6jmA), following the state auditor's identification of thousands of unauthorized invoices from the past year, totaling more than $400 million. On Monday, the state auditor [provided an update](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQhZ_GdI48g_lJQbEN3d12S2TsIYxk7AgPJLz9KnmFW5-j7xx9_36mH8eR0-Ov22iQ95KnRK8SSYT2ybvtuV1F2JeDwubO8tgW3ilMaLDRABpwCo7jNdkqDLMJd6hogK7Fu-37vq3WwTnTNV1X1372xvZo2w4XaDvfNh5qX1c8fGAAgIAGsLndjZ3vfY2e7DKDmZU1FFjyfpOVhN6uLPHKJ-03n7cqDqvIs6j6j8JR4ehmD_UnUTg-cxGFo3cx6hfLuuYY9HKkwOmhvyRd8iEr7UkfiU_aC8ulizgh7Y7vqF44haKtMXrjGDknzenM7KkoHKEHi01vFI7VPoTn61MpbaeseWyO4_ei_E7sOeD_AAAA___FlI31) regarding a review of TSU's finances, revealing that she had identified 743 invoices totaling $282.2 million from vendors with expired contracts and 8,144 invoices totaling $158 million dated before their requisitions. ## Mavs GM Nico Harrison Fired
The Dallas Mavericks [fired general manager Nico Harrison](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL_O2zAMxJ9G3hSItOM_g4Yu2r6uHQ1aohMhkhVIiv357QsXaF-gCwmQh8Pv7tfX_EU--O3xkyLr-r2meeOjBK6V82wpvuNZqrflwy7SXh5x9ZndjArvEkACzo58OOeucXpRboKGNQw9Tt04jn3z1LQO7XJvEWEcJ8VjR62aBurWRUHf99R4fZkBAAIqwPttUt0yjS1a7tYF1CI6xc7XlG_1yZW_qazhTDvnm02xCfpZ67uI9odAI9Acx3H7FKrJ0XkJBJpSUz6v_U65FoFmW0igibRz9vZ1XS4EgQbg7_j3lFdguXmb5JNy9iVt0qbNsmMZPi-SLm3WWxl9qfRigWYcEIZ2uCs1CDRN1u59-BB86QfRqUckH_6Q1__re9f4OwAA__8M0JMK) yesterday after a 3-8 start to the season, including a 116-114 loss to the Milwaukee Bucks the night before. Harrison's departure comes nine months after a controversial trade sent generational star Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers for injury-prone center Anthony Davis. The trade [caused an uproar](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2upSAUBeCnwQ4D-3D9KSimsbvTTmm2sFUy_Bjg6PHtJ84r3PpLVtZaf77nb3Texe03BtL1s6Y50lU81Up5NhiOcJfqTHmTDXiWLawuk51BwBeXkkuYLTp_z6qxehF2lA1p2XcwqmEYumbXi-hlN_YvZQaQQOuIol8Be0W4isHKxuknTEoJEoSEr3YUahmHFxhS6yLFwpQg62rKbd2p0gfL6u90Um5NCo3Xe61HYa9fDCYG03VdLZUjPshgigsymEpN-WYwzQwmZxlMqhth6BU8lt7ZUOEBT8rO_C2cPgeZSpY_W_lGkTJ6HjDiRplHZxLfMWdXUmyytsflvHel65kSW0Dn__eqP3vz1PAvAAD__009ihA) among Mavericks fans, who have continued to chant "Fire Nico!" during the team's home games. Dumont initially defended Harrison's decision to let Doncic go. In an [open letter](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L-utCAUBPCn0Y4NHFCxoPgau_3aW5ojHJUsiAH239vf7L7C7SeT-c3Pdb6iD_7Y_mMkU19rmg96lkC1Up4txjO-S_W23MlFfJQtrj6Tm4FDx4RgAmaHPrxn1TqzcDeKlowYehiV1rpvdwMS0OpVyUWhk4vsbYer1Oh4N1jkuvXmUyaEAAFcQHcZuVpGLcGSWhfBl0Zxcr6mfKk7VXphWcM7PShfbIptMHutZ2nkvwamBqYlENqdcqYz5fqJNDBhrt4GKg1M0AktxdCxw9vEdszZl3SwL4t9iGyLLKaj7oXhWimzmtH5Y2PhfkPm0mG9ZQFvlEubjTufPgRf-qFRfIvow3dV_duXDwO_AQAA___ikotW) to Mavericks fans, Dumont said he understood "the profound impact these difficult last several months have had" and thanked them for "holding us accountable," adding that he's "fully committed to the success of the Mavericks."

Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Camp Mystic Families File Lawsuits Families of the 25 campers and two counselors who died at Camp Mystic during the Fourth of July flash floods have filed at least four lawsuits against the camp and its owners, "gross negligence and reckless disregard for safety." The largest lawsuit, filed by seven of the victims' families, the camp "put profit over safety" in dismissing concerns about placing its cabins in a flood zone, as well as failing to adopt a legally required evacuation plan and ignoring warnings about the impending disaster. The families the deaths of 27 campers and counselors an "entirely preventable tragedy." They are seeking jury trials and millions in damages. "The camp is ready to move on, but these girls and their parents first deserve transparency and justice ," the same lawsuit states. In a statement, Camp Mystic said, “ We continue to pray for the grieving families.” Its attorneys the allegations as "categorically false" and they "intend to demonstrate and prove that this sudden surge of floodwaters far exceeded any previous flood." Texas Southern University Under Investigation Gov. Greg Abbott has directed the Texas Rangers to investigate Texas Southern University for , following the state auditor's identification of thousands of unauthorized invoices from the past year, totaling more than $400 million. On Monday, the state auditor regarding a review of TSU's finances, revealing that she had identified 743 invoices totaling $282.2 million from vendors with expired contracts and 8,144 invoices totaling $158 million dated before their requisitions. "I don’t want one more taxpayer dollar spent until this issue is resolved ," Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick said. Hours later, Abbott ordered a criminal investigation into the university's finances. "Waste, fraud, and abuse will not be tolerated ," . In a statement, TSU said it has and "enacted corrective measures" to address the deficiencies identified in the report, including the implementation of a new procurement system. Mavs GM Nico Harrison Fired The Dallas Mavericks yesterday after a 3-8 start to the season, including a 116-114 loss to the Milwaukee Bucks the night before. Harrison's departure comes nine months after a controversial trade sent generational star Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers for injury-prone center Anthony Davis. The trade among Mavericks fans, who have continued to chant "Fire Nico!" during the team's home games. On Monday night, team governor Patrick Dumont was seen wearing a Luka Doncic Lakers jersey, who shared that Dumont admitted trading the Slovenian star was a mistake. Dumont initially defended Harrison's decision to let Doncic go. In an to Mavericks fans, Dumont said he understood "the profound impact these difficult last several months have had" and thanked them for "holding us accountable," adding that he's "fully committed to the success of the Mavericks." | Flying together with our sponsor Recycling 400+ waste streams others won't process partners with hundreds of major brands, including Coca-Cola, Gillette, Whole Foods, and Taco Bell, to recycle a wide range of products, such as razors, sauce packets, and coffee pods. TerraCycle has been profitable for over 10 years, transforming their mission-driven recycling solutions into revenue-driving success. Their business model has seen , hitting $45M last year. First-half 2025 revenue is up 11% vs. last year. And they’re dominating the search engines for recycling solutions. Go ahead and look up how to recycle any hard-to-recycle trash, like “recycle cigarette butts” or “recycle contact lenses,” and see who shows up. Early backers have already seen 17-20% of their investment capital back in dividends. Now it’s your turn. . | ➤ Attorney General Ken Paxton is suing Austin-based nonprofit Jolt Initiative for allegedly organizing a voter registration scheme to allow illegal immigrants to vote. He is also for allocating more than $1.3 million to provide legal services to illegal immigrants facing deportation. () ➤ Lubbock: U.S. Rep. Jodey Arrington, the chairman of the House Budget Committee, announced he will not run for reelection next year. The Republican was first elected to the U.S. House in 2016. () ➤ Polk County: Residents previously ordered to evacuate due to a breach at the Carter Lake Dam have been cleared to return home after the lake's water level was lowered below the point of damage to the embankment. () ➤ Fort Hood: Army Maj. Blaine McGraw, the OB-GYN suspended last month from the on-base clinic over accusations he secretly recorded patients during exams, was sued Monday by an alleged victim. The lawsuit also accused the Army of dismissing previous complaints against McGraw. () ➤ Josephine: The Muslim-centric EPIC City development has been renamed The Meadow to alleviate "public confusion over whether the development would become a separate municipality." The development has been the subject of multiple investigations over the past year. () ➤ U.S. Sens. Ted Cruz and John Cornyn have filed legislation to enforce the terms of a 1944 treaty that requires Mexico to transfer 1.75 million acre-feet of water to the United States every five years. Mexico was 865,136 acre-feet short of its obligation for the recently concluded cycle. () ➤ Anderson County: Dallas businessman Kyle Bass, the CEO of Conservation Equity Management, is suing the Neches & Trinity Valleys Groundwater Conservation District for invalidating his company's permit application for 43 high-capacity water wells in response to a lawsuit from a local poultry farm. () Enjoy reading The Texas Flyover?mailto:?cc=editor@thetexasflyover.com&subject=My%20new%20favorite%20news%20source&body=Hey!%0A%0AI%E2%80%99ve%20been%20reading%20a%20new%20newsletter%20every%20morning%20called%20The%20Texas%20Flyover.%20I%20love%20it!%20I%20think%20you'll%20love%20it%20too.%0A%0AIt%E2%80%99s%20my%20new%20favorite%20source%20for%20Texas%20news.%20They%20cover%20everything%20happening%20around%20the%20state%E2%80%94usually%20politics%2C%20sports%2C%20finance%2C%20science%20%26%20tech%2C%20etc.%0A%0AI%20think%20you%20should%20give%20it%20a%20try.%0A%0AJust%20copy%20this%20link%20and%20paste%20it%20into%20your%20browser%3A%20https%3A%2F%2Fthetexasflyover.com%2F%3Futm%3DDL%0A%0ALet%20me%20know%20if%20you%20like%20it.%20I%20think%20you%20will! to share with your friends and family. ✈️ | ➤ The Texas Longhorns held onto their position as the No. 4 team in the country in the AP Top 25 women's college basketball poll, while Baylor jumped up nine spots to No. 7. TCU was the only other Texas team, coming in at No. 17. () ➤ Thirteen football players received suspensions following Texas State's postgame brawl with Louisiana on Saturday. Louisiana safety Tyree Skipper was suspended for the rest of the season. () ➤ Texas led all states with six total bids to the NCAA women’s soccer tournament. TCU, Baylor, UTSA, Texas State, Texas Tech, and Houston Christian all made the cut. () Yesterday’s Results: | | | | | Flying together with our sponsor ➤ Protect your wallet and identity with the compact, , which slides effortlessly into your wallet and uses advanced electromagnetic shielding to instantly block hidden RFID skimmers from accessing your contactless cards and passport information. () | ➤ Galveston's Gulf Copper & Manufacturing Corporation, which is being acquired by Canadian shipbuilding company Davie, is expanding its partnership with Mississippi-based Ingalls Shipbuilding to manufacture additional structural units for the U.S. Navy's Flight III Arleigh Burke-class destroyers. () ➤ Tesla aims to remove "safety riders" from its Robotaxis in Austin by the end of the year as it plans to expand service to Dallas and Houston. Meanwhile, Waymo recently announced its driverless taxis will be available in Dallas next year. () ➤ The company leading the development of Texas A&M's RELLIS Campus has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after a major investor withdrew from the project. () ➤ The Terrell City Council has approved a massive $3 billion master-planned community after resident opposition prompted members to in September. The 1,545-acre Terra Nova development will include 3,600 single-family homes, 1,200 multi-family units, and a 48-acre mixed-use district. () Flying together with our sponsor ➤ The #1 weight‐loss quiz you’ll wish you took sooner. Answer a few simple questions and unlock your . This personalized quiz connects you to tailored treatment options and expert guidance for real results. () | Flying together with our sponsor The world’s biggest brands turn to TerraCycle In a world where 95% of waste isn’t recycled, TerraCycle builds for “unrecyclable” products and packages by working with hundreds of major brands, like Coca-Cola, Gillette, Whole Foods, and Taco Bell , to recycle razors, sauce packets, coffee pods, and so much more. With over 8 billion items kept out of landfills, they’re becoming the world’s go-to name in recycling. It’s fueling TerraCycle, with 75% revenue growth since 2020. Previous investors have already seen returns of 17-20% . Now’s your turn. . This is a paid advertisement for TerraCycle’s Regulation CF offering. Please read the offering circular at | ➤ Austin is the No. 1 city in the U.S. for veterans, according to a recently published WalletHub study that examined factors such as livability, affordability, income growth, and access to VA health clinics. () ➤ Researchers at Texas A&M University have developed the first nationwide framework to help the National Park Service manage feral hog populations based on environmental conditions, legal requirements, and visitor use. The framework will be tested in Texas, Tennessee, and Hawaii before being rolled out nationwide. () ➤ Concord Church in Dallas distributed $2,000 checks to members who work for the federal government on Sunday after being asked to write their names on a prayer list. "We want you to know your church loves you," Pastor Bryan Carter said. () ➤ Houstonians experienced 77 hours of travel delays due to traffic congestion last year, according to a by the Texas A&M Transportation Institute. Congestion cost commuters in DFW, Austin, and San Antonio 69, 64, and 48 hours of travel delays, respectively. name: Elise Stefanik
subject: Bad news for the worst governor in America
body:
Major 2026 Announcement

| | | | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/btWm/?__vbtrk=MzMyOTgxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMyOTgxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

Friend, I just made a major announcement and I wanted to tell you first: **I’m in.**

name: Kevin Sorbo
subject: America needs strong and courageous boys again
body:
America needs strong and courageous boys who grow into strong and courageous men of honor.

| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z35gtfbe8kudjr28f1v9ht3s4p6uthrim8gok7q8_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cjhm6o76cth2bs)
---
Dear Fellow Conservative, I'm reaching out to you today because I think you and I agree. America needs strong boys again. America needs courageous boys again. America needs strong and courageous boys who grow into strong and courageous men of honor. But we will never get back to being a nation that values men and the unique role God created for them unless each of us steps up to do our part right now. **[That's why I'm asking you to please join me in supporting BRAVE books in the next 24 hours to get God's Word, "Be strong and courageous!" to a boy who longs to hear that message. ](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zi168326d41pl1tnq8rv16rtnpa5thsoivr2u4l0_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cjhm6o76cth2bs)** Because right now a little boy is sitting in a classroom or a library story hour with a pink-haired they/them teacher or librarian. He dreams about hitting the homerun to win the game for his team, or coming screaming in on a firetruck to save everyone from a burning building, or of rescuing the princess from the evil enemy. But his woke teachers and librarians tell him those ideas are "toxic." They tell him he must be meek. He must be quiet. He must be more like the girls. And after they teach him to act like a girl, they suggest that maybe he IS a girl. What's going on with our boys at the hands of radical "educators" is as evil as it sounds. Back when you and I were growing up boys climbed trees, skinned their knees, carried slingshots in back pockets, played knights and pirates with sticks as swords, then got cleaned up on Sunday to sit before our Lord. Our teachers and librarians fed our imaginations with stories of heroism and adventure: The Three Musketeers, Paul Bunyan, Treasure Island, White Fang, The Swiss Family Robertson, and so much more... But today, radical "educators" are bubble-wrapping our boys and pushing a feminized "woke" agenda on them.
---
| | They bring in men wearing full makeup, dresses, and high heels for "Drag Queen Story Hours" and read books like these to children of all ages... _How To Be a Real Man._ Aimed at kids aged 3-7 and featuring a bearded man in a pink tutu, this "must-have" picture book 'smashes the age-old assumption' about masculinity.

Parents protesting this book have been thrown out of school board meetings because they attempted to read excerpts aloud and the material was deemed too pornographic for a school board meeting.

No more! And maybe you've heard of the book, Gender Queer. Parents protesting this book have been thrown out of school board meetings because they attempted to read excerpts aloud and the material was deemed too pornographic for a school board meeting.

[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z66kdet0oo6jqqnufmvl6g1hcu9drlkkbmkm55kg_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cjhm6o76cth2bs)
---
| | But the book is O.K. for middle school libraries?? Crazy! I wish I could tell you that this was only happening in the most radicalized communities in our nation. But you know that's not true. Every night on the news you see another story of a boy who wants to play on a girls' team, or a boy who wants to use the girl's locker room. Or a boy who's turned his confusion and frustration into violence. I hope you see that we've got to step in and stop this madness for all our sakes. We've got to fill boys with Godly lessons that teach them to step into their roles as strong and courageous leaders in our country and in our families. But the Left has had a monopoly on the leftist messages that swamp our boys in school, movies, TV, and now even in sports. Until now. **[I'm asking for your support because a small Christian book publisher aptly named BRAVE Books needs it immediately.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zne9pbq1fqs77u9glbf59d6osf43rkm6tqk77nio_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cjhm6o76cth2bs)** Started by a father who had his first child and couldn't believe the destructive messages that were being thrown at young kids through books and television... **BRAVE Books** now battles leftist "educators," the radicals at Scholastic Books, Hollywood, Marxist librarians, drag queens, and all the rest by creating the high-quality, wholesome, Godly books, and entertainment that conservative parents need to raise brave boys who grow into men of honor. Books like: _The Night the Snow Monster Attacked_ by General Michael Flynn - Showing kids how to be good and strong leaders. _Unmuzzle Me, Please!_ By Rogan O'Handley (aka DC Draino)- Teaching kids the value of free speech. _Paws Off My Cannon_ by Dana Loesch - Explaining to kids why it's important to protect others. And many others that teach the truth about gender identity, the importance of humility not pride, and on and on. I was honored when BRAVE Books asked me to write a book with them that inspires boys to be courageous. My name is Kevin Sorbo and maybe you recognize me from the 1990's TV show "Hercules" or from my work in inspirational movies like "God's Not Dead" and "The American Miracle." But you've probably also seen me on Fox News, Real America's Voice, or Newsmax calling out the Left and their lies. Facebook banned me for telling the truth about Covid, I get daily online hate for daring to say that boys can't be girls, and Hollywood canceled me long ago for being outspoken about my conservative and Christian values. I wear all that as a badge of honor. **Because being a lion in this culture is way more important to me than being a sheep. ** **That 's why my BRAVE Book is called ****The Test of Lionhood****.**
---
| | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z1puvr5pbcul8o2bjgctgq8nouio3a5178pb4evo_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cjhm6o76cth2bs)
---
| It teaches boys to face obstacles with grit and determination. It teaches boys to be brave. It teaches boys to be men. The pink-haired radicals at Scholastic Books would never publish something so overtly masculine for boys. But **BRAVE Books** is unafraid.
---

Since you're an informed patriot, you may have heard of **BRAVE** 's "See You at the Library" movement.
---
| | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zdtc0frfbor92dfp6c1ic5kgajhgtmsm3fvpbd48_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cjhm6o76cth2bs)

| It started when **BRAVE Books** stood up to the awful anti-God, anti-family, anti-America Drag Queen Story Hours that were taking over our public libraries. Led by my friend Kirk Cameron, **BRAVE Books** started a counter movement called "See You at the Library Day" where parents and grandparents all across the country held patriotic, God-centered Story Hours of their own to fill children's hearts with goodness, gratitude, and God. At **BRAVE Books** Story Hours we pledge allegiance to the flag, sing "God Bless America", and read wholesome and Godly books.

I'm writing you today and asking for your support because **BRAVE Books** has set an**** ambitious goal to expand their "See You at the Library" movement to reach over 250,000 children in the next year - America's 250th birthday! But they can't do it without you. [**So, will you please consider sending in a sponsorship gift of $35, $50, $75, $100, or more?**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z7ficlkijsomjvmh2goajad03ejk1hl4313695c0_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cjhm6o76cth2bs) Your sponsorship gift helps send a 'Story Hour Kit' to every parent, grandparent, and patriot who's organizing an event at their library. The kit includes everything they need to reach kids with your conservative values in our public libraries: instructions for booking their library space and running their Story Hour, small American flags for 50 little ones, fun stickers, wholesome books, Bible memory verse cards, and other specialty items. **[Your sponsorship gift](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z8k7gmgpe6ehvqtf0qsfiged29k76c1n2slhrev0_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cjhm6o76cth2bs)** is the only way **BRAVE Books** can fund Story Hours for 250,000 children to counter the radical leftists who are teaching our boys to be girls and our girls to be boys. If now is a time when you can send a gift of $125 or more, know that that's enough to sponsor one entire 'Story Hour Kit' yourself. Single handedly, you're helping **BRAVE Books** reach 50 children with our wholesome, Godly, and patriotic message.

You can also bring this critical book to your local library, school, or charity to reach a young boy who may have no other way to get the message that he can be strong! **[Whatever you can give whether it's $35, $125, or anything higher or lower please send in your sponsorship gift in the in the next 24 hours so it's sure to be here when BRAVE Books needs to ship out their next 'Story Hour Kits!"](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zj1c4q20rdr342si9h7pjq3cpkb1sibktmba1tb0_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cjhm6o76cth2bs)** Raising brave boys who grow into men of honor is a critical mission for all of us and I'm so glad that you're with us. May God Bless You, May God Bless Our Children, and May God Bless America,

| [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T2ba58664-4218-4d88-82ce-f231316a453e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) [I’m personally reaching out to you today to ask for your help to keep November in the strongest position possible by splitting a donation between my campaign and Blue Amp Action.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T16747bce-9c6e-4fad-8ca4-a697e7cb8a19/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Small donations from folks like you are what’s going to get us over the finish line. $10 might not seem like much, but it makes a world of difference in a Senate race like this one: [$10](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3fc8804d-d891-4a9c-9ca7-79362061e1a1/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) will help us launch online and TV ads so we can beat back attacks from our GOP opponents. [$10](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf6c43fb7-db50-4539-ab98-237b64ddc1b3/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) will help us begin to recruit and train volunteers all over the great state of Minnesota. [$10](https://default.salsalabs.org/T985e4554-59c9-4311-a110-f29bfecb84e2/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) will help us reach voters on their doorsteps and in their communities. [$10](https://default.salsalabs.org/T430f6af6-153e-4d4b-8e6f-056c3af65efc/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) will help send me to the Senate to keep up the fight for lower prescription costs, good-paying jobs, and health care access. [$10](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tca3b8a05-1af3-4d48-8bd3-656b327f35ed/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) may not seem like much compared to the $600 million war chest Trump is prepared to use to defeat Democrats like me — but $10 donations from thousands of grassroots supporters like you will help us stop Trump and his MAGA loyalists in the Senate. [So I’m humbly asking you today: Can you pitch in $10 or more — split between me and Blue Amp Action — to meet our November goal, Peter? I really appreciate anything you can give:](https://default.salsalabs.org/T758f0fd5-c69e-4228-8cfa-f9d0ec0610c8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade, it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7256f30f-7620-4067-ad36-3e0ff7d290b1/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

subject: Respond to our Trump Satisfaction Survey before the clock strikes midnight >>
body:
Please respond before 11:59 PM!

| | | | Peter, we're so sorry to reach out like this, but your submission has been flagged as **[MISSING]** from our database! Senate Republicans released our brand-new [TRUMP SATISFACTION SURVEY](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTExMl8xMS1FSi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3NTAwODYtMTA0MyZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTExMTJfMTEtRUouMTEwMTE3X3QxNzUwMDg2LTEwNDMifQ/EaMcryk8uOQ9XfUdiGHIew) with the goal to have 100 completed submissions.
**So far, only 99 have responded... _which means we’re just waiting on YOU!_**
[ Peter, all responses will be immediately flagged for President Trump’s team, so it’s important that you help us complete our survey before midnight.
](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTExMl8xMS1FSi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3NTAwODYtMTA0MyZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTExMTJfMTEtRUouMTEwMTE3X3QxNzUwMDg2LTEwNDMifQ/Yn6irMiBr_OmtmenO97wGg)**Trump Satisfaction Survey**
**Registered to:** Peter**Due:** 11:59 PM (ET)1.) Do you approve of the ICE deportations of illegal aliens?2.) Do you approve of banning men from women’s sports?3.) Do you approve of tapping into American energy resources?4.) Do you approve of using our military to address the border crisis?5.) Do you approve of ending taxes on tips?_*** Optional Question: Are there any questions, comments, or concerns you’d like to share with your Republican Senate Majority? ***_[ TAKE THE SURVEY →
](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTExMl8xMS1FSi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3NTAwODYtMTA0MyZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTExMTJfMTEtRUouMTEwMTE3X3QxNzUwMDg2LTEwNDMifQ/IRW9LPwvQwMBdGBuws4OBA)MAGA Republicans and our entire team look forward to hearing from you. Please don’t delay and risk our survey going incomplete!

Thank you,
_
Team Marsha Blackburn_[ TAKE THE SURVEY →
](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTExMl8xMS1FSi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3NTAwODYtMTA0MyZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTExMTJfMTEtRUouMTEwMTE3X3QxNzUwMDg2LTEwNDMifQ/FKmimyZlw0XmUyQ3DEMwEA)| | |

Fellow Patriot, They shot my father. Then, they killed my friend Charlie Kirk. And still -- _the Left hasn't won._ But if we back down now... they will. That's why I'm asking you to stand with me -- and support an organization Charlie built to fight back and WIN: **Turning Point Action.** Charlie was assassinated for daring to teach young Americans to love this country. My father was almost murdered for refusing to give up on America. **My new book -- _Under Siege_ -- is the story of how we've endured and how YOU can help fight back.**
---
[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=45bac9f139eb439a89a0ceb63ee52d07&_e=vm8hGzu8lyQmex8mWWttlfpJa8e5FD-HpzVv7BA8_PWbmCfMC2StVS7xTl3NBLoUllD9S6vBqDyoucryzzriJe9ukbJkbl1fjp23Q1EyGiQ2QNSOvHVBhUvOt2fSK7d1IxslQehLKRYMD0d4ZwIa2PRYqtZwp1Qa9BD24-GzzM8kMZ1-CEE38E0Yfk5yG-FrJYLp6V6wpF5u-mrUgIW3uV9cg321p7CyG4rpsjo6C-5p4qjkpFfjtX8Z8NoLFsMkE0JpN20-EBgzQXUA7YWdiQuSWm6AuL7ap4gmdmjfO_F00AKmvsjBpxr7xMbonu2ewco4PRjI5EtUGDxMLy8QKc1xFq0yU7WzGmN-l5RU7cdgiFjiat78gyYdKXu0y3_JyMJRzMBx4eKOXPvVFKd0oituGgSipiq-dhJGJYcZaP-59btlptMehv8hf7Td4YgRth9mghl7AmcnWB-xSFZ2puKZhO1Sra_0h0f5I2JmYu6PSCt5BTJBCDUOzrMIRHKpiG4a96R28be8YtkfcQWuxxojmATupELoUsujuqyjvCIv1Glqy19NV1ObnFp0OlBJPneXVUGOpOc68Q__JdF9aw25Qbm0wiUSKBLhemriZd1VrY7kngYtSlJLP9Xyq_6T88ihr8xBdJF3BdfjU52gJpMHGnwE7Wq-Bwv_7oYhScEF3hAxn6-U4iMp7tJjUiw2UvIoZDegZ66uOWC9IiCOH_hQQtNsbOtdSr9tQw5Vnlw%3D)

name: NEGOP
subject: REMINDER: 📣 You're Invited! Campaign and Candidate Virtual Bootcamp Series
body:
You don't want to miss this!
| | |
---
| |
---
| You're Invited!
---
| We're excited to invite you to the next installment of the 2025 Campaign and Candidate Virtual Bootcamp Series, hosted in partnership with the Republican National Committee (RNC).
This series of training sessions are designed to equip you with the tools, strategies, and confidence to run a successful campaign and win. These trainings are designed for candidates running for office, campaign staff and volunteers who are involved or want to be involved in campaigns at any level, or those just wanting to learn the basics of running a political campaign. The [Virtual Bootcamp series](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Li-iZiqm6aMl-oI0dX9oK5-kwl_4GD6UwdXuEVsKBi3ZfZHtaLzO-hPscUvv8pjqw7aasnNixjta9Y2xiXlKqXseZjwpjR1RjVNwxLSgBK5N3PO6iTIXwU7-fl_f5UL1ZITEV0htypq2goge_LvUeUNrAB0qVcNq4Obcvln2UthKT9j6TfDJJRGcNG5h1icEOtPYJE4HBi0=&c=6NRwOP-YUyopHr0ZaH2I0hiIdWq9kgnHfusumTWoYvZd2_dXkQeedw==&ch=LEGITOXPxCCMCMp2hoLnAHc7Y4miZOvnXvkXm47ammNGytD854ee0A==) will take place over four weeks. Each session will have its own registration link, so be sure to sign up for the ones you plan to attend:

November 13, 2025 at 6pm - Training 4: Political - The Ground Game and Putting it All Together
* Build grassroots infrastructure, execute voter contact strategies, manage mail campaigns, implement absentee voting, early voting, and Get Out The Vote (GOTV) efforts to mobilize voters, along with election integrity.
* Register Here:[ https://secure.anedot.com/negop/virtual-bootcamp-4](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Li-iZiqm6aMl-oI0dX9oK5-kwl_4GD6UwdXuEVsKBi3ZfZHtaLzO-nV_G8t7neFTY0NGRGfn_uXtIxhtTGKgFijoU5DzJiPk9_9ri9GzqMHHV5hwjCKsM5kTCEQP-OyYw4AjEtxW5RC9SvfXzQkg88fQNAYubaC6XGf_QTU8zhFweMtkO0n11qBttb2Qq3uQ&c=6NRwOP-YUyopHr0ZaH2I0hiIdWq9kgnHfusumTWoYvZd2_dXkQeedw==&ch=LEGITOXPxCCMCMp2hoLnAHc7Y4miZOvnXvkXm47ammNGytD854ee0A==)

If you want to win, skip the guesswork - this is where it starts. Training, tools, and the roadmap to victory.
If you have questions, please reach out to Joe Hagerty at [political@ne.gop](mailto:political@ne.gop)
Sincerely, Joe Hagerty Political Director Nebraska Republican Party
---
---
| | | | |
---
| | | | NEGOP | 1610 N St. | Lincoln, NE 68508 US

name: Shontel Brown
subject: I’m a no.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress
body:
Republicans refuse to lower health care costs.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119116746/582003194/1438484806?refcode=EM_C_251112no_N_SB_90DO_5_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMmJlZDA5Ni1jOWJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WGX5nO6uMwERwK2HUn_qahArPnJnEkkxx13HgRG9RtQ=) **First:** Republicans kept the government shut down for weeks because they refuse to lower health care costs.

**Then:** They released a proposal to reopen the government that does nothing to prevent millions of Americans’ healthcare premiums from skyrocketing by protecting Medicaid cuts and letting ACA subsidies expire.

**Now:** We are about to face higher bills and lost coverage. My constituents and people across the nation can’t afford these cuts.

**Peter, I’m a no.**

I will not stop fighting to ensure that folks don’t have to choose between having health care, putting food on their table, and paying their rent or mortgage. We deserve better.

Together,

Shontel Brown | Shontel Brown is proud to be elected as the Congresswoman for Ohio’s 11th District. She has spent the last decade serving the people of Ohio and building coalitions. As she enters her second full term in Congress, she will use that experience to continue the fight for affordable quality health care, jobs that pay fair wages, a strong public education system, and to preserve and protect our democracy for generations to come. Will you join her and be the change we need?
---

_This post is all about gerrymandering. 1 **If you live in Missouri, sign the petition to undo the state 's recent gerrymander! You'll have to do it in person; [here's more info on where you can sign](https://substack.com/redirect/d575c3b4-ddf5-468a-bd15-f0a81a3ecc9a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**_2 _The deadline to gather enough signatures to qualify for a ballot measure is in early December, so get on it._
[ Sign Missouri's petition for fair maps](https://substack.com/redirect/d575c3b4-ddf5-468a-bd15-f0a81a3ecc9a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
In the months since [Texas kicked off its gerrymander](https://substack.com/redirect/7ce4805f-9a59-48f9-b95a-f811755a5a08?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), it's become clear that the Republicans probably overplayed their hand.
When I [first wrote about this back in August](https://substack.com/redirect/fa6cca60-124c-4108-ad01-2ad95ba6c914?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), I predicted that when the dust settled, Republicans would probably net two House seats. But as the tit-for-tat gerrymanders have played out more, it's become [possible that the Democrats will actually ](https://substack.com/redirect/8ca703a9-eb49-4993-8367-97e9e6915a97?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[gain](https://substack.com/redirect/8ca703a9-eb49-4993-8367-97e9e6915a97?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ seats](https://substack.com/redirect/8ca703a9-eb49-4993-8367-97e9e6915a97?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), in large part because of how aggressive Democrats have been in pushing for state constitutional amendments.3
[](https://substack.com/redirect/eb3335f0-4a85-4bad-805b-5f24968737c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |
#### [Mutually assured gerrymandering](https://substack.com/redirect/eb3335f0-4a85-4bad-805b-5f24968737c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

| Aug 13
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/eb3335f0-4a85-4bad-805b-5f24968737c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
I supported California's efforts to [redraw its Congressional districts](https://substack.com/redirect/4b8c73f0-5d11-43e5-a8d2-d6d37397f6fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to counter what was happening in Texas, but I also called out that the whole fight around gerrymandering is really bad for Americans:
> The American people lose out here. Why? More gerrymandering leads to fewer competitive races and fewer Americans whose votes really matter.
> There are 435 seats in the U.S. House of Representatives. Of those 435, [only 40 seats](https://substack.com/redirect/967e31cd-893a-48e7-8f40-5f69163d1a5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--just 9%!--are likely to be competitive. **That means that 91% of us --including basically everyone in Missouri--votes in an election where the outcome is all but decided**.
But amidst all of the gerrymanders, there's an unusual bipartisan opportunity that may emerge: because of all the districts that have been redrawn, **a lot of members of Congress are about to lose their jobs**.
Untethered from regular pressures to win elections, they have a chance to come together and create a bipartisan coalition to fight gerrymandering. Why might this work? Because for once, doing the right thing is in their self-interest. And self-interest is the most powerful motivator in politics.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTc4NDUyMDM0LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzYyOTU0NDk3LCJleHAiOjIwNzg1MzA0OTcsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.FwJQGIgNsVopgKugCatMvuDdNMoG5OkqWzx5ih6qJfg?)
> _**Article summary:**_
> * _Gerrymandering is happening all over the U.S., even though it 's immensely unpopular among Republicans, independents, and Democrats._
> * _Because of gerrymandering, a lot of members of Congress are about to lose their jobs. They all have very strong personal incentives to push for nonpartisan redistricting._
> * _That group could push for meaningful changes. There 's nothing more impactful they could do with their last year in Congress._
>

### Why gerrymandering happens
Gerrymandering is immensely unpopular. It doesn't matter if you're a Democrat or a Republican; people do not like it.
[82% of Americans](https://substack.com/redirect/98ce2f4a-96bb-4264-83af-59dbfe635eaa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) say that Congressional seats should be drawn by nonpartisan commissions. [Overwhelming majorities of Republicans, independents, and Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/462f99c4-47f1-4737-b4e1-811b9efcd54b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) agree that gerrymandering is a threat to democracy, and basically everyone agrees that it shouldn't be legal for parties to gerrymander themselves to power.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/169956a2-fa11-4604-ad85-9ccb4110bd04?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: [YouGov](https://substack.com/redirect/462f99c4-47f1-4737-b4e1-811b9efcd54b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
For something so overwhelmingly unpopular, why does this keep happening? **Because the people in power have the least incentive to change anything**.
Sure, it's what the American people want. But why would you vote yourself into a more difficult election? Why make election results more unpredictable? Why make it harder to elect people to share your agendas and policy goals?
Ballot measures to end gerrymandering in [Colorado](https://substack.com/redirect/43784616-c2f5-4692-b1c7-e59d5ddc4c81?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Michigan](https://substack.com/redirect/340b5060-ee66-4c10-b398-4b7a004a8c9c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [Utah](https://substack.com/redirect/f8f09071-f867-44da-a4a9-2fa6d40498c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)4 were successful because it's something voters want. But when you leave those reforms in the hands of elected officials, they don't happen, because no one in elected office wants to make their own life harder. And that's why they haven't gone anywhere at the federal level.
With the latest surprise mid-decade gerrymanders, there's a new opportunity.

### The Coalition of Losers
(Or the Gang of the Gerrymandered. Whatever you prefer.5)
_The New York Times_ says that the [impact of the gerrymanders](https://substack.com/redirect/8ca703a9-eb49-4993-8367-97e9e6915a97?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) will be "roughly even." What they mean is that when every state has maximally carved itself up, Republicans and Democrats won't really have gained any partisan edge against one another.
But do you know who doesn't feel like that impact is "roughly even"? California Republican Congressman [Kevin Kiley](https://substack.com/redirect/354e9d01-455a-4ae1-942f-85643d0b1595?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Kiley represents a [sprawling Congressional district](https://substack.com/redirect/abb172f2-d546-4737-9e06-9564c50b9433?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that covers most of the California-Nevada border. He's also just 40, young by political standards. I don't know Kevin, but I'm guessing he has ambitions for higher office.6 Yes, that's tricky as a Republican in California, but maybe he could climb into Republican House leadership one day.
So another Republican might vote like Kevin Kiley. But if you're Kevin Kiley, you would _very_ strongly prefer to be that Republican, versus another Republican from Texas who's coming in to replace you.
In any event, because Californians passed [Proposition 50](https://substack.com/redirect/3f12edbb-2362-4728-87ba-54f2060a28b9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to gerrymander the state, he'll likely be out of a job in 14 months.7 So will four of his colleagues from California and a bunch of other Republicans across the U.S. The same is true on the other side of the aisle; Democrats in Texas, Ohio, Missouri, and elsewhere could find themselves out of a job too.
Suddenly, **we find ourselves with at least 19 members of Congress, and possibly more, who have a very strong incentive to push for nonpartisan redistricting** , because it's how they get to keep their job.
**That 's our Coalition of Losers**: the members of Congress who may not share a party or policy goals, but who are very much in the same boat in wanting to keep their jobs. Here's that ever-evolving list.8
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/70a34830-c2b2-444f-b48d-90e4ca7e3494?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This group could come together to push for common-sense reforms around redistricting, and they have very little to lose. (What they have to gain: their jobs.) The people who stand to benefit from all of these gerrymanders are five Republicans in Texas and five Democrats in California, but they're not in Congress yet.
Already, Kevin Kiley is [making some noise](https://substack.com/redirect/724e4704-681f-4c90-8baf-83c1554f35a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that he's been left for dead by Republican leadership:
> "I did hear the Speaker [Mike Johnson] say something to that effect at a press conference, but then he also went and said, 'Well I think Republicans are going to come out ahead in the redistricting across the country' which I honestly did not appreciate," Kiley said.
> "I think this whole redistricting thing is terrible for the country, and you shouldn't be celebrating whether your party is going to gain in one state or across the country or not, we shouldn't be drawing lines in a partisan way," Kiley said.
Let's see if Kiley--and the others who only have another year left in Congress--want to put their money where their mouth is.

### Is a federal law for nonpartisan redistricting feasible?
Well, the status quo isn't working, and I haven't seen any better ideas.
I do think there'd be enough votes to bring this to a vote (through a [discharge petition](https://substack.com/redirect/cec90db6-2e56-4d9c-b3b1-5eeb26000416?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)9) and pass it in the House. Why I'm confident:
* On the Democratic side: the [For the People Act of 2021](https://substack.com/redirect/62db977e-233d-4ee7-bfca-a799b31ce1ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which included national nonpartisan redistricting, was cosponsored by [literally every Democrat](https://substack.com/redirect/57abeeaf-e4ac-4df7-995e-44bc2e4f9b91?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the House.
* On the Republican side: you don't need many Republicans to join this bipartisan group. They've already been sold out by Republican leadership and have nothing to lose. (What they have to gain: support for a popular policy, and their jobs.)

From there, it'd go to the Senate for a vote. Senators have different personal incentives; there's no redistricting in the Senate. Republicans would only play along if it really looks like they're losing ground.
And in the long-shot scenario that it gets to the President for a signature, would Trump sign? Republicans would _really_ have to be falling behind for him to get behind this.10
### Winning doesn't happen overnight
So, do I think this is likely to pass? No, not in the next few years. But here's what I do think:
* Americans believe that there's [less bipartisanship than in the past](https://substack.com/redirect/f1f3b9e3-6326-4d68-9eed-91c07f34ee63?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but Americans [still want the parties to work together](https://substack.com/redirect/cafb06b0-d020-463c-95d7-465a8211aaf5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* A bipartisan group of lawmakers pushing for something that's overwhelmingly popular like this does create political pressure on anyone running for office to take a position on gerrymandering.
* A reminder that public opinion isn't fixed,11 and politicians do change their minds.
* Democrats and Republicans can work together on issues, especially when they're overwhelmingly popular. The bill to [increase the smoking age to 21](https://substack.com/redirect/34f6d958-7cde-43b3-83c9-89b8fed53291?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) passed with bipartisan support. So did the bill that [legalized gay marriage](https://substack.com/redirect/f7fc47fc-a8db-4469-9d65-85df1be268b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Most members of Congress leave little lasting legacy; most are just rubber-stamp votes for their party.
The irony of America's Coalition of Losers is that, even though they're about to be out of work, they have an opportunity few members of Congress truly have: to shape the public narrative, to move public opinion, and to change public policy for the better.

I considered writing about a few different things, since it's been an eventful week in politics:
* Democrats won decisively in the [Virginia](https://substack.com/redirect/6d218e6e-a3d1-4326-a557-7e6ce6c72801?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [New Jersey](https://substack.com/redirect/0b554000-789a-4d15-9e5f-a77ba7bc68e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) gubernatorial elections. In both, energy costs were pivotal issues, something [I wrote about two weeks ago](https://substack.com/redirect/66d40883-d170-44db-a5ef-b8594c3387b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* The other major victory: California's [Proposition 50](https://substack.com/redirect/3f12edbb-2362-4728-87ba-54f2060a28b9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which gerrymanders California and balances out gerrymanders in Texas.
* Then, there's the end of the government shutdown. [Nate Silver](https://substack.com/redirect/7d49bb28-a6cf-41c2-872e-15fe6ca02f34?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Matt Yglesias](https://substack.com/redirect/42f2848e-a219-4b9a-934a-647b0d37fff7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) offered good perspectives on how that was handled, and I don't have anything novel to add.

2
The (rough) process for these kinds of ballot measures in Missouri:
* When the state legislature passes a bill, if Missouri voters don't like what was passed, they have a limited period of time to collect signatures for a ballot measure. They have to gather signatures from [5% of the state's voters](https://substack.com/redirect/0702270c-d8dc-4dd6-bd6a-151bcfcf5219?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* If enough signatures are gathered, then a ballot measure is sent directly to the voters. They'll be asked whether or not they approve of the legislation.
* If a majority of voters disapprove of the state's gerrymander, then Missouri will go back to using its old Congressional maps, not the ones they just created.

3
I don't think Republicans who suggested this in the first place thought it was possible for California to pass Proposition 50 on such short order. And I really don't think they thought it was possible that amendments in [Virginia](https://substack.com/redirect/474a3b50-8931-4392-bfa9-6777bab97d80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (and potentially in [Colorado](https://substack.com/redirect/29d74bb3-4842-4887-b1f4-78cb4f86249d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) would be on the table.
4
Utah's recent history of nonpartisan redistricting is tortuous:
* Voters passed a ballot measure in 2018 that mandated that the state use an independent redistricting commission.
* The [state legislature all but ignored it in 2022](https://substack.com/redirect/b3b69d97-faab-40d0-9d7a-eccad0e5395d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by splitting Salt Lake City across the state's four Congressional districts.
* In August, the courts ruled that the [districts need to be redrawn](https://substack.com/redirect/b2235454-c878-4b87-aa06-a2bfc4c8dd47?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* The [legislature drew new maps](https://substack.com/redirect/75062a20-f352-412e-8821-2bda069bdae0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in October that were [back in the courts](https://substack.com/redirect/3b01e96d-e5f9-4876-8c2d-25b59c99ca11?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) until yesterday, when a judge [ruled against the legislature's new map too](https://substack.com/redirect/ad9374dc-b384-409c-9dc7-7c3c48b17bf4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Utah's Republican Party is also [considering a ballot measure](https://substack.com/redirect/75062a20-f352-412e-8821-2bda069bdae0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that would throw out its nonpartisan redistricting.

So who knows! But the likeliest scenario is that Democrats will pick up one U.S. House seat in Utah.
5
By the way: as [someone who was personally gerrymandered](https://substack.com/redirect/149d02f0-d1d8-4ae3-91e4-1574fed2ddeb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) when I ran for Congress in '22, I consider myself one of these losers.
6
Doesn't take an investigative reporter to figure this one out: [he ran for Governor in the 2021 recall election](https://substack.com/redirect/5e8ce2c0-72b9-4385-b4e6-adbc9b487a1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). He didn't come close to winning (and the recall failed anyway).
7
He hasn't given up yet; he says [he's considered six (!) different Congressional districts](https://substack.com/redirect/724e4704-681f-4c90-8baf-83c1554f35a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) where he might run. But I think it's highly unlikely.
8
A few notes on who's in this table:
* This list will undoubtedly change (and grow) over the coming weeks and months.
* Obviously, elections aren't predictable; sometimes people win even when no one expects them to win.
* Redistricting in a lot of states isn't finalized, so I'm working off _[Politico](https://substack.com/redirect/94b37b5d-fdae-4fe8-a296-d9c71160a00e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ reporting in Virginia](https://substack.com/redirect/94b37b5d-fdae-4fe8-a296-d9c71160a00e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to guess who's about to lose their job, and off of _[Roll Call](https://substack.com/redirect/468ab91b-e1cb-4f5e-89a4-ab70d26d4cbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ reporting](https://substack.com/redirect/468ab91b-e1cb-4f5e-89a4-ab70d26d4cbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the same info from Ohio.
* Missouri, as discussed, has a mechanism to claw back its gerrymander. Fingers crossed!
* Indiana [may also try to gerrymander](https://substack.com/redirect/dcce3d53-aaf0-41f2-aa8b-f1f008392c6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [Democrat Andre Carson](https://substack.com/redirect/d44e23de-347f-4dd6-862c-42eb25e2eefd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) out of his district and create an additional Republican seat.
* I'm excluding Utah from this list--see their redistricting saga above--because a Republican may end up losing his job _because_ of nonpartisan redistricting. But that's not set in stone; it's likely to be a very competitive district.

9
This is precisely what's happening with the [vote to release federal files related to Jeffrey Epstein](https://substack.com/redirect/b0682139-1426-447c-8b0e-fe89b942ac8e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
10
If Colorado, Oregon, Washington, Minnesota, and other Democratic states start pushing for aggressive gerrymanders, maybe that'd happen. But that's not a likely outcome.
11
Just look at how much things have shifted on [gay marriage](https://substack.com/redirect/2340599f-6f76-442b-a136-d3d3350407a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [marijuana](https://substack.com/redirect/1936b6a1-d0f8-414e-846a-36fb2e493bd1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [the death penalty](https://substack.com/redirect/6172d59a-6b0f-4dc0-9cb8-698d026c337b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), for instance.
