* * *
# [What Business Leaders Can Learn From the 'Mighty Midwest'](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=178696661&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4Njk2NjYxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjA1NjksImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1MjU2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Nm-LD__HiNXBAVTbZ7nO2uDeFiGTVOy_cf4clToBe7I)
### In a culture obsessed with flashy innovation, the heartland has plenty of lessons to teach us.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Nov 12
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=178696661&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4Njk2NjYxLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2Mjk2MDU2OSwiZXhwIjoxNzY1NTUyNTY5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.UZUxs6v7dQFi5uUgFXiLbt9pXF8Z6nW3gXOA5SCCWKo&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=178696661&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4Njk2NjYxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjA1NjksImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1MjU2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Nm-LD__HiNXBAVTbZ7nO2uDeFiGTVOy_cf4clToBe7I&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=178696661&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4Njk2NjYxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjA1NjksImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1MjU2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Nm-LD__HiNXBAVTbZ7nO2uDeFiGTVOy_cf4clToBe7I)
---
| | [](https://substack..t1qBoWWeQpn-icX5wID08tG0AAZMk457ifNZ2Ewqf6I?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/what-business-leaders-can-learn-from?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

I'm willing to make a bold prediction that America's next economic revival won't come from Silicon Valley or Wall Street, but from the Midwest.
A few weeks ago, I spoke to small business owners at the [Disabled American Veterans](https://substack.com/redirect/f9b213cc-5c71-4505-aae6-d62a64731b69?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (DAV) headquarters in Cincinnati, and heard a phrase that's been stuck in my head ever since: the "Mighty Midwest _. "_
The slogan captures something we've lost sight of in our race for scale and innovation in our major cities. The Midwest offers a different kind of strength that's steady, not showy. It doesn't need to shout to prove its worth. It just gets the job done.
Every time I visit Ohio, I fall for its modest charm all over again. There's something about the place that reminds me of the summer I was eighteen, working overnight graveyard shifts at the Hershey Reese's factory in central Pennsylvania. Back then, I was saving up before joining the Army, smelling like peanut butter and chocolate every night.
My first trip to Ohio came years later, when I ran the Army doing early morning PT (physical training) or what the cadets jokingly called "Break the Secretary." We did our workouts in Ohio State's football stadium, known affectionately as "the Shoe." Afterwards, I got to chat with legendary coach Urban Meyer. I returned last year for Memorial Day at the National Veterans Museum, and once again, the same feeling hit me: this place runs on quiet strength.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/640a0f08-7965-45ad-8389-0059d9c38665?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Standing with Army ROTC cadre at the Ohio State University, September 2016
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e84dc523-6fd8-465a-ac1e-f0f7b60d90f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
At the Veterans Museum and Memorial in Columbus, Ohio, May 2024, with Bill Doyle of Project One Vet at a Time.
If my writing resonates with you, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..C6iwM66cmzekJhO-xMZECcWsInsFHSrTuqOtDcpbt_Q?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=178696661)
But this most recent trip back hit different - their mix of grit and grace is always palpable, and frankly, in business and life, I now value people more who still measure worth by reliability more than rhetoric.
Our U.S. semiconductor revival is being spearheaded by Intel in Ohio: the company is [investing $28 billion to build two advanced chip facilities](https://substack.com/redirect/3ecd0b6b-3592-4a2e-80ab-6f18f4e6b790?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in New Albany on a site designed to accommodate up to eight. The project will bring around 3,000 permanent Intel jobs, plus many more in construction.

But it's also the leadership of their people. Ohio is a place that quietly produces extraordinary leaders who understand the power of steadiness when others rush aimlessly ahead.
One of the leaders embodying the mighty Midwest ethos happens to be the new CEO of Starbucks, Brian Niccol. He's a native of Philadelphia, but spent his formative years in Ohio, which is evident by his picture being displayed throughout the Cincinnati/Northern Kentucky International Airport-- a tribute to his time spent at Miami University of Ohio and Procter & Gamble. He's now steering Starbucks through a massive reset after successfully turning around Taco Bell and Chipotle. Known for his creative marketing and operational acumen, Niccol led Chipotle with sales nearly doubling--from $4.9 billion in 2018, his first year as CEO, to [$9.9 billion in 2023](https://substack.com/redirect/2285ccfa-16ce-4174-a49a-de7bd29b061f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Net income also grew roughly sevenfold over the same period. His recent hiring by Starbucks in 2024 sent its [stock soaring 25 percent](https://substack.com/redirect/110ceaac-b4d4-4c9e-ac81-994bd8ff4685?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) within hours, marking the company's biggest single-day gain since its market debut in 1992.
# The Power of Pruning
Niccol has launched a plan called **" Back to Starbucks."** The company plans to close about one percent of its underperforming U.S. stores, not because it's shrinking, but because it's growing smarter. Niccol wants Starbucks to feel warm and human again, rather than a brand that had become efficient but soulless. So, it's closing the pickup-only and mobile-order cafes that feel more like airport kiosks than coffeehouses. It's a return to fundamentals: upgrading stores, retraining teams, and reinvesting in the brand experience. Going back to 'The Starbucks Coffee Company.'
Niccol's business strategy feels distinctly Midwestern. You don't always have to grow by adding more; you can also grow by focusing on what's working and what's not.
Closing stores can seem like a failure. But anyone from the Midwest, or anyone who has led in uniform, understands the discipline of pruning. We get rid of underperforming or unethical soldiers because we demand excellence for life or death missions, and leaders know they are there to make the tough calls.
You don't cut to shrink. You cut to strengthen.
Niccol identified coffeehouses "where we're unable to create the physical environment our customers and partners expect," he [said recently](https://substack.com/redirect/812f2a14-6bab-4328-94c6-d41b39b7a65d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Those stores are closing while others are being modernized or relocated.
It's operational maturity in motion: reallocating capital, refining systems, and strengthening the network. It's the same mindset that defines elite military leadership and strong operators in business. Niccol brings what I call an 'Operator Economy mindset': focusing on fundamentals, leading from the front, communicating relentlessly, and always leaving things better than you found them.
Veterans-turned-CEOs know leadership isn't always about constant motion. It's about direction, readiness, and resilience. The Midwest understands that, too.
The idea that "fewer stores can mean better business" may sound odd to Wall Street. But Starbucks' story is a reminder that sustainable growth often starts with subtraction.

Starbucks' reset hinges on three core principles:
* **Quality over quantity:** Every closure improves the ratio of high-performing stores to low-performing ones.
* **Operational excellence:** Fewer, stronger units mean better training, service consistency, and profitability.
* **Cultural clarity:** A smaller network makes it easier to uphold values and brand standards.

These are the kinds of leadership skills I aim to cultivate in my daily work. In October, I recorded a podcast with author and CEO coach Kevin Lawrence for WWD Voices: Retail Rx on how leaders can sustain both personal and corporate growth in today's business climate. You can [listen here](https://substack.com/redirect/5093f072-b871-4d82-8701-fc1a2e5b24b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[| Balancing Surviving and Thriving While Building Fairchild Fashion MediaEpisode
---|---](https://substack.com/redirect/974e8860-273d-4774-b89d-2e389dd69701?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
# The Operator Economy in Action
In the Operator Economy composed of builders and entrepreneurs, value creation comes from quality of execution, not expansion. Leadership looks more like stewardship, and steady hands matter more than fast pivots.
It's also the mindset that drives many of the veterans I meet through Disabled American Veterans and [Owners in Honor](https://substack.com/redirect/0842a63f-d0f1-40dc-9e96-84fe6e838be3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), two organizations helping former service members build and run small businesses with the same precision they once brought to the battlefield.
I first saw this operator mindset in my platoon mate, Cadet Robert Bruce, at Fort Knox, Kentucky, in 1994. Bruce was a 6'3, 200 lb soldier from THE Ohio State University. He always seemed to just get it.

I, on the other hand, was a scrappy 6'1 145lb hockey player from King's College, proud to lead our team in both points _and_ penalties--which, depending on your perspective, is either impressive or alarming. I was new to the Army and was learning every day the little leadership lessons from folks like him.

That summer, we spent our days drenched in sweat, patrolling what felt like endless miles of woods. Cadet Bruce was never late for formation and always volunteered to carry the SAW--the heavy squad-automated weapon everyone else hoped to avoid. But he never complained. Cadet Bruce was tough and always quick with a smile or verbal jab because life is too short not to have fun busting chops while you're embracing the suck of training when most college kids are at the beach or planning their next happy hour excursion.
The Midwest is full of Cadet Bruces. People who show up every day and do the thing that needs doing.
If you have the sacred opportunity to lead teams, invest in companies, or build communities, ask yourself:
Where can you prune for strength this year?
Whether you're leading a platoon, a portfolio company, or a coffee empire, the mission is the same: serve with integrity, lead with courage, and build something that lasts.
_Secretary Patrick J. Murphy & his venture partners have invested over $250 million in 200 companies, mostly veteran-owned. He now runs the geopolitical unit at Hilco Global. He also has no idea where Cadet Bruce, OSU'96, is, but says hello._

If my writing resonates with you, consider . Annual paid subscribers in the U.S. will receive a signed copy of my 2008 memoir, (shipping not available to Alaska, Hawaii, and U.S. territories). In a culture obsessed with flashy innovation, the heartland has plenty of lessons to teach us. | | | Nov 12 | | | | | | | | | | | | I'm willing to make a bold prediction that America's next economic revival won't come from Silicon Valley or Wall Street, but from the Midwest. A few weeks ago, I spoke to small business owners at the (DAV) headquarters in Cincinnati, and heard a phrase that's been stuck in my head ever since: the "Mighty Midwest . " The slogan captures something we've lost sight of in our race for scale and innovation in our major cities. The Midwest offers a different kind of strength that's steady, not showy. It doesn't need to shout to prove its worth. It just gets the job done. Every time I visit Ohio, I fall for its modest charm all over again. There's something about the place that reminds me of the summer I was eighteen, working overnight graveyard shifts at the Hershey Reese's factory in central Pennsylvania. Back then, I was saving up before joining the Army, smelling like peanut butter and chocolate every night. My first trip to Ohio came years later, when I ran the Army doing early morning PT (physical training) or what the cadets jokingly called "Break the Secretary." We did our workouts in Ohio State's football stadium, known affectionately as "the Shoe." Afterwards, I got to chat with legendary coach Urban Meyer. I returned last year for Memorial Day at the National Veterans Museum, and once again, the same feeling hit me: this place runs on quiet strength. | | ---|---|--- Standing with Army ROTC cadre at the Ohio State University, September 2016 | | ---|---|--- At the Veterans Museum and Memorial in Columbus, Ohio, May 2024, with Bill Doyle of Project One Vet at a Time. If my writing resonates with you, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. But this most recent trip back hit different - their mix of grit and grace is always palpable, and frankly, in business and life, I now value people more who still measure worth by reliability more than rhetoric. Our U.S. semiconductor revival is being spearheaded by Intel in Ohio: the company is in New Albany on a site designed to accommodate up to eight. The project will bring around 3,000 permanent Intel jobs, plus many more in construction. But it's also the leadership of their people. Ohio is a place that quietly produces extraordinary leaders who understand the power of steadiness when others rush aimlessly ahead. One of the leaders embodying the mighty Midwest ethos happens to be the new CEO of Starbucks, Brian Niccol. He's a native of Philadelphia, but spent his formative years in Ohio, which is evident by his picture being displayed throughout the Cincinnati/Northern Kentucky International Airport-- a tribute to his time spent at Miami University of Ohio and Procter & Gamble. He's now steering Starbucks through a massive reset after successfully turning around Taco Bell and Chipotle. Known for his creative marketing and operational acumen, Niccol led Chipotle with sales nearly doubling--from $4.9 billion in 2018, his first year as CEO, to . Net income also grew roughly sevenfold over the same period. His recent hiring by Starbucks in 2024 sent its within hours, marking the company's biggest single-day gain since its market debut in 1992. The Power of Pruning Niccol has launched a plan called " Back to Starbucks." The company plans to close about one percent of its underperforming U.S. stores, not because it's shrinking, but because it's growing smarter. Niccol wants Starbucks to feel warm and human again, rather than a brand that had become efficient but soulless. So, it's closing the pickup-only and mobile-order cafes that feel more like airport kiosks than coffeehouses. It's a return to fundamentals: upgrading stores, retraining teams, and reinvesting in the brand experience. Going back to 'The Starbucks Coffee Company.' Niccol's business strategy feels distinctly Midwestern. You don't always have to grow by adding more; you can also grow by focusing on what's working and what's not. Closing stores can seem like a failure. But anyone from the Midwest, or anyone who has led in uniform, understands the discipline of pruning. We get rid of underperforming or unethical soldiers because we demand excellence for life or death missions, and leaders know they are there to make the tough calls. You don't cut to shrink. You cut to strengthen. Niccol identified coffeehouses "where we're unable to create the physical environment our customers and partners expect," he . Those stores are closing while others are being modernized or relocated. It's operational maturity in motion: reallocating capital, refining systems, and strengthening the network. It's the same mindset that defines elite military leadership and strong operators in business. Niccol brings what I call an 'Operator Economy mindset': focusing on fundamentals, leading from the front, communicating relentlessly, and always leaving things better than you found them. Veterans-turned-CEOs know leadership isn't always about constant motion. It's about direction, readiness, and resilience. The Midwest understands that, too. The idea that "fewer stores can mean better business" may sound odd to Wall Street. But Starbucks' story is a reminder that sustainable growth often starts with subtraction. Starbucks' reset hinges on three core principles: Quality over quantity: Every closure improves the ratio of high-performing stores to low-performing ones. Operational excellence: Fewer, stronger units mean better training, service consistency, and profitability. Cultural clarity: A smaller network makes it easier to uphold values and brand standards. These are the kinds of leadership skills I aim to cultivate in my daily work. In October, I recorded a podcast with author and CEO coach Kevin Lawrence for WWD Voices: Retail Rx on how leaders can sustain both personal and corporate growth in today's business climate. You can . The Operator Economy in Action In the Operator Economy composed of builders and entrepreneurs, value creation comes from quality of execution, not expansion. Leadership looks more like stewardship, and steady hands matter more than fast pivots. It's also the mindset that drives many of the veterans I meet through Disabled American Veterans and , two organizations helping former service members build and run small businesses with the same precision they once brought to the battlefield. I first saw this operator mindset in my platoon mate, Cadet Robert Bruce, at Fort Knox, Kentucky, in 1994. Bruce was a 6'3, 200 lb soldier from THE Ohio State University. He always seemed to just get it. I, on the other hand, was a scrappy 6'1 145lb hockey player from King's College, proud to lead our team in both points and penalties--which, depending on your perspective, is either impressive or alarming. I was new to the Army and was learning every day the little leadership lessons from folks like him. That summer, we spent our days drenched in sweat, patrolling what felt like endless miles of woods. Cadet Bruce was never late for formation and always volunteered to carry the SAW--the heavy squad-automated weapon everyone else hoped to avoid. But he never complained. Cadet Bruce was tough and always quick with a smile or verbal jab because life is too short not to have fun busting chops while you're embracing the suck of training when most college kids are at the beach or planning their next happy hour excursion. The Midwest is full of Cadet Bruces. People who show up every day and do the thing that needs doing. If you have the sacred opportunity to lead teams, invest in companies, or build communities, ask yourself: Where can you prune for strength this year? Whether you're leading a platoon, a portfolio company, or a coffee empire, the mission is the same: serve with integrity, lead with courage, and build something that lasts. Secretary Patrick J. Murphy & his venture partners have invested over $250 million in 200 companies, mostly veteran-owned. He now runs the geopolitical unit at Hilco Global. He also has no idea where Cadet Bruce, OSU'96, is, but says hello. The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. You 're currently a free subscriber to From the Desk of Honorable Patrick J. Murphy. For the full experience, upgrade your subscription. Annual paid subscribers will receive a signed copy of my 2008 memoir, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Patrick J. Murphy ? ?

body:
Now that I’ve been at the DFL for a few months, I wanted to reach out and introduce myself! | | | **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119136407/582061358/1893605772?refcode=header&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzU5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2UyMTZlNy1kYmJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZEyOy2GHTGfbVX3mTE4d0Pin4wd7DYUHM2KbbBw62yA=)**

Peter, Jim Heighington, Executive Director of the Minnesota DFL here. Now that I’ve been at the DFL for a few months, I wanted to reach out and introduce myself!

Before joining the DFL, I was an adviser to the Obama Administration and the United Nations Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights on LGBTQ equality and served as Chief Digital Officer at GLAAD, working to reduce bias in artificial intelligence and expand the organization’s digital advocacy infrastructure.

Each step of my career path I’ve focused on transforming community advocacy through technology and other methods, and I’m thrilled to continue this work at the Minnesota DFL. I will bring that same passion and experience to help us win up and down the ballot next year.

The DFL is working every day to organize our communities and make sure we’re able to keep Minnesota moving forward.**We know that Donald Trump and his MAGA agenda are the exact _opposite_ of what Minnesotans need right now, which is why we’re building a team to defeat his agenda in 2026.**

[**Will you give $10, $25, $50, or whatever amount works for you to make sure we’re ready to take on Donald Trump in 2026 and fight for communities across the state?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119136408/582061359/-1399320998?refcode=body&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzU5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2UyMTZlNy1kYmJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZEyOy2GHTGfbVX3mTE4d0Pin4wd7DYUHM2KbbBw62yA=)

Once I'm sworn in, I'll be the FINAL House Democrat needed to force a floor vote to release the Epstein files. name: Jack Schlossberg
subject: I'm running for Congress!
body:
I know you’re likely getting a ton of emails from candidates and causes right now, Peter.
But I have to ask: Is there anything I can say to convince you to chip in $25 between Real Texas PAC and my Senate campaign in Ohio? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]
What if I told you that right after I joined this race, experts at The Cook Political Report increased our odds of winning?
What if I told you that CNN and The New York Times report that Ohio is “in play” – but that corporate special interests are gearing up to spend millions against me?
What if I told you that misleading attack ads have already hit the airwaves – and that whether we’re able to counter these smears and reach voters with the truth depends on people like you?
This grassroots, small-dollar donor movement is our best chance to flip Ohio’s Senate seat. But we’re facing an uphill battle, and I need folks like you by my side to win.
So please, split $25 or more between Real Texas PAC and me to power our campaign forward, win in Ohio, and keep up the fight for working people. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Sherrod Brown and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?express_lane=true&refcode=101725_2&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?express_lane=true&refcode=101725_2&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?express_lane=true&refcode=101725_2&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?express_lane=true&refcode=101725_2&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?express_lane=true&refcode=101725_2&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2&amount=25]
Thank you so much,
Sherrod Brown
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]
The Real Texas PAC
1150 N Loop 1604 W
Ste 108-230
San Antonio, TX 78248
United States
Contributions or gifts to The Real Texas PAC are not tax deductible. Contributions from corporations, labor organizations, treasury funds, and federal government contractors are prohibited. Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. All contributions from corporations and labor unions will be deposited in the non-contribution account. Please contact us at realtexas@mbacg.com if you would like a different allocation.
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/Pq6mZqR8vUu32u6MFdTiQw2?unsubscribedata=TcaVNTMsp%2F%2BM21YxBFscF7BypWeLR9vFYBMQueAWKYnP66KEJZcPgAusbOx9aOo1%2Fjzw7m%2BgayYnKzsUnTI%2Fhq785CkCpIz%2Be68II%2Fh94k97DeIzTsg5B4nohdPQ2x1olqKt7mTGO8PuOmAftmRw9yt8HNry4QC%2BPKz%2FVAbXNC9dCg6Rscs7jB1minNeYfiXeUVFj%2FZak8%2Fb6RFmynhQECwpLpthzS55NIhp7lBtdHj9X8FuzaEk63%2F8w%2BIiAUvjPJ9hX91z%2F3ErTjgw8%2FQbX8fpNLF5myFJcwIu47pw%2FPlhL5Q0%2F%2BCS6wHNzok9F44hdpBQAxjVvDNLyVhV0VYisP1xujG8D1Juq%2FgU4ZT28FCjmnD3qGeI3HcPwWK8QI48wfhi1h9RxXZZmLCPXmDDxVXxDYF7WlT3XTgXCBoEdfM%3D .

name: Kathy Barnette
subject: Elon Musk Called Him a “Clear Thinker” — Kaizen Asiedu on Self-Respect and the Power to Respond
body:
When Elon Musk called Kaizen Asiedu "a clear thinker," he recognized something rare -- a voice cutting through the noise with truth and conviction.

| |

# [Elon Musk Called Him a "Clear Thinker" -- Kaizen Asiedu on Self-Respect and the Power to Respond](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1249381&post_id=178455181&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NDU1MTgxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjE5MjksImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1MzkyOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEyNDkzODEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.KCsUwDbeh6uKGhH3nFEuU_UauzIdcag1_CxsfiG5u7s)
Nov 12
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1249381&post_id=178455181&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NDU1MTgxLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2Mjk2MTkyOSwiZXhwIjoxNzY1NTUzOTI5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTI0OTM4MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.hP-QMdyAsJyUTxDsTgWzh0zlq5cR-UVeQDy3cvbUe-k&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1249381&post_id=178455181&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NDU1MTgxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjE5MjksImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1MzkyOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEyNDkzODEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.KCsUwDbeh6uKGhH3nFEuU_UauzIdcag1_CxsfiG5u7s&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1249381&post_id=178455181&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NDU1MTgxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjE5MjksImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1MzkyOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEyNDkzODEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.KCsUwDbeh6uKGhH3nFEuU_UauzIdcag1_CxsfiG5u7s)
---
| | [](https://substack..JQ3SE0uY2UAJAMdE40GTLTDY3d5b4ZVfmdt77MrHpa8?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/kathybarnette/p/elon-musk-called-him-a-clear-thinker?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

When Elon Musk called Kaizen Asiedu "a clear thinker," he recognized something rare -- a voice cutting through the noise with truth and conviction.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/af0c352f-e7ae-4812-aec1-4230d14460d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
At the _[Seed& Roots Summit](https://substack.com/redirect/af0c352f-e7ae-4812-aec1-4230d14460d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_, Kaizen delivers a bold message to Black America: it's time to stop blaming racism and start reclaiming self-respect and the power to respond to the world around us. This is about mindset, not victimhood -- strength, not resentment.

His words remind us that freedom isn't granted by others; it's cultivated within. Because the real revolution begins in how we think.

subject: BREAKING: Epstein Emails Directly Implicate Donald Trump
body:
Epstein called Trump "the dog that hasn't barked" in newly released emails.

| |
---|---|---

# [BREAKING: Epstein Emails Directly Implicate Donald Trump](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=178701119&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NzAxMTE5LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjIzNzgsImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1NDM3OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.lp-CMSHj3P9i-_J9MgdoS8reTkfpawpIgSVdCjSe4CU)
### Epstein called Trump "the dog that hasn't barked" in newly released emails.
| | [Mike Nellis](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---
| Nov 12
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=178701119&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NzAxMTE5LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2Mjk2MjM3OCwiZXhwIjoxNzY1NTU0Mzc4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDI1ODExNSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.M9yByo_WLlR5PLndRgPilZs-IuE7AQF1c4_Prc3YQCc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=178701119&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NzAxMTE5LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjIzNzgsImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1NDM3OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.lp-CMSHj3P9i-_J9MgdoS8reTkfpawpIgSVdCjSe4CU&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

---

_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio and expand our team with a video editor. If you've been enjoying the work, now's the time to back it. Paid subscribers make it possible--just $8/month makes a huge difference: ](https://substack.com/redirect/4eb4dc4c-059a-44d5-8612-81cd7999421a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/4eb4dc4c-059a-44d5-8612-81cd7999421a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
This morning, House Democrats released new emails from Jeffrey Epstein's estate that further implicate Donald Trump in the Epstein scandal--and in the abuse of young women and men. On Epstein's plane. At his house. Wherever.
The Democrats on the House Oversight Committee were generous when they said these emails "raise serious questions" about Trump and his knowledge of Epstein's horrific crimes.
I'd say it doesn't raise questions--it gives us answers.
All year, Trump and his MAGA mouthpieces have claimed he didn't do anything wrong. But they've refused to release these files. They've lied about the dumbest, pettiest shit--like whether Trump doodles, whether he ever sent a birthday card to Epstein, or when Mike Johnson suggested Trump was an undercover FBI informant trying to bring down the whole thing. They lied about all of it.
Now we know why.
In these emails, Epstein tells Ghislaine Maxwell about Trump spending hours at a house with one of the victims (name redacted). He calls Trump "the dog that hasn't barked." He talks about conversations with Trump about Mar-a-Lago. He mentions Trump asking Ghislaine to stop stealing his employees.
And then there's the kicker--an email exchange between Epstein and Michael Wolff about how Trump could "hang himself" by lying on CNN about being on Epstein's plane and in his homes. They literally talk about using it as leverage if Trump wins the election in 2016. They were strategizing for a bribe--for a pardon, most likely.
None of this should shock anyone who's been paying attention. You can see the guilt on Trump's face. You can see it in every move Kash Patel, Pam Bondi, and the rest of the cover-up crew have made.
* * *
_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio and expand our team with a video editor. If you've been enjoying the work, now's the time to back it. Paid subscribers make it possible--just $8/month makes a huge difference: ](https://substack.com/redirect/4eb4dc4c-059a-44d5-8612-81cd7999421a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/4eb4dc4c-059a-44d5-8612-81cd7999421a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
I'm proud of the Democrats on the House Oversight Committee who've fought to keep this in the news--who won't let the public forget that young women and men--children as young as 14 years old--were groomed, sexually exploited, and abused. By Epstein, by Ghislaine Maxwell, potentially by Trump, and by other rich and powerful predators.
Every time we talk about the Epstein files, we're talking about more than just Epstein. It's a window into Trump's soul. Into what he believes and the principles he lives by.
He believes the rich and powerful should never be held accountable. That they can overtly and blatantly steal, cheat, hurt people--including kids--and get away with it. That there are no consequences for them.
And that's not just wrong--it's the rot at the core of this country.
Same as after the 2008 crash. One of the biggest nationwide mistakes of my adult life--watching the people who destroyed the economy walk away clean. Nobody went to prison for that.
And here we are again. One person--just one--has gone to prison in this entire scheme where Epstein and Maxwell trafficked and abused people. One. And we're supposed to call that justice?
I don't care if more names come out and they're Democrats. Lazy MAGA influencers always ask, "What if Bill Clinton's in there?" Then hold Bill Clinton accountable. I don't give a damn what party someone's in. If they were on the plane, on the island, at the house--they need to answer for it.
Because if we can't hold people accountable for this--what does that say about us?
I'm glad we have these emails now. But I'm not naive enough to think anything will come of it right now. Trump has the DOJ in his pocket. Pam Bondi will make sure there's no investigation. The FBI, Kash Patel--they've probably known about this the whole time. And they've chosen to look the other way. They've done nothing to uphold the Constitution and enforce the law, an oath they all took.
Mike Johnson is FINALLY swearing in Rep.-elect Grijalva today, which means the Epstein discharge petition can move forward--but the rumor mill is swirling that one of the Republicans who signed it is planning to back out. I'm hearing Nancy Mace--the South Carolina congresswoman seen walking out of that closed-door meeting with Epstein victims in tears back in September--may be the one backing out, but who knows.
So what happens next? The usual Trump play: deny, deflect, distract. Indict someone else. Create a spectacle. Call it a hoax. A lot of people think Adam Schiff is about to get indicted, even though it's well reported they don't have enough evidence to do so. Wouldn't surprise me. They'll use that to change the subject, spin the narrative.
But we can't let that work. Trump is implicated in the Epstein files. That needs to stay front and center.
This isn't about partisanship. It's not left vs. right. It's about whether we're going to let the most powerful people in the world abuse and destroy lives without consequence.
And again--this is who Trump is. The White House, under him, has become a protection racket for the rich. For anyone who pays up or pledges loyalty. He's already pardoned a wild number of criminals: Rudy Giuliani, George Santos, January 6th rioters, sexual abusers, drug traffickers.
Hell, he's flirting with pardoning Diddy! They moved Maxwell into a minimum-security prison, and House Dems have evidence she may get a commutation soon.
He thinks proximity to power is immunity. And honestly? Nothing in his life has ever proven him wrong. He's a convicted felon--32 counts--and he's still sitting in the White House.
That's why Democrats need to lock in and not let this go. Keep hammering the truth. Keep reminding people.
It's been ten years and people still don't get the full scope of this scandal. Still don't see Trump for who he really is. But that's okay.
Because we're going to show them.
Yes, we'll keep talking about affordability, corruption, and security. We have to. But the Epstein files? That's the gateway. That's the crack in the armor. And for once, Trump has backed himself so far into a corner that his playbook may actually fail.
The American people deserve to know.
Victims, whose lives have been upended, deserve real justice and accountability.
And our government has the responsibility to deliver on this.
We need the full truth--about Epstein, about Maxwell, about Trump, and about every single person who helped them.
Burn it down to the roots.

He's already pardoned a wild number of criminals: Rudy Giuliani, George Santos, January 6th rioters, sexual abusers, drug traffickers. Hell, he's flirting with pardoning Diddy! They moved Maxwell into a minimum-security prison, and House Dems have evidence she may get a commutation soon. He thinks proximity to power is immunity. And honestly? Nothing in his life has ever proven him wrong. He's a convicted felon--32 counts--and he's still sitting in the White House. That's why Democrats need to lock in and not let this go. Keep hammering the truth. Keep reminding people. It's been ten years and people still don't get the full scope of this scandal. Still don't see Trump for who he really is. But that's okay. Because we're going to show them. Yes, we'll keep talking about affordability, corruption, and security. We have to. But the Epstein files? That's the gateway. That's the crack in the armor. And for once, Trump has backed himself so far into a corner that his playbook may actually fail. The American people deserve to know. Victims, whose lives have been upended, deserve real justice and accountability. And our government has the responsibility to deliver on this. We need the full truth--about Epstein, about Maxwell, about Trump, and about every single person who helped them. Burn it down to the roots. Mike Nellis is a Democratic strategist and entrepreneur who has raised over $1 billion for Democratic campaigns and causes. He 's the founder of Authentic.org, an award-winning fundraising and advertising agency, and a former Senior Advisor to Vice President Kamala Harris. This Substack is where he writes with endless urgency about the issues of the day--and how we can save the Democratic Party and our democracy. You 're currently a free subscriber to Endless Urgency with Mike Nellis. This Wednesday is the deadline -- and our baby girl is leading the charge [to make a difference.](https://substack.com/redirect/715cdb36-74aa-44b1-92ea-29939e3fb635?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
She is an elite athlete -- training six days a week. She takes honors and AP classes and carries a perfect 4.0 GPA. She's disciplined, kind, and focused -- the kind of young woman who believes hard work and heart can change the world.
Click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/715cdb36-74aa-44b1-92ea-29939e3fb635?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to donate.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/715cdb36-74aa-44b1-92ea-29939e3fb635?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Now she's putting that same drive into something bigger than herself. She's serving as a team leader for her school's **Mini-THON** , a student-led movement that raises funds and awareness for **[Four Diamonds](https://substack.com/redirect/715cdb36-74aa-44b1-92ea-29939e3fb635?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[,](https://substack.com/redirect/715cdb36-74aa-44b1-92ea-29939e3fb635?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) an organization devoted to conquering childhood cancer.
These kids train, organize, and fundraise -- not for themselves, but for children fighting the hardest battle of all.
Will you help her reach her goal before the Wednesday deadline?
We couldn't be prouder of her -- not just for her achievements, but for her heart. Thank you for standing with her, and with every family praying for a cure.
**For the kids. For the future. For the fight.**

name: Happening now
subject: Trump summons Boebert to situation room over Epstein
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
It’s part of a desperate attempt by Trump to stop Congress from gathering enough signatures for a petition that would release the Epstein files.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001sYlbv0o8kwf6FYVemh_0O3yfd_-uRTCqAwu2MAv9oy2RPbZmNlNeHUspmztJ_nuNmMfn0kyNtPiAqOpxFci1kMcEMwjuoNKMszVXbBCO3-dUzGtkRGAUIsa7fp_IBKfjNtNuMbFmlBaYAwuVvqWO_ZwrBmPTXVcJGqAo3I1jleZ4EACGO6i45EcEOSyVi91EBrEn8ICarAlMMLEiRO9D_qc3J85tM-qHFshT6EtrZOY=&c=ACq_rYP33ZKRBdU-W3HWnu1b7KPWgRaSQHfODrAFp-mpz11nGNrcTA==&ch=W3bqLfbkNXvmNDVrN_lAKMHSvmmLOEtytVz8u0tq1jmiOj40nlYcNg==)
---
|

Peter,
Moments ago, Lauren Boebert left the White House after being summoned by Trump for a meeting in the Situation Room bunker that included Attorney General Pam Bondi and FBI Director Kash Patel.
It's part of a desperate attempt by Trump to stop Congress from gathering enough signatures for a petition that would compel the Justice Department to release the Epstein files to the public.
This comes as Congress is expected to gain enough signatures to release the Epstein files this evening when Democrat Adelita Grijalva (D-AZ) is officially sworn in.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001sYlbv0o8kwf6FYVemh_0O3yfd_-uRTCqAwu2MAv9oy2RPbZmNlNeHUspmztJ_nuNmMfn0kyNtPiAqOpxFci1kMcEMwjuoNKMszVXbBCO3-dUzGtkRGAUIsa7fp_IBKfjNtNuMbFmlBaYAwuVvqWO_ZwrBmPTXVcJGqAo3I1jleZ4EACGO6i45EcEOSyVi91EBrEn8ICarAlMMLEiRO9D_nk00QUivh7PhzQn_npiIHc=&c=ACq_rYP33ZKRBdU-W3HWnu1b7KPWgRaSQHfODrAFp-mpz11nGNrcTA==&ch=W3bqLfbkNXvmNDVrN_lAKMHSvmmLOEtytVz8u0tq1jmiOj40nlYcNg==)
---
This is a five fire alarm. Trump is panicking that the files will be released.
Boebert has stated in the past that she would sign the discharge petition, but after today's meeting at the White House, she refused to comment on whether she will be signing on to the petition. If she flips, the Epstein files will not be released.
We expect her to flip given her vulnerable hold on her district and the intense pressure the administration is laying down on her.
Team Trisha is monitoring the news closely. If Boebert flips, this will be a betrayal to her constituents and Republicans and Democrats alike who are demanding to learn the truth about Trump's ties to Epstein's human trafficking ring.
Mark our words, if she flips, this will be the end of her career. Her dwindling support from her base will collapse.

body:
Our House Majority is in jeopardy, and I need your help before midnight.

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTE1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTExMl8wRS1GUi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3NDk1NTMtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTExMTJfMEUtRlIuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzQ5NTUzLTI4OTkifQ/em2-vFMI1_9xLe6jO3XICQ)Peter,
It’s GOP Freshman Troy Downing. I want to shoot straight with you.
The November mid-month deadline is less than **60 hours** away, and my critical GOP seat is in danger. Kamala, AOC, Bernie, and their far-left allies are targeting Montana to take me down and flip the House.
As a Trump-endorsed combat veteran, I’ve faced tough battles before, but this one’s different. The future of the conservative House Majority, President Trump’s MAGA agenda, and the entire America First movement are on the line.
But I know if just a handful of Patriots chip in today, we’ll hold the line and defend the House. But there’s no time to waste
**[It will make all the difference if you can spare just $15.](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTE1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTExMl8wRS1GUi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3NDk1NTMtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTExMTJfMEUtRlIuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzQ5NTUzLTI4OTkifQ/I4Piy1fwhzUi5zI6V_6OoQ)** We’re miles behind on our goal, and every dollar helps us stay in the fight for our future.[DONATE BEFORE MIDNIGHT
](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTE1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTExMl8wRS1GUi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3NDk1NTMtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTExMTJfMEUtRlIuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzQ5NTUzLTI4OTkifQ/Gl-76FkU5JYNAdoiiJ1Isw)This is DEFCON 1, Peter. It’s now or never
Thank you for your support,
Troy DowningCombat Veteran in Congress| |

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: Peter, you blew me out of the water
body:
I can’t thank you enough

# Elon Musk Warns of America's $36 Trillion Dollar Debt Bomb
# The system is crumbling, protect your wealth or suffer the fallout.
---
Elon Musk has avoided two major financial crises before. He pulled Tesla and SpaceX back from the brink of collapse and built two of the most valuable companies in history.

Now, he's sounding the alarm about America's $36 trillion debt time bomb that could destroy the fabric of our society.

While head of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) under President Trump, Musk exposed just how bad things are:

✅ Runaway government spending has pushed national debt to unsustainable levels
✅ The Federal Reserve's rate hikes are squeezing the economy, making inflation irreversible
✅ The stock market is on shaky ground, putting traditional 401(k)s, IRAs, and TSPs at risk

With Trump back in charge, major spending cuts are coming. While necessary, these cuts may send shockwaves through Wall Street, creating unpredictable market turbulence.

That's why financial elites aren't waiting to react, they're moving their wealth now.

For the everyday American who's worked hard to build their nest egg, Trump preserved a IRS loophole that allows you to protect your retirement savings before billions in American wealth are lost.

name: Voter Action Project
subject: Another case headed to the Supreme Court about mail-in ballots
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
DemDocket: SCOTUS to Hear GOP Lawsuit That Could Radically Restrict Mail Voting [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=111225_1&amount=25]
Team: Mail-in ballots are under attack again.
This week, the Supreme Court agreed to hear arguments in RNC v. Wetzel, a lawsuit challenging Mississippi’s law allowing a five-day grace period for late-arriving ballots mailed by Election Day. In simpler terms, it's a case about whether your vote will count if you mail it on or before Election Day, but your ballot arrives after Election Day.
It's clear why a political party -- in this case, the Republican National Committee (RNC) -- is claiming that states must reject ballots that arrive after Election Day. Doing so would potentially disenfranchise a large number of voters, restricting the number of participants in an election. And the data is clear, over the last several election cycles, Republicans have been outnumbered in the use of mail-in ballots.
The RNC is making a blunt decision that their ability to win elections matters more than your vote. It's a cynical attempt to stay in power by suppressing ballots that are delivered late by the postal service. It's the kind of partisan work that shows they'd rather believe in their own power and control than the principles of democracy, of a country where all our votes should count, regardless of party.
Team: We cannot allow blatant partisanship to overrule our right to vote. Help make every mail-in ballot count in Mississippi and beyond with $25 or whatever you can today to make every voice heard in our elections >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=111225_1&amount=25]
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=111225_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=111225_1&amount=25]
GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=111225_1&amount=50] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=111225_1&amount=100]
GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=111225_1&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=111225_1&amount=35]
Our position is clear: If your ballot was postmarked by Election Day, it should count.
This shouldn't be a partisan question. No political party -- Democrat, Republican, Green, Working Families, Libertarian, Reform, Conservative -- should be against voting. No party in our democracy should want to restrict the fundamental right of our fellow citizens to make their voice heard through voting.
But now we're facing a case where the Supreme Court’s decision could strike down or uphold laws in 16 states (and Washington D.C.) allowing late-arriving ballots postmarked on or before Election Day to be counted if they arrive within a set grace period.
How do we know what is at stake? Data from the 2024 Massachusetts elections shows that their three-day grace period for ballots to arrive after Election Day resulted in a 60% drop in rejected ballots as compared to no grace period. That means thousands of votes still counted, more than 1.5% of the total vote count. In a close election, that 1.5% could easily mean the difference between winning and losing.
Our fundamental right to vote should matter so much more than a partisan desperation to win. Chip in $25 or whatever you can to help us defend the use of mail-in ballots and make every vote count in elections nationwide >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=111225_1&amount=25]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=111225_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=111225_1&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax-deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=a5pyZv4ASbmzt4xkjRgaP%2F8LQcBOPw8JVRfIL%2FNwfvUYykZsHxnnY5BPOE4MVbQZJaITNZtW95aJrVlJdYvIVhsug4QbDWi5Y0jwVyOJ0QxwuQqKTr2ZIVIW8hfjd6YFlsMB0Nyc37OM9iUeWJlzQMTLmQt6ppRgMl1Ki3%2FnYMh5HsG5BEbMbb%2FgPc1g7xTDlKB4Wf7tz2HBmQOOg9b6o9wXTKJAbkALwKDonUjA0VSEI1JrtEjwNsqPlsrpMoqzEHC9J7Dfygi5GlmvXHmULyn8aLA7z%2Bf4yVPuLuRB3rHvnzPZ4x4XRGJev%2F0dawQHxiUHbXkaRFmRCjDxaAVAinN6U%2Fq2c20vp2obmzYcXO7XO47Q1XGN%2FXMidw26SSpSeHlxgpLYGVKQcd5ETAr9lrUbgY7bmQEPW2IWwdYaAKQ%3D .

name: Forward Party
subject: The shutdown MIGHT be ending, but the dysfunction that caused it is still there
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Forward Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
At Forward, we are creating a movement that brings together independent lawmakers, voters, and state partners to put people over parties and reject shutdown politics.

| | [ ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=JgyyvCfQwldlSeD7KJl_Ih7269o_4hwqOhPs1_PHvgI&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mid_november_low_dollar&n=1)
---
| Peter, Good news: there are signs the government shutdown could finally be ending. But that doesn’t mean everything is okay. The deal still [leaves critical programs, like health care subsidies, hanging in the balance.](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=oQtRf_4FdoKC3hjgRdclGROzQMiNM3xwiIqu_u3vLZu7OoEDuS9jadMq8MCsDC16WXlXStllSQou0XYCWWEG4wAi9-BNZbeNTZsHo4msCKx_A6cJTNenWTCsXiE_gTY5&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mid_november_low_dollar&n=2) In other words: the shutdown might be ending, but the dysfunction that caused it is still there. **That’s why we’re building something different. Will you chip in $25, $50, or $100 to help us fix what Washington won’t?** | [](https://home.forwardparty.com/donate?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mid_november_low_dollar&n=3 "Chip in now to power independent leaders") [Chip in now to power independent leaders](https://home.forwardparty.com/donate?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mid_november_low_dollar&n=3)
---
At Forward, we are creating a movement that brings together independent lawmakers, voters, and state partners to put people over parties and reject shutdown politics.
Just last week in Arizona, we announced a partnership with the newly-formed Arizona Independent Party to give independent candidates for local, state and federal offices a spot on the ballot and independent voters more choices on Election Day.
Wins like this show what’s possible when independents work together. Now it’s time to take it nationwide.
**In order to scale this momentum heading into 2026, we need your help. Will you chip in $25, $50, or $100 today to help us:**
* Recruit and launch candidates ready to replace chaos with cooperation
* Build and scale our campaign infrastructure so independents can finally compete on equal ground

[](https://home.forwardparty.com/donate?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mid_november_low_dollar&n=4 "Donate today to defend the Constitution and
elect Forward leaders!") [Donate today!](https://home.forwardparty.com/donate?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mid_november_low_dollar&n=5)
---
If you’ve already contributed this year, thank you. If not: this is the moment.
**When Washington shuts down, the rest of us have to step up.** It's time for something new. Your donation today helps pragmatic independents not just protest but compete and win in 2026.
Thank you for believing we can do better.
**Not Left. Not Right. Forward.**
In action and optimism,
Forward Party
[](https://home.forwardparty.com/?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mid_november_low_dollar&n=6)

name: JoAnna Mendoza (via Emily Randall for Congress)
subject: Peter, did you see this?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
See below for an update from JoAnna Mendoza, the Democrat running in our adopted swing district, Arizona’s 6th! –Team Emily

–Team Emily_
* * *
Hi Peter, it’s JoAnna Mendoza. Emily said I could reach out with a very exciting update: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119143457/582083858/989746443?amount=10&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.11.12_MendozaPoll_90do&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZDI1OTc4OS1lNGJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gJ6y_wYQbYzu4zIgIOgl5u9rnx8VUKbKLJeBnj-84ys=) **Flipping AZ-06 blue is essential to flip the House next year – and as of this morning, we’re on track to do it. Our underdog movement is actually leading in the polls!** Peter, this is all thanks to everyday folks who chipped in and helped us build this campaign from the ground up. Without this community, we wouldn’t be able to keep reaching voters where they are and bringing them into our fight to flip AZ-06 blue. **But this poll is sure to attract attention from GOP megadonors in and out of my district, and we need all hands on deck to combat the wave of attack ads and misinformation they’re about to throw at me.** Our strategy of reaching voters on the ground and online is working, but we need your support to reach the voters who will decide this race – and the House. [_**So please, will you split a donation between Emily Randall and my campaign to flip AZ-06 blue? >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119143458/582083859/989746443?amount=10&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.11.12_MendozaPoll_90do&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZDI1OTc4OS1lNGJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gJ6y_wYQbYzu4zIgIOgl5u9rnx8VUKbKLJeBnj-84ys=) Emily Randall is proud to represent Washington’s 6th District in Congress. She grew up in a working-class Union household in Port Orchard and was the first in her family to attend college. After serving in the State Senate and passing nearly 200 bills, Emily made history in 2024 as the first queer Latina elected to Congress. Now, Emily is fighting for WA working families in D.C. and organizing to take back the House. _**Can you chip in $6 or any amount and join our team?**_ [DONATE NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119143465/582083866/1120488449?refcode2=footer&amount=6&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.11.12_MendozaPoll_90do&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZDI1OTc4OS1lNGJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gJ6y_wYQbYzu4zIgIOgl5u9rnx8VUKbKLJeBnj-84ys=)
