name: Aaron Regunberg from All right, then, I'll go to hell
subject: Americans Hate AI. Will Democrats Join Them?
body:
There's widespread anger at the AI industry--and last week's elections showed that it's a winning issue.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..KAsTZO2Tnk23VV7smqsqCUL1OQ3r87stirnDL8bI_oM?) for more
---
# [Americans Hate AI. Will Democrats Join Them?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=178692386&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NjkyMzg2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjUzNTAsImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1NzM1MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.JO33yhMfWWtO9MajLbrtd5-TsOeGGLmZUx_O5I74up4)
### There's widespread anger at the AI industry--and last week's elections showed that it's a winning issue.
| | [Aaron Regunberg](https://substack.com/@aaronregunberg)
---
| Nov 12
---
| [](https://substack.com/@aaronregunberg)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=178692386&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NjkyMzg2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2Mjk2NTM1MCwiZXhwIjoxNzY1NTU3MzUwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDk4MTE3MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.eeNtT3la59e37BU0N67Hf6sc2HJJcMzGOoRN0Jj53xA&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=178692386&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NjkyMzg2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjUzNTAsImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1NzM1MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.JO33yhMfWWtO9MajLbrtd5-TsOeGGLmZUx_O5I74up4&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=178692386&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NjkyMzg2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjUzNTAsImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1NzM1MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.JO33yhMfWWtO9MajLbrtd5-TsOeGGLmZUx_O5I74up4)
---
| | [](https://substack..5f3go-zfTOZcmhF7GZ_venyFa-Ejd7sS4fKaytZbbcM?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/aaronregunberg/p/americans-hate-ai-will-democrats?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

It's the hubris that really galls.
Last week, while [recording a podcast](https://substack.com/redirect/84af36f7-5bb5-4c8d-86ae-05486d11ef7a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at the abundance-themed "Progress Conference," OpenAI CEO Sam Altman [said](https://substack.com/redirect/7a606011-faaf-4b97-9a6a-e66759b2f249?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "When something gets sufficiently huge, whether or not they are on paper, the federal government is kind of the insurer of last resort, as we've seen in various financial crises ... Given the magnitude of what I expect AI's economic impact to look like, I do think the government ends up as the insurer of last resort."
It would be ill-advised, amid the populist surge in American politics, for even a well-liked leader of a well-liked industry to come out and say they're expecting American taxpayers to bail them out if and when the need arises. Altman is [not well-liked](https://substack.com/redirect/43bea7e1-66c0-4aef-9267-717ccd6eadb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and neither is [his industry](https://substack.com/redirect/9f49c7d7-1d31-43bb-8480-fa46900ffb65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In fact, AI billionaires may soon become among the [top villains](https://substack.com/redirect/8f34502e-c493-4294-9b76-393478e71373?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in American society. It's a dynamic that could provide Democrats with the perfect wedge issue to ride back to power--if they can muster the political courage to take the people's side over [some of the country's wealthiest corporations](https://substack.com/redirect/909163ef-fd96-42ba-b60e-941b796eb8ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9hYXJvbnJlZ3VuYmVyZy5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWNjb3VudCIsInAiOjE3ODY5MjM4NiwicyI6NDk4MTE3MCwiZiI6dHJ1ZSwidSI6OTA1MDExOTIsImlhdCI6MTc2Mjk2NTM1MCwiZXhwIjoyMDc4NTQxMzUwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMCIsInN1YiI6ImxpbmstcmVkaXJlY3QifQ.NOoLkw_uOZVhDdaFtdqNB8-KxhBDIW04N2NzxJ2VKCs?)
Last week's election results demonstrated the first concrete proof of the potency of an anti-AI message, as the effects of AI data centers on utility bills played a significant role in several major Democratic victories. In New Jersey, Governor-elect Mikie Sherrill's [closing argument](https://substack.com/redirect/0c78d06b-18a2-4071-8716-9bb43d3e72bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was a pledge to freeze electricity rates, which have [soared](https://substack.com/redirect/13686e1f-9ea7-49cb-9343-fbe49a54211b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) because of data center demand. In Virginia, Governor-elect Abigail Spanberger won after [pledging](https://substack.com/redirect/2b6eadd7-0526-40af-b969-8c36754b68ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to make data centers "pay their own way," and many Democrats went even further. At least one candidate, John McAuliff, flipped a seat in the House of Delegates by focusing [almost entirely](https://substack.com/redirect/0986a8f4-9721-42be-b2dd-ec39773c7b75?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on tying his Republican opponent to the "unchecked growth" of data centers, with an ad that asked, "Do you want more of these in your backyard?" And in Georgia, Democrats won their first non-federal statewide races in decades, earning 60 percent of the vote against two Republican members of the Public Service Commission by [criticizing](https://substack.com/redirect/832e3a2d-8022-49ce-b4f8-9f1fa78d252a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Big Tech "sweetheart deals" and [campaigning](https://substack.com/redirect/8a10e2f8-6e06-4497-b73d-9d2a013f1692?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for policies "to ensure that the communities that they're extracting from" don't end up with their "water supplies ... tapped out or their energy ... maxed out."
As these election results suggest, data center opposition is remarkably bipartisan. A large proportion of Big Tech's AI infrastructure buildout is occurring in red states, like Indiana, Texas, Ohio, and West Virginia, where data centers have added [billions of dollars](https://substack.com/redirect/53ecbf8f-a7de-48f6-bdc9-cc3c50abf68a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to household energy bills and inspired serious hostility from Democrats and Republicans alike. As one conservative anti-data center activist in Oklahoma [said](https://substack.com/redirect/4b3608c3-b1de-43f7-b0be-3cfe080463b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "We'd probably see our elections flip, too, if people started running on it." Or as Virginia state Senator Danica Roem [put it](https://substack.com/redirect/e3e7cfb8-8372-44b8-8738-77034d43746b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "There are a lot of people willing to be single-issue, split-ticket voters based on this."
You would think that such an intensely cross-cutting issue would be at the center of Democrats' monthslong debate over their party's future. But many of the voices that have been [most vocal ](https://substack.com/redirect/4449d8c7-4bdb-4a0e-b467-eb1ea64f2e28?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)on the need for the Democratic Party to begin competing in Republican-leaning areas have been entirely silent on this topic. None of the recent reports from centrist and abundance advocacy groups like [WelcomePAC](https://substack.com/redirect/b5982436-82ab-4d37-88ed-2e59f86dcd8d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or [Searchlight](https://substack.com/redirect/b553723f-d0d1-405f-8d1a-a9c8fb6716e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) even mention AI or data centers--which makes more sense when you learn that these organizations are themselves [funded](https://substack.com/redirect/b1163d64-81da-4c5a-bbd7-a9c87037520a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by [Big Tech](https://substack.com/redirect/9f366b0d-bee0-4d4e-8dbe-44cfd17bc970?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) billionaires, who have also begun outfitting [massive super PACs](https://substack.com/redirect/1303cd04-b488-4aa9-bd6c-1885d4e3ee68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with hundreds of millions of dollars to influence pro-AI policies in Washington.
Here is a clear and simple way for Democrats to transform their party's brand to appeal to working Americans who are being crushed by high costs and are angry at a system that feels rigged against them. Because, as electorally compelling as an anti-AI position is today, it will become politically essential in the years ahead--for three important reasons.
First, there is the likelihood that the AI industry is [building up a bubble](https://substack.com/redirect/02bc3ee5-2045-4c93-90c6-075270484707?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that, when it bursts, will take down the global economy. OpenAI is currently valued at $500 billion, and is laying the [groundwork](https://substack.com/redirect/b4661c14-acb1-4e48-9600-a60b4e7a22dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for a $1 trillion IPO. This valuation is speculative, rather than based on the company's earnings, which are abysmal. In the first half of 2025, OpenAI made $4.3 billion in revenue and posted a net loss of $13.5 billion, a ratio that is only getting worse as its capital expenditures continue skyrocketing. Even more infuriating, AI billionaires seem to be purposefully __ inflating this bubble with sketchy [circular financing strategies](https://substack.com/redirect/fb5fe118-d015-4e5a-94a2-831e24b7a098?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), in which companies like OpenAI receive billions of dollars in investments from chipmakers, cloud computing companies, and other Big Tech corporations, which they then send back to those same companies to pay for chips, computing power, and other services. Indeed, just this week it was [reported](https://substack.com/redirect/f8df42e5-8094-4d0e-82ee-6b4ae6b42bf7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that Michael Burry, the investor made famous in _The Big Short_ for his pre-2008 bets against the U.S. housing market, has placed 80 percent of his portfolio into investments shorting Big AI companies.
When this thing pops, it won't be the filthy rich scammers behind the bubble who will lose out. It will be regular people: One former IMF chief economist [estimates](https://substack.com/redirect/f20afd07-a044-4443-a971-4ef2e4786269?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that a crash could wipe out $20 trillion in wealth held by American households. Meanwhile, as Altman's quote at the top of this essay foreshadowed, AI billionaires are already maneuvering for taxpayers to bail them out of the crisis they are willfully creating, with OpenAI's CFO recently suggesting that a government loan guarantee might be [necessary](https://substack.com/redirect/e4dee806-343d-4457-a1b8-a8c51068535e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to fund the investments needed to keep the company afloat. It's pretty easy to predict how all of this will go over with the voting public.
Beyond the profoundly compelling politics of a market crash and bailout, there's the equally potent issue of AI-driven job losses. These hits have already begun. Last summer, IBM [replaced](https://substack.com/redirect/49825d78-3811-474c-93ea-cfb68ba4cff6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) hundreds of employees with AI chatbots, and UPS, JPMorgan Chase, and Wendy's have all begun following suit. The CEO of Anthropic has [warned](https://substack.com/redirect/7088c2ca-ee28-45d6-bb88-1b73d91b1330?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that AI could lead to the loss of half of all entry-level white-collar jobs; McKinsey [estimated](https://substack.com/redirect/267bb8c7-048d-4af6-845a-4d7743815335?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that AI could automate 60 to 70 percent of employees' work activities; and a recent [report](https://substack.com/redirect/267bb8c7-048d-4af6-845a-4d7743815335?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from Senator Bernie Sanders and the Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Committee found that AI could replace 89 percent of fast food and counter workers, 64 percent of accountants and 47 percent of truck drivers. The anger that such dislocations will generate against "[clankers](https://substack.com/redirect/1cc10e5e-bc8d-4c87-8787-3aa3138a4793?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"--yes, anti-AI frustration has already inspired the explosion of a Gen Z meme-slur--is hard to overstate, potentially exceeding the anti-NAFTA resentment that Trump rode to the White House in 2016. Democrats should be doing everything in their power to get on the right side of that voter fury.
Finally, there's the more immediate, [enshittifying](https://substack.com/redirect/47c9c22f-35e1-4da6-aeef-07425c217ffe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) reality of AI's actual effects on our daily lives, which bear so little resemblance to the tech-utopian tall tales that Big Tech has been spinning. We were promised cures for cancer; what we got instead was a technology that's impoverishing [millions of artists](https://substack.com/redirect/fba1a999-1fc0-4c62-8c1a-15bb4f8f6d44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), undermining the economic foundations of journalism, filling the internet with unbearable slop, forcing itself into every nook and cranny of our digital landscape, damaging public trust in our elections, exacerbating the epidemic of [loneliness and alienation](https://substack.com/redirect/9f90c07c-1ae3-4f46-9291-2a6ad9ee5690?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) among adolescents, inspiring [genuine psychosis](https://substack.com/redirect/34d2346d-e716-4bb9-8cd8-9b3d5dc65d65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in users, [undermining](https://substack.com/redirect/5dde635e-9f50-4ea4-ad62-6a3bc4fec442?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) our critical thinking skills, [exposing](https://substack.com/redirect/3800e7c7-73a1-4522-be5d-c2d2f6d2d826?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) children to sexual content, and even, in far too many instances, [egging on](https://substack.com/redirect/1d53f0e4-4e03-4e8b-861d-0876d4b3cb13?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) young people to kill themselves.
Americans understand they're getting a raw deal on this. A [Pew Research Center poll](https://substack.com/redirect/9f49c7d7-1d31-43bb-8480-fa46900ffb65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the spring found that only 17 percent of Americans believe AI will have a positive impact on the United States over the next 20 years, while only 11 percent say they are more excited than concerned about the increased use of AI in daily life, compared to 51 percent who say the opposite. And a January [Axios poll](https://substack.com/redirect/8f34502e-c493-4294-9b76-393478e71373?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found that 72 percent of Americans have a negative opinion about how AI will impact the spread of false information, with 64 percent saying the same about its impacts on social connections.
Of course, not everyone feels this way. But it's worth noting that concerns about AI are particularly prevalent among the working-class voters that Democrats have felt the greatest urgency to win back. According to an April [Quinnipiac poll](https://substack.com/redirect/f536be95-e68a-4e2c-b060-876580f56daa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), while the majority of wealthy Americans thought AI would do more good than harm in their day-to-day lives, Americans earning below $50,000 thought it would do more harm than good by a two-to-one margin, with only slightly better numbers for Americans in the $50,000-$100,000 income range.
All of these interconnected dynamics are coming together to create a massive opening for Democrats to eviscerate the Trump administration, which has gone all in on fast-tracking permitting for AI infrastructure, rolling out the [red carpet](https://substack.com/redirect/db80b5e0-1751-4ecd-a5e6-83e5b3502853?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for AI billionaires, and even [threatening](https://substack.com/redirect/8c1ac196-18f4-4345-a059-e7194dfff736?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to cut federal funding for states that try to regulate AI. But to fully exploit that opening, Democrats must be able to point to an authentic and believable--in short, a real--record of standing up to the industry. That may require a fight with members of the party's corporatist wing, who remain ever-ready to sacrifice Democrats' electoral odds at the altar of their big-business backers.
Confronting AI's modern-day robber barons could be rocket fuel for the Democrats in 2026 and 2028. The party is increasingly focused on oligarchic power and affordability concerns, and here's a winning issue that unites those messages. It's such an obvious opportunity that it should not require much persuasion. But can the Democrats see that--or can they be made to?
* * *
This essay appeared in _[The New Republic](https://substack.com/redirect/309a2324-9ad3-4894-bebd-1d0c95f00893?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_. They're great, check them out!

clean_body: There's widespread anger at the AI industry--and last week's elections showed that it's a winning issue. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more There's widespread anger at the AI industry--and last week's elections showed that it's a winning issue. | | | Nov 12 | | | | | | | | | | | | It's the hubris that really galls. Last week, while at the abundance-themed "Progress Conference," OpenAI CEO Sam Altman , "When something gets sufficiently huge, whether or not they are on paper, the federal government is kind of the insurer of last resort, as we've seen in various financial crises ... Given the magnitude of what I expect AI's economic impact to look like, I do think the government ends up as the insurer of last resort." It would be ill-advised, amid the populist surge in American politics, for even a well-liked leader of a well-liked industry to come out and say they're expecting American taxpayers to bail them out if and when the need arises. Altman is , and neither is . In fact, AI billionaires may soon become among the in American society. It's a dynamic that could provide Democrats with the perfect wedge issue to ride back to power--if they can muster the political courage to take the people's side over . ? Last week's election results demonstrated the first concrete proof of the potency of an anti-AI message, as the effects of AI data centers on utility bills played a significant role in several major Democratic victories. In New Jersey, Governor-elect Mikie Sherrill's was a pledge to freeze electricity rates, which have because of data center demand. In Virginia, Governor-elect Abigail Spanberger won after to make data centers "pay their own way," and many Democrats went even further. At least one candidate, John McAuliff, flipped a seat in the House of Delegates by focusing on tying his Republican opponent to the "unchecked growth" of data centers, with an ad that asked, "Do you want more of these in your backyard?" And in Georgia, Democrats won their first non-federal statewide races in decades, earning 60 percent of the vote against two Republican members of the Public Service Commission by Big Tech "sweetheart deals" and for policies "to ensure that the communities that they're extracting from" don't end up with their "water supplies ... tapped out or their energy ... maxed out." As these election results suggest, data center opposition is remarkably bipartisan. A large proportion of Big Tech's AI infrastructure buildout is occurring in red states, like Indiana, Texas, Ohio, and West Virginia, where data centers have added to household energy bills and inspired serious hostility from Democrats and Republicans alike. As one conservative anti-data center activist in Oklahoma , "We'd probably see our elections flip, too, if people started running on it." Or as Virginia state Senator Danica Roem , "There are a lot of people willing to be single-issue, split-ticket voters based on this." You would think that such an intensely cross-cutting issue would be at the center of Democrats' monthslong debate over their party's future. But many of the voices that have been the need for the Democratic Party to begin competing in Republican-leaning areas have been entirely silent on this topic. None of the recent reports from centrist and abundance advocacy groups like or even mention AI or data centers--which makes more sense when you learn that these organizations are themselves by billionaires, who have also begun outfitting with hundreds of millions of dollars to influence pro-AI policies in Washington. Here is a clear and simple way for Democrats to transform their party's brand to appeal to working Americans who are being crushed by high costs and are angry at a system that feels rigged against them. Because, as electorally compelling as an anti-AI position is today, it will become politically essential in the years ahead--for three important reasons. First, there is the likelihood that the AI industry is that, when it bursts, will take down the global economy. OpenAI is currently valued at $500 billion, and is laying the for a $1 trillion IPO. This valuation is speculative, rather than based on the company's earnings, which are abysmal. In the first half of 2025, OpenAI made $4.3 billion in revenue and posted a net loss of $13.5 billion, a ratio that is only getting worse as its capital expenditures continue skyrocketing. Even more infuriating, AI billionaires seem to be purposefully inflating this bubble with sketchy , in which companies like OpenAI receive billions of dollars in investments from chipmakers, cloud computing companies, and other Big Tech corporations, which they then send back to those same companies to pay for chips, computing power, and other services. Indeed, just this week it was that Michael Burry, the investor made famous in The Big Short for his pre-2008 bets against the U.S. housing market, has placed 80 percent of his portfolio into investments shorting Big AI companies. When this thing pops, it won't be the filthy rich scammers behind the bubble who will lose out. It will be regular people: One former IMF chief economist that a crash could wipe out $20 trillion in wealth held by American households. Meanwhile, as Altman's quote at the top of this essay foreshadowed, AI billionaires are already maneuvering for taxpayers to bail them out of the crisis they are willfully creating, with OpenAI's CFO recently suggesting that a government loan guarantee might be to fund the investments needed to keep the company afloat. It's pretty easy to predict how all of this will go over with the voting public. Beyond the profoundly compelling politics of a market crash and bailout, there's the equally potent issue of AI-driven job losses. These hits have already begun. Democrats should be doing everything in their power to get on the right side of that voter fury. Finally, there's the more immediate, reality of AI's actual effects on our daily lives, which bear so little resemblance to the tech-utopian tall tales that Big Tech has been spinning. We were promised cures for cancer; what we got instead was a technology that's impoverishing , undermining the economic foundations of journalism, filling the internet with unbearable slop, forcing itself into every nook and cranny of our digital landscape, damaging public trust in our elections, exacerbating the epidemic of among adolescents, inspiring in users, our critical thinking skills, children to sexual content, and even, in far too many instances, young people to kill themselves. **DPT, ISSA Nutrition Specialist, Co-Founder**
**NativePath**
[ Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/2e423c00-fb48-42dd-86e4-dc539ee54f0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
**P.S.** If you or someone you love has ever taken a statin, **[this could be the most important article you read this week](https://substack.com/redirect/2e423c00-fb48-42dd-86e4-dc539ee54f0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[.](https://substack.com/redirect/2e423c00-fb48-42dd-86e4-dc539ee54f0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# **Mamdani says he will reach out to Trump before taking office**
Mayor-elect Zohran Mamdani **[confirmed Tuesday he will contact President Trump](https://substack.com/redirect/ef0f91e9-ea9f-4742-939d-13e6eda0f9ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** before taking office on New Year's Day, telling NBC New York the relationship between City Hall and the White House "will be critical to the success of the city," though he didn't specify when he would make contact but said he would be "proactive" in trying to thwart Trump's immigration and crime policies.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef0f91e9-ea9f-4742-939d-13e6eda0f9ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Willing to work with Trump on shared goals:** The 34-year-old mayor-elect said he would tell Trump "I'm here to work for the benefit of everyone that calls the city home, and that wherever there is a possibility for working together toward that end, I'm ready," adding he's willing to discuss lowering the cost of living or delivering cheaper groceries, but "if [the administration's policies] are to the expense of those New Yorkers, I will fight."
* **Trump softens tone after threatening federal takeover:** While Trump threatened before the November 4 election to withhold federal money from NYC and execute a federal takeover if the "Communist" Mamdani was elected, the president slightly softened his tone after the Democratic socialist's victory, saying last week "I hope it works out for New York" and "we'll help him a little bit, maybe," with Mamdani revealing he has consulted with Gov. Kathy Hochul about responding to Trump's threats.

* * *
# **Students launch TPUSA chapter after teacher mocks Kirk**
Students at Barrington High School in Rhode Island **[are launching a Turning Point USA chapter](https://substack.com/redirect/2f03411f-0bfa-4d24-94ac-884411f095da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to promote conservative values after social studies teacher Benjamin Fillo posted a video mocking Charlie Kirk following his assassination, saying Kirk "thought he proved how tough he was with his words... What a piece of garbage. Look what happens... Bye, Charlie!"
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2f03411f-0bfa-4d24-94ac-884411f095da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Teacher placed on leave, school charges $117K for records:** Fillo was placed on administrative leave after his video post, and when conservative activist Nicole Solas requested public records to review his curriculum including handouts, assignments, videos, and emails, Barrington Public Schools said it would charge her $117,132 to gather all the materials.
* **Students face pushback but remain committed:** Brayden Ryan, vice president of the chapter, said starting the group has been "difficult" due to the area's political demographics, with some students campaigning to reinstate Fillo through petitions, but the conservative students want to create "a free and safe space" for others to "speak their own personal values, such as their religious beliefs and their political beliefs."
* **Chapter president emphasizes respectful approach:** Caleb Kaplan, president of the TPUSA chapter and fellow freshman, told Fox News Digital that despite the pushback, "we don't reward hate with hate" and instead "we push harder and we try to recruit more people to join," while the school district confirmed the group is following the standard protocol for proposed student clubs.

* * *
# TODAY: House to vote on bill to end longest shutdown in US history
The House will **[vote Wednesday to reopen the federal government](https://substack.com/redirect/0b41c479-e749-4e4e-9ed8-b7634a498d25?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after a bipartisan funding bill cleared the Rules Committee overnight, bringing lawmakers closer to resolving the longest shutdown in U.S. history.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/0b13bf21-da26-4df9-9133-7b3494240c3c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Rules Committee approval** : The deal passed after a six-hour hearing that concluded around 1 a.m. Wednesday, with all Republicans voting in favor and all Democrats voting against. GOP leadership expects the bill to pass the full House with near-unanimous Republican support, advancing the legislation to President Donald Trump for his signature.
* **Democratic amendments rejected** : House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries made an unexpected appearance to advocate for his amendment extending COVID-era Obamacare subsidies for three years, though all Democratic amendments were defeated.
* **Legislation specifics** : The bill extends fiscal year 2025 funding until Jan. 30 and incorporates three appropriations bills covering Agriculture, FDA, Veterans Affairs, military construction and the legislative branch. It reverses Trump administration layoffs from October with affected workers receiving back pay. The Senate approved the legislation 60-40 Monday night with eight Democrats coming to support Republicans.
* **Points of contention** : Democrats and some Republicans objected to a provision permitting GOP senators to sue the federal government for $500,000 for covertly obtaining their phone records during Jack Smith's investigation. While Senate Democrats secured a guaranteed vote on Obamacare subsidies, Speaker Mike Johnson offered no comparable commitment in the House.

name: Brian Lenney
subject: The $26 Million Question: Why Are Idaho Taxpayers Funding Teachers’ Side Hustles?
body:
Under the hoof of the unregulated online learning platform where teachers collect unreported income and taxpayers pay triple what private alternatives charge

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..byZs2NNFpeRjghWP2YenC9x3DVweyiX1XYUhs2DlXls?) for more
---
# [The $26 Million Question: Why Are Idaho Taxpayers Funding Teachers' Side Hustles?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=178706092&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NzA2MDkyLCJpYXQiOjE3NjI5NjU2OTAsImV4cCI6MTc2NTU1NzY5MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.FU1tX4fpe7V8UknkSwi-LWEVA_D5KT9HRwLkN76r2kM)
### Under the hoof of the unregulated online learning platform where teachers collect unreported income and taxpayers pay triple what private alternatives charge
| | [Brian Lenney](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---
| Nov 12
---
| [](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=178706092&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NzA2MDkyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2Mjk2NTY5MCwiZXhwIjoxNzY1NTU3NjkwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjExNDgyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.PMBksJahCx21VynNW3iHppVV9LIsjE5Ec9T3z2d5bu8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/brianlenney/p/the-26-million-question-why-are-idaho?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/54228256-01bc-484a-85eb-3036fa1f6a90?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The Idaho Digital Learning Alliance (IDLA) was created in 2002 to solve a real problem: rural students who needed access to courses their small schools couldn't offer, like Japanese 3 or AP Physics. It was supposed to be Idaho's answer to online learning, giving students across the state access to specialized courses through a state-run platform.
_Twenty-three years later, IDLA has become something else entirely._
With a $25.8 million annual budget (a 158% increase since 2019) and virtually no oversight since its founding statute was last updated in 2008, IDLA has evolved into a side hustle for public school teachers who collect thousands of dollars in "unreported" income, often (allegedly) while they're supposed to be teaching their regular classes.
If you've never heard of IDLA, you're not alone. I didn;t knwo what it was until I got elected. That;s because it's flown under the radar for two decades, operating as a "governmental entity" that's not quite a state agency, not quite a school district, and not subject to the procurement rules, competitive bidding requirements, or accountability measures that apply to almost every other entity spending taxpayer money in Idaho.
Here's what you need to know about where your tax dollars are going.
## The "Off-the-Books" Payment System
Here's how it works:
Public school teachers sign contracts with IDLA to teach online courses, getting paid between $87.50 and $170 per student enrolled. With a typical class of 30 students, that's $2,625 to $5,100 per course, and when you add in bonus payments of $35 per student, teachers can pocket another $500 to $1,000 per course.
So, a teacher running multiple IDLA courses while holding down their full-time teaching job can easily make an extra $3,000 to $5,000 per course, which means that per semester, they could be pulling in an additional $10,000 to $20,000 in income.
_**And here 's the kicker: this money is completely off the books. **_
It doesn't appear in the salary data reported to the legislature or in any public accounting of teacher compensation, which means the legislature thinks they know what teachers are being paid when they actually don't.
## The Problem Gets Worse
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fe5d26c2-3590-4fb6-9e06-18bb24a90c6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
These teachers aren't just moonlighting in the evenings; many are teaching IDLA courses during regular school hours while they're being paid their full-time salary by their school district.
Think about that. You're paying a teacher's full salary to teach at your local school, and that teacher is also getting paid thousands more by IDLA, _but during the school day, they 're managing IDLA students instead of focusing on the job you're already paying them to do._
Why isn't this prohibited? Why isn't there a rule that says if you're going to teach IDLA courses, you do it on your own time, not during hours covered by your employment contract?
## What Are We Actually Paying For?
I've talked to IDLA parents and students, and the picture they paint is concerning.
This isn't traditional teaching; there's no 1:1 time between students and teachers, and students describe it as an email exchange course where you watch videos, complete assignments, and occasionally email questions. It's correspondence education (maybe like taking a course on YouTube). Some have even had to hire tutors to help with IDLA because they said "the teacher doesn't teach us anything."
_**Is that worth $500+ per student per course?**_
Because that's what IDLA costs when you combine the $445 in state funding with the $40 to $75 in fees that districts and students pay. Compare that to private sector online learning platforms, which charge around $150 per enrollment for the same format, the same correspondence-style instruction, at one-third the cost.
## The PERSI Problem
Here's another oddity. IDLA has roughly 80 employees (not counting the contract teachers), and all of them are enrolled in PERSI, Idaho's public employee retirement system.
But IDLA employees aren't state employees, and they're not technically school employees either. IDLA is defined in statute as a "governmental entity," but it's not a school district, not a state agency, not an independent corporate body. They're in a special category that lets them enjoy public employee benefits while avoiding the accountability and oversight that comes with being an actual state agency or school district.
IDLA teachers are paid more than many teachers around the state, but they're not held to the same standards as school teachers because they don't follow state salary schedules and don't participate in the career ladder system.
## Why Not Let the Market Work?
The state legislature funds IDLA at $445 per course enrollment while districts must pay private sector providers out of their own budgets, which means IDLA is "free" to them (paid by the state).
This creates a distorted market. Of course districts choose IDLA over private providers when they can pick the option that costs them nothing.
But taxpayers are footing a bill that's more than three times what the private market charges, and we're protecting IDLA from competition while paying premium prices for what students describe as correspondence courses.
If IDLA is providing value worth $500 per course, great! Let districts pay for it from their operational budgets and see if they still choose IDLA over cheaper alternatives (or let families use Advanced Opportunities funds to pay for it if they think it's worth it).
But stop forcing all Idaho taxpayers to subsidize a program that costs triple the market rate and has minimal accountability.
## The Transparency Gap
IDLA was created in 2002, and the underlying statute hasn't been updated since 2008, which means for 17 years, IDLA has operated without much supervision, doing whatever it wants.
There are no procurement rules, no requirment to follow state purchasing laws, no competitive bidding on contracts. The board is mostly made up of school district representatives who are also IDLA's customers, with only two independent members.
_When an entity gets nearly $26 million in taxpayer funding, we should expect transparency._
We should know how money is being spent, whether teachers are double-dipping on public salaries, and if we're getting value for our money.
Right now, we don't.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4b8f1682-b6cc-4bca-84c0-d07f1efe8600?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## What Should Change?
The fix isn't complicated:
**Maybe we should just stop letting full-time public school teachers teach IDLA courses during school hours...** if they want the extra income, fine, but do it on your own time, and require that IDLA payments be reported as part of salary data so the legislature knows what teachers are actually making.
**Maybe we change the funding model...** let the entity that benefits pay the cost, whether that's a district paying from their operational budget, a family using Advanced Opportunities funds, or anyone else who wants to use IDLA. If IDLA is worth the price, people will pay, and if not, IDLA will need to deliver better value or adjust their pricing.
**Maybe we stop double-paying ...** if a student is taking an IDLA course during the school day, the district shouldn't get ADA funding for that student while IDLA also gets paid. Pick one.
**Mayeb just kill it overall ...** stop funding IDLA directly and let schools and families choose what works best for them.
_IDLA can continue to exist, but it should compete on merit, not on preferential funding that shields it from market pressures while inflating costs for taxpayers._
Idaho taxpayers are spending $25.8 million per year on a program that pays teachers off-the-books income during school hours, costs three times what private alternatives charge, provides what students describe as correspondence courses with minimal direct instruction, operates with minimal oversight or accountability, and enrolls employees in public retirement systems despite not being a state agency.
That makes no sense and it's not fair to taxpayers.
It's time for the legislature to shine some light on IDLA's budget and practices, because the more people know about how this system actually works, the harder it becomes to justify maintaining it in its current form.

---

clean_body: Releasing the Epstein files is about to get real | The Jane Fonda Climate PAC is standing with Democratic Representative-Elect Adelita Grijalva, who still hasn't been sworn in after her landslide victory back in September. Please read Adelita's email and= = Hi there, it's Adelita Grijalva, the Democrat who’s waited longer than any other representative in recent history to take office after winning a special election. Once I'm sworn in, I'll be the FINAL House Democrat needed to force a floor vote to release the Epstein files. We already know that Trump has lied about the files — and Democrats are closer than ever to getting to the bottom of it. But we need your help to keep the pressure on. Will you rush a split contribution between my campaign and the Jane Fonda Climate PAC today to show Republicans that we will release the files, uncover the corruption, and kick them out of power in 2026? = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split contribution will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, Rep.-Elect Adelita Grijalva Arizona's 7th District Adelita Grijalva is not soliciting more than $5,000 per calendar year from individuals or other federally permissible sources, nor is she soliciting any funds from corporations, labor unions, or any other federally-prohibited sources. | Follow along: | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Peter,

A socialist just took over America's largest city.

clean_body: 💪 Big Gretch Energy 💪 | Hi, folks. You know, in Lansing, they call me governor. But in Detroit... = It’s never been a name I thought about or would have picked, but it’s one I really appreciate. It’s funny, too. To me, Big Gretch is the persona I try to live by — showing up with joy, grit, and fighting like hell for what I believe in, powered by a whole lot of grassroots energy behind me. It reminds me that I’m never in this fight alone. Every day, I see people stepping up to power this movement. Like Anthony in Detroit, who gave $10. Or Nancy in Philadelphia, she pitched in $25. Even Susan in Phoenix, who chipped in $5, made a difference. That’s how we build strength. That’s how we take on the big fights. That’s how we win. But here’s the thing. We’re fast approaching the middle of our fundraising quarter, and we’re not where we need to be to hit our goals. We’re putting big plans into motion, and if we want to go toe-to-toe with the people trying to drag this country backward, we need to pick up the pace. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Anthony, Nancy, Susan — and you. name: Chris Pappas
subject: Public service is about helping
body:
[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1001&akid=3430%2E582294%2ECOnKZc ]Chris Pappas for Senate[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1002&akid=3430%2E582294%2ECOnKZc ]Chris Pappas for Senate

People in New Hampshire understand the system is not
working.

There’s a major concern across the country and here in the Granite State
that GOP special interests and wealthy corporations have an outsized
influence in our political system.

And everywhere I go, I hear from Granite Staters — veterans, parents,
small business owners, people who have done everything right, asking: Why
does the system feel so rigged?

Donald Trump and his fellow MAGA Republicans have abandoned the central
purpose of public service: improving the lives of everyday Americans.
Instead Trump is focused on renovating the White House, screwing over our
farmers, and shutting off food assistance for the poorest people in the
country.

That’s why the system feels broken to so many. Rising health care costs
and the threat to key benefits like SNAP underscore the urgency of this
fight.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251112_2_system.kFOn5m&refcode=em20251112-2&t=1003&akid=3430%2E582294%2ECOnKZc ]That’s why I’m asking: If you're able, will you pitch in
$5 today to support my campaign and send me to the Senate to
fight to fix this broken system?

New Hampshire needs a Senator who does two things: act as a check against
power and fight for you. And that’s what I’ve always done. I’ve stood up
to Big Pharma to lower drug costs. I’ve taken on corporate polluters who
contaminate our air and water and predatory companies that have ripped off
our veterans — because that’s what public servants ought to do.

Public service is about creating a better future that people deserve.

It’s critical that we show up, continue to listen, and learn from the
people of this state. They have an important story to tell. We need to see
more of the Granite State sense of common spirit in our country —
especially in the halls of the U.S. Senate.

I hear from people all across New Hampshire who want to see their leaders
show up, listen, and deliver. They want leaders who are willing to take on
big fights in Washington.

And in a swing state like this one, they want someone who can win. They
want someone who can beat the GOP — something I have done four times in a
row in one of the swingiest districts in the country.

We’re in turbulent times, but here’s what I know: Granite Staters don’t
flinch. We don’t give in. We fight back. With the grit and determination
that built this state, we will get our country back on track: stronger,
fairer, freer, and working for everyone again.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251112_2_system.kFOn5m&refcode=em20251112-2&t=1004&akid=3430%2E582294%2ECOnKZc ]This campaign is only
possible with a strong foundation of grassroots supporters. Can you make
your very first contribution to power our fight for New
Hampshire?

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251112_2_system.XgBDKw&amount=5&refcode=em20251112-2&t=1023&akid=3430%2E582294%2ECOnKZc ]Donate $5 »

Thank you,

Chris Pappas

clean_body: [ ]Chris Pappas for Senate[ ]Chris Pappas for Senate People in New Hampshire understand the system is not working. There’s a major concern across the country and here in the Granite State that GOP special interests and wealthy corporations have an outsized influence in our political system. And everywhere I go, I hear from Granite Staters — veterans, parents, small business owners, people who have done everything right, asking: Why does the system feel so rigged? Donald Trump and his fellow MAGA Republicans have abandoned the central purpose of public service: improving the lives of everyday Americans. Instead Trump is focused on renovating the White House, screwing over our farmers, and shutting off food assistance for the poorest people in the country. That’s why the system feels broken to so many. Rising health care costs and the threat to key benefits like SNAP underscore the urgency of this fight. [ ]That’s why I’m asking: If you're able, will you pitch in $5 today to support my campaign and send me to the Senate to fight to fix this broken system? New Hampshire needs a Senator who does two things: act as a check against power and fight for you. And that’s what I’ve always done. I’ve stood up to Big Pharma to lower drug costs. I’ve taken on corporate polluters who contaminate our air and water and predatory companies that have ripped off our veterans — because that’s what public servants ought to do. Public service is about creating a better future that people deserve. It’s critical that we show up, continue to listen, and learn from the people of this state. They have an important story to tell. We need to see more of the Granite State sense of common spirit in our country — especially in the halls of the U.S. Senate. I hear from people all across New Hampshire who want to see their leaders show up, listen, and deliver. They want leaders who are willing to take on big fights in Washington. And in a swing state like this one, they want someone who can win. They want someone who can beat the GOP — something I have done four times in a row in one of the swingiest districts in the country. We’re in turbulent times, but here’s what I know: Granite Staters don’t flinch. We don’t give in. We fight back. With the grit and determination that built this state, we will get our country back on track: stronger, fairer, freer, and working for everyone again. [ ]This campaign is only possible with a strong foundation of grassroots supporters. Can you make your very first contribution to power our fight for New Hampshire? [ ]Donate $5 » Thank you, Chris Pappas You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:

name: Team Cori
subject: What Cori’s been up to
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori For Us
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act]
Hi Peter,
It’s been just over a month since Cori Bush officially launched her election campaign to represent St. Louis in Congress, and she’s hit the ground running.
Here’s what Cori’s been up to, what’s next, and how you can help keep it going. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-msnbcvideo?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-msnbcvideo?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act]
She appeared on MSNBC’s Velshi [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-msnbcvideo?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act] for her first national interview of the campaign, reminding the country what true people-powered leadership looks like.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-joyreidshow?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-joyreidshow?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act]
She sat down with Joy Reid for one of the most powerful and personal conversations of her career. It was real. It was raw. It was true. And it’s worth the watch. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-joyreidshow?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act]
She marched and spoke at the No Kings Day parade , leaving folks with a powerful message about what our children and our children’s children will have to endure if we don’t stand up and fight Trump’s fascism.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act]
She even ran into the cutest little Wonder Woman, a feminist icon who stands for compassion, courage, and justice.
Cori doesn’t take a dime from corporate PACs. That means you are the reason our movement can continue to fight for justice, housing, and dignity for all. Every dollar you give helps us organize neighborhood by neighborhood, block by block, the way we’ve always built power in St. Louis.
So, before you close this email, will you take 30 seconds to make a contribution? $5, $10, or whatever you can contribute helps power Cori’s campaign. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act]
DONATE TODAY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act]
And if you’re able, make your donation monthly. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act&recurring=1] Monthly donations are what keep this movement going during the slower months. The headlines may quiet down, but our work never stops. Your recurring donations help us plan ahead, pay our organizers, and stay ready for whatever comes our way.
MAKE IT MONTHLY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act&recurring=1]
Because there’s no way in hell Cori is slowing down anytime soon.
Thank you and onwards,
Team Cori
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act] Cori Bush is a pastor, nurse, survivor, and formerly unhoused single mother of two who knows what it means to struggle and what it means to rise .
As a Congresswoman, she has always shown up when it’s hardest: securing disaster relief, lowering insulin costs, and delivering millions for our communities.
Across St. Louis, families are being priced out and pushed out. Cori Bush is fighting back for affordable housing, fair wages, and utilities as a human right. Be part of this people-first movement. Join Team Cori today and sign up to volunteer. [https://www.coribush.org]
DONATE TODAY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act]
DONATE MONTHLY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251112_campaignupdate_act&recurring=1]
Paid for by Cori For Us
Cori For Us
PO Box 12404
St. Louis, MO 63132
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/W8UekF_Ks0eg_1uL1zIi9A2?unsubscribedata=mOxZxWXISXlYXKHXaNwRq6JREmeKumK5ZN6QofPv9H4zbmtk9O4aCI0pct6rs9dm9ZKO7rJN4FEO%2BMuhSui3xxCYNSgYuD1RlYPVGIBKfC7eQu7r0GkeU5Z%2FGXfnzejzeyoklMubhDGMPmcljyHnWW6Se9gVDxm4uZQWgGG0QPSI5Lex6o8zz2kSw25gy8jMpX88u6IptXOMm1kyI0c3pub1FxmXAR65uhmj%2Fpez4mLJimoF6BwCCFLwR6htZnGCLoTCikFOedMJohKLHZfNpkNPDmo50j4kh8QYIRT9%2BsbrqutufN8mR9YXXtCs6NelDSKgmNmkDHXvxfAAqvgoMmFExKVnkhnojD5r%2FgjcsC37KMhiAZYTckpSYnRxxW7yVXGrs03Czzsn0g5vLa91NDrvNdIFQRYoja9TaBCBnc2%2F85PCaFMAZTwbKty2AWR2 .

name: Nancy Mace for Governor
subject: From Nancy Mace: How to Deport More Illegal Aliens
body:
Representative Nancy Mace unveils her plan to help Donald Trump Deport even more illegal aliens than ever before

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us14.campaign-archive.com/?e=ddc57b5ede&i=*|ITERATION_UNIQUE_ID|*&u=f826f615915b06bdee33acac8&id=7c3a80c10f)
---
[|
---](https://nancymace.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f826f615915b06bdee33acac8&id=dfecf0515a&e=ddc57b5ede&i=ca5a50d556)
[|
---](https://nancymace.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f826f615915b06bdee33acac8&id=94be26d48f&e=ddc57b5ede&i=ca5a50d556)
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
# Cost of Living. Inflation. Roads. Bridges. Illegals. Crime. Education. Healthcare.
We’re the only campaign with a plan, all others are copy and pasting our plans and we love our opponents for it! Let’s talk about the invasion of illegal aliens in South Carolina next!
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | **South Carolina is supposed to be a red state, but our state allowed a sanctuary sheriff run wild the last four years - until we stepped in**

Last year, after a whistleblower came forward, I was the first to uncover South Carolina, a state which banned sanctuary cities, had a real, bonafide sanctuary sheriff. This is not an exaggeration. I was horrified to learn...
**Sanctuary Sheriff Kristin Graziano** was letting out illegal aliens back out onto the streets of South Carolina who were murderers, rapists and pedophiles. A lot of them. She refused to work with ICE. And in fact, announced she would NOT work with ICE for the parking lot of El Pincho Taco - I kid you not!

When I stood up last year, I was joined by a handful of state house lawmakers, like Tommy Hartnett, Jordan Pace and others. But not many. Not enough. Certainly, no one running for Governor today said anything about this sanctuary sheriff when they had the chance.
**They were too timid. Too scared. And I don’t know why.**
President Trump’s return to the White House has sparked immediate action on border security and illegal immigration. He declared a national emergency at the southern border on his first day, deploying military support and recommitting to the wall’s construction, reflecting the American people’s historic mandate for stronger border policies. This is your mandate in action.
As President Trump secures our national borders, South Carolina is still under siege. Drug cartels have infiltrated our communities, and over 5,000 crimes committed by illegal aliens were reported in 2024 alone.
A college student’s **illegal alien killer just got one year in jail** for a fatal hit and run, the very _bare minimum_ allowed by South Carolina law. This case was prosecuted by Attorney General Alan Wilson by the way. This isn’t a hit, it’s just a plain fact, you can be the judge.
I am the **ONLY** candidate for Governor who **ended the career of a sanctuary sheriff** in **OUR STATE**.
Here is my plan as **Governor** :
* End ALL sanctuary loopholes
* **$1,000 fine per day for any business employing illegal aliens**
* Work with DHS and ICE to provide facilities to house illegals for deportation. South Carolina already has **over 400 beds** available to house illegals in the deportation process but the state is not using those facilities to assist ICE
* Defund any elected official or agency or department who does not work with ICE
* Deputize state and local law enforcement to assist in deportation

Some of the most complex problems can be solved with some of the simplest, easiest plans to implement. This isn’t rocket science.
Thank you for your attention to this matter.
Sincerely,
[](https://nancymace.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f826f615915b06bdee33acac8&id=510a6f550d&e=ddc57b5ede&i=ca5a50d556)
---
[|
---](https://nancymace.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f826f615915b06bdee33acac8&id=63c04c9bab&e=ddc57b5ede&i=ca5a50d556)

Nancy Mace for Governor 295 Seven Farms Dr Ste C-186 Daniel Island, SC 29492-8001 USA

name: KMR HQ
subject: Today’s vote. Tomorrow’s stakes.
body:
|
#
Friend, Today, the House will vote on the Senate bill to reopen the government – a deal that will lead to health insurance costs skyrocketing for millions of families. **Kristen is voting no** – because families need us to deliver solutions that lower costs, not another short-term fix that fails to deliver. If this bill is signed into law, it will let Affordable Care Act tax credits expire – cutting off the financial help millions of families rely on to afford health insurance. Without that support, premiums could double or even triple overnight for parents, teachers, small business owners, and workers who are already stretched too thin. Kristen is standing firm because families in mid-Michigan shouldn’t pay more just so billionaires can pay less. **This vote is just one example of the fights ahead – and why keeping this seat matters so much.** Kristen has been in Congress for less than a year, but she’s already taken on MAGA extremists – fighting back against cuts to programs like SNAP, standing up to DOGE’s attempts to close Social Security offices in communities like Saginaw, and working to expand the services families depend on. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119147661/582099353/1492467139?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MmI5NDkxYS1lYmJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HE0b8dksevDiQOd9_eQ2orKkOcrfXlHSTIqkSZzXMqs=&emci=0da223cc-d2bf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=92b9491a-ebbf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=27257697) So today, as Kristen stands up to vote no on a deal that would put millions in jeopardy, we’re asking you to stand with her. [**Will you add your name to endorse Kristen’s re-election campaign? We’re hoping to collect at least 500 signatures by midnight.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119147664/582099354/1492467139?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MmI5NDkxYS1lYmJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HE0b8dksevDiQOd9_eQ2orKkOcrfXlHSTIqkSZzXMqs=&emci=0da223cc-d2bf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=92b9491a-ebbf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=27257697) | [Sign your name](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119147671/582099360/1492467139?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MmI5NDkxYS1lYmJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HE0b8dksevDiQOd9_eQ2orKkOcrfXlHSTIqkSZzXMqs=&emci=0da223cc-d2bf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=92b9491a-ebbf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=27257697)
---
Thank you,

clean_body: | Friend, Today, the House will vote on the Senate bill to reopen the government – a deal that will lead to health insurance costs skyrocketing for millions of families. Kristen is voting no – because families need us to deliver solutions that lower costs, not another short-term fix that fails to deliver. If this bill is signed into law, it will let Affordable Care Act tax credits expire – cutting off the financial help millions of families rely on to afford health insurance. Without that support, premiums could double or even triple overnight for parents, teachers, small business owners, and workers who are already stretched too thin. Kristen is standing firm because families in mid-Michigan shouldn’t pay more just so billionaires can pay less. This vote is just one example of the fights ahead – and why keeping this seat matters so much. Kristen has been in Congress for less than a year, but she’s already taken on MAGA extremists – fighting back against cuts to programs like SNAP, standing up to DOGE’s attempts to close Social Security offices in communities like Saginaw, and working to expand the services families depend on. So today, as Kristen stands up to vote no on a deal that would put millions in jeopardy, we’re asking you to stand with her. | Thank you, KMR HQ P.S. If you’ve already endorsed Kristen, we appreciate your support and hope you can reaffirm your endorsement now that the election is less than a year away. | We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Thank you for supporting Kristen McDonald Rivet for Congress! Kristen for Michigan P.O. Box 854 Bay City, MI 48707 United States

The tide is turning in the Empire State and the numbers show that voters are ready for a change: That’s right.Recent polling shows me LEADING the worst Governor in America Kathy Hochul, and I am just getting started. Patriot, the stakes couldn’t be higher... AOC, Zohran Mamdani, and Kathy Hochul are going to destroy New York . Patriot, that’s where you come in. I am humbly asking for your Day One Endorsement. I have a reputation in D.C. for winning hard races, from flipping a deep blue Obama district Republican in my very first campaign, to ousting Liz Cheney from GOP Leadership, I have never backed down from the tough fights that truly matter. So, I’m asking you to stand with me for one more tough fight. . In Liberty, Elise Stefanik Proud New Yorker | | | | | | | | | | _

subject: 250 years of American valor with your free American flag
body:
A powerful symbol. A historic moment. A mission worth joining.| | | [](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=2bc998c41b364435a8a3a57aafeb04ed&_e=tLNYyYA36YvVxn60Uy1aDF9D5HyhaAl1kTsPfaK4Q8q_ZOUXvPRfFPiX3xfohNDtDAwKn1i8gTXGFckgPel1hISTdJxbqwvcbcTAxKJ4n19GRGbUSWGyy6rSDuaTo6NK3a5DdiFVMgfMvv1xCoPcF9ppyhRsuY_GWe3CcwVGN_PqF_tMRH6Q-IPY_bxNkzIhN4sHc406M2M9hHvCPkngh2BMwK6oZHVdYTm6I4VrwF2L__vOz6S6kcTu8u7A-YBCSCajyu6chEtAScR-Mv9FsiOZoINoxcGths4ODTh8bjlikh7PBZ0p9xC-E6BTVusiRIyERLCPgE1pAZMzySKXp9kG1yK3pk-ewC_Z8Mmtz57vCZf7vVyAqtMk1wLf9BXnc0Otjgy-lsQW6jc_oQ-aEp1878gH_mi6oIg-kETU1DHZ7nj9-3_dcuWPq5sr7XyX8B4PAH2QCmw5gEAC18qArHH1baB-GslsSRO8jVVKfBgNwUpmIaZPNE5GU_nNGzaRrVnaOywupMa5Fq-S_GDPAXpNqMaXDHLH2MNRYNuZSQYMSxToBbN_gZGsfOdV0w0v97irjL4bKYYQnKm4jeC7-2fcNnyUrvyia-Pux7raY7v0T5TdM-LgXEpwPPj4Ge4elD1cwGPq9W80gCzKDczPcr9Rzp7x3ATsi5nqWyr_ISVHS-GaKgq2Ixx6PI4pQRCu-CLrBfNfMGkRZuboS5oFVrJdwEql96pYyzp0O7BRGdeuqvLuZ2L3KsSiGJDpxHIFPqNoEAfiByY5QSIAQgqjDw%3D%3D)
---
Peter, For more than two decades, American Veterans Center has been dedicated to honoring the sacrifice and preserving the legacy of our nation's heroes. Each year, we host the **National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C.** , bringing Americans together in a powerful display of remembrance and patriotism. We also proudly produce **" American Valor: A Salute to Our Heroes,"** a nationally televised tribute to the men and women who have served across generations.

But our work goes far beyond public events. At the heart of our mission is a commitment to **preserve the stories of veterans from every era of service** -- from the final voices of World War II, to those who served in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 generation. Through these efforts, we ensure that future generations understand the cost of freedom and the courage it has taken to defend it.

This year, American Veterans Center is marking historic military milestones: the 250th anniversaries of the U.S. Army, Navy, and Marine Corps...the 80th anniversary of the victories that won World War II...and key events in the Korean War 75 years ago, and the Vietnam War which ended 50 years ago this year.

clean_body: A powerful symbol. A historic moment. A mission worth joining.| | | Peter, For more than two decades, American Veterans Center has been dedicated to honoring the sacrifice and preserving the legacy of our nation's heroes. Each year, we host the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. , bringing Americans together in a powerful display of remembrance and patriotism. We also proudly produce " American Valor: A Salute to Our Heroes," a nationally televised tribute to the men and women who have served across generations. But our work goes far beyond public events. At the heart of our mission is a commitment to preserve the stories of veterans from every era of service -- from the final voices of World War II, to those who served in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 generation. Through these efforts, we ensure that future generations understand the cost of freedom and the courage it has taken to defend it. This year, American Veterans Center is marking historic military milestones: the 250th anniversaries of the U.S. Army, Navy, and Marine Corps...the 80th anniversary of the victories that won World War II...and key events in the Korean War 75 years ago, and the Vietnam War which ended 50 years ago this year. We may never have another opportunity like this to recognize their sacrifice on a national scale. This is one of our most ambitious efforts ever, Peter -- a national salute to those who fought to make and keep America free. Together, we 're capturing the stories of WWII veterans before it's too late...and preserving seldom-heard accounts from those who fought in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 wars. And with your gift today, I'll send you a free, full-size American flag as my thank you. To some, it may look like just a flag. But to those who served, it 's a promise kept. It flew over the beaches of Normandy. It sailed with the Pacific fleet. It was stitched into the sleeves of every soldier who never made it home. This flag reminds us of all they sacrificed, and what we 're now called to defend. Let it fly proudly as a reminder that freedom isn't free -- and that this generation will not forget the sacrifices of the last. Peter throughout American history, our nation's flag has stood tall. I hope you 'll take this opportunity to receive your American flag and fly it proudly, today and every day as a symbol AND promise of freedom. Thank you for standing with America's heroes -- past, present, and future. Sincerely, Tim Holbert President American Veterans Center The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. subject: Meet the community behind Democratically
body:
We’re building a movement rooted in empathy, fairness, and action.

[](https://go.m.democratically.org/l/-jet7XmoTVngfMCSc95-7I2HF9WE7_y0tU015FUe7Yc)

Who we are says everything about what we believe.
At Democratically, we’re a collective of writers, advocates, and everyday citizens who know that progress doesn’t happen by accident — it happens because people like you care enough to act.
We bring clarity and purpose to the political noise — offering trusted stories, action guides, and ways to connect that help turn shared values into shared power.
Our vision is a democracy that works for everyone: one that protects rights, uplifts voices, and ensures every person has the freedom to thrive.
When you read, share, or take action through Democratically, you’re not just engaging — you’re helping shape a more compassionate, informed America.

body:
#
Peter,
November SNAP benefits have been restored for over 440,000 Minnesotans, but Donald Trump is still trying to use hungry Americans to play politics.
Governor Walz fought Trump and made sure that Minnesotans are able to have access to food and the resources they need — this in addition to him signing into law free breakfast and lunches for Minnesota’s students and announcing over $4 million in funds to shore up local food banks.
This fight isn’t over though, Peter. Donald Trump’s politics know no lows. **But Tim Walz is fighting back — and he needs your help.**
**[Will you chip in $5 today to help make sure Governor Walz has the resources to fight against Donald Trump and his cruel agenda?](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251112.5TOP2w&amount=5&refcode=em20251112-72&t=1&akid=72%2E34582%2Eo5E3Gs)**

Peter, For more than two decades, American Veterans Center has been dedicated to honoring the sacrifice and preserving the legacy of our nation's heroes. Each year, we host the **National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C.** , bringing Americans together in a powerful display of remembrance and patriotism. We also proudly produce **" American Valor: A Salute to Our Heroes,"** a nationally televised tribute to the men and women who have served across generations.

But our work goes far beyond public events. At the heart of our mission is a commitment to **preserve the stories of veterans from every era of service** -- from the final voices of World War II, to those who served in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 generation. Through these efforts, we ensure that future generations understand the cost of freedom and the courage it has taken to defend it.

This year, American Veterans Center is marking historic military milestones: the 250th anniversaries of the U.S. Army, Navy, and Marine Corps...the 80th anniversary of the victories that won World War II...and key events in the Korean War 75 years ago, and the Vietnam War which ended 50 years ago this year.
---
[](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=97f61ef8fc244b1ea5c724b6f4ea2da5&_e=rzPI9YeF03neJxMsYQZCesbmHmgVHZk8mxlQWU4MOlHFyuqev4kah0MdoizD60fEnlOUxGBHxNm8KslxMlFoWFiypJdeXAUP1lbVSQtbJp_A1kukINGGeU6qnk7n8xIEZYAZLwZL33pqvk33SRDlKGegzLfOaxIAnOBRCCLVi_fgeZXK3PYrdv19XMKElxkcjDgE0adqdLK5-j6oy3oJbuwitEjFhtKq5cPaJFOI1FwYL3Dt0tmZDbvQsL-eo6AsT6l_LtY70T9GUIVZW3usLDPddda1D-N3zDVzTJc-S-rYpNGjMoMRL2TpdDWD2t1WjL4qlsHTBNW7NrGwYzIAOVrYAAfq3W_YfwnZ2P4h8lQjALRf9FiPXM_edEmymM59AoE7ShcvVyDpozQ0y25jsEfWTmIIeorR9vaiOp-wJVNUlFpVy_dyT5mFcyZTRXjQVs3x-pDYd2HzJ7HXQyJ9fXHzgbNzlWhLq-p5EL_C37DcMoZUsYR36Kzv8-8SmAibjtevEqIxDTTn3vEQVRUBYA4_z7m2mhiE4fQHkecxLS-zWioEOaWhD9OeqreWYTuCL4LOyGrK6pfmZRQaN02H73soA_6U3w5Ap9kxLMyam_A7PepyrvuoWmJd5r47CpM5oGDej0xSyR4Zy4N5eU148lxPu4sXEschJcO_dfM4FNmuZo3Bleo2I4pbTlJ-ih-lFN8dfvLMKAabtW5vhmH95_F4C9ggVOq8lZlI_q15BiYHNq2ISri8o6AWjWkf2MN4m2dtDehA5TwbkK9g2DHZ8Q%3D%3D)

This is one of our most ambitious efforts ever, Peter -- a national salute to those who fought to make and keep America free.

**Together, we 're capturing the stories of WWII veterans before it's too late...and preserving seldom-heard accounts from those who fought in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 wars.**

And with your gift today, I'll send you a free, full-size American flag as my thank you.

To some, it may look like just a flag.

_But to those who served, it 's a promise kept._

It flew over the beaches of Normandy.

It sailed with the Pacific fleet.

It was stitched into the sleeves of every soldier who never made it home.

**This flag reminds us of all they sacrificed, and what we 're now called to defend.**

Let it fly proudly as a reminder that freedom isn't free -- and that this generation will not forget the sacrifices of the last.

[**So please, Peter make your gift today. Help us preserve their legacy while there's still time — and proudly fly the flag they fought for.**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=97f61ef8fc244b1ea5c724b6f4ea2da5&_e=rzPI9YeF03neJxMsYQZCesbmHmgVHZk8mxlQWU4MOlHFyuqev4kah0MdoizD60fEnlOUxGBHxNm8KslxMlFoWFiypJdeXAUP1lbVSQtbJp_A1kukINGGeU6qnk7n8xIEZYAZLwZL33pqvk33SRDlKGegzLfOaxIAnOBRCCLVi_fgeZXK3PYrdv19XMKElxkcjDgE0adqdLK5-j6oy3oJbuwitEjFhtKq5cPaJFOI1FwYL3Dt0tmZDbvQsL-eo6AsT6l_LtY70T9GUIVZW3usLLz30fFzfUV5agjnEN6mFLK5yqP0-BNgqs3Up7klv_AuufA0Ta2qC8AR0vi7S_yp_cKd0hgeN_pSCPORgRszOjl3U1L6r2JNrgM20w0JTG5EmL7seqIpJyQSDLfG0nw0ZIizWmR56-xUNgcE1KFnbfCTu-dbcblkNg8w8Cd2J9H5RnH9V3GnQBDe_MpijTLNfZqfuunsvyLlupP4PpEkMCvNL9itZcRWHeDPVAy1cOlDMNicDxeC0BPOPuHy7q6ZOtk8vaUuzQQbKd0OHU0ppN7PTprHOvT_E2K1L2HqgeTHc9VMPa8jsrH1iNengjVVBk1baya2PW83tzu5YuwGc3NUJj7g8aOICihpCjuNSH3IbeQ6uOM3bZiU21IsqCV2gFylZ7xwcu-jEu2Ja5ZKkKtbWOjFzohrCjqNky8lJQRqWdGGerFHbYs08Y_RVXjXKlKZJUKUOh3wD1ql8DhfZ8rr0nXJlXx7ai-MaW0KOxPFyfzc6gj-zBlFake1h74W2g%3D%3D)

Peter throughout American history, our nation's flag has stood tall.

**I hope you 'll take this opportunity to receive your American flag and fly it proudly, today and every day as a symbol AND promise of freedom.**

Thank you for standing with America's heroes -- past, present, and future.

clean_body: A powerful symbol. A historic moment. A mission worth joining.| | | Peter, For more than two decades, American Veterans Center has been dedicated to honoring the sacrifice and preserving the legacy of our nation's heroes. Each year, we host the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. , bringing Americans together in a powerful display of remembrance and patriotism. We also proudly produce " American Valor: A Salute to Our Heroes," a nationally televised tribute to the men and women who have served across generations. But our work goes far beyond public events. At the heart of our mission is a commitment to preserve the stories of veterans from every era of service -- from the final voices of World War II, to those who served in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 generation. Through these efforts, we ensure that future generations understand the cost of freedom and the courage it has taken to defend it. This year, American Veterans Center is marking historic military milestones: the 250th anniversaries of the U.S. Army, Navy, and Marine Corps...the 80th anniversary of the victories that won World War II...and key events in the Korean War 75 years ago, and the Vietnam War which ended 50 years ago this year. We may never have another opportunity like this to recognize their sacrifice on a national scale. This is one of our most ambitious efforts ever, Peter -- a national salute to those who fought to make and keep America free. Together, we 're capturing the stories of WWII veterans before it's too late...and preserving seldom-heard accounts from those who fought in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 wars. And with your gift today, I'll send you a free, full-size American flag as my thank you. To some, it may look like just a flag. But to those who served, it 's a promise kept. It flew over the beaches of Normandy. It sailed with the Pacific fleet. It was stitched into the sleeves of every soldier who never made it home. This flag reminds us of all they sacrificed, and what we 're now called to defend. Let it fly proudly as a reminder that freedom isn't free -- and that this generation will not forget the sacrifices of the last. Peter throughout American history, our nation's flag has stood tall. I hope you 'll take this opportunity to receive your American flag and fly it proudly, today and every day as a symbol AND promise of freedom. Thank you for standing with America's heroes -- past, present, and future. Sincerely, Tim Holbert President American Veterans Center The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. (C) American Veterans Center 1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201
