---
| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.
---

These are facts the authorities are desperate to hide.| | | | November 12 | | | | | The truth they want to hide could destroy lives. | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. body:
[1]Powered By People

After big wins in Virginia, New Jersey, and beyond last week, we took a
hard look at the data. Here’s what we know: we can flip seats in Texas
too.

Key districts across the state are tightening fast. Just a few thousand
votes separated Democrats and Republicans last cycle—and that’s exactly
where our work makes the biggest difference. When we organize early,
register new voters, hold community events, and knock on doors year-round,
we can close that gap and move seats from red to blue.

Here are just 5 of the tightest races from the last cycle:

📍Dallas–Fort Worth Area

* District 112: Angie Chen Button (R) 53.9% | Averie Bishop (D) 46.1%

📍San Antonio Area

* District 121: Marc LaHood (R) 52.5% | Laurel Jordan Swift (D) 47.5%

* District 118: John Lujan (R) 51.7% | Kristian Carranza (D) 48.3%

📍The Rio Grande Valley

* District 37: Janie Lopez (R) 55% | Jonathan Gracia (D) 45%

📍Corpus Christi

* District 34: Denise Villalobos (R) 55.4% | Solomon Ortiz (D) 44.6%

These districts and others like it aren’t out of reach—they’re within
striking distance. But to flip them, we have to organize earlier and
double down now, not in a year.

That’s where you come in. When you adopt a district, you’re joining the
fight right where it matters most. Your support helps us hire organizers,
host town halls, register voters, and train volunteers to reach people
where they are. This is the kind of grassroots infrastructure that flips
seats and builds lasting power in every community. And it’s the kind of
work that can’t wait.

districts? Your support helps us scale our organizing efforts so more
Texans are heard, more voters are engaged, and more seats flip from red to
blue.

We’re growing because the map demands it—and because Texans everywhere are

subject: Delco's third straight tax hike hits homeowners hard
body:
News + editorial to start your week | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | |
---
| | | Good morning, and welcome to _Broad + Liberty_ 's Weekly Reads.

We've moved to Wednesdays to accommodate _Fideri News's_ new flagship weekly newsletter, the Sunday Bulletin, which should hit your mailboxes in our old time slot on Sunday mornings.

And as always, to stay up to date on late-breaking stories and analysis, [subscribe to our daily email list](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=05bb3c4418&e=b7b3eddce2) and get the best of _Broad + Liberty_ delivered straight to your inbox.
---
|
---
|
### **1. [19%, 24%, 5% — Delaware County’s tax-increase trifecta hits average homeowner for extra $409 annually](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=ee72658d1c&e=b7b3eddce2)**
_By Todd Shepherd_
The first [draft](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=e3e0648477&e=b7b3eddce2) of Delaware County’s 2026 budget proposes a nineteen percent tax increase, a move coming just one year after the county levied a 24 percent tax increase in property taxes for the current year, and a five percent hike the year before that in 2024. Using the median home value of $255,000 as an example, that homeowner would have had a tax bill of $763 in 2023. If the nineteen percent increase is approved, and when also calculating the increases from the last two years, the same homeowner would have a tax bill next year of $1,172 — a total increase of 53 percent requiring an extra $409 per year. **Why It Matters**. The county posted the executive director’s [proposed budget](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a152f8476c&e=b7b3eddce2) online Friday, days after Democrats, who control all five seats on the council, won resoundingly at the polls. Current council member Richard Womack was re-elected, and the county’s controller, Joanne Phillips, was promoted to the council seat being vacated by Kevin Madden, who is term-limited.

Republicans made the 24 percent tax increase for 2025 a central plank of the campaign. Council members have complained that years of underfunding by previous Republican-controlled councils left the county in a precarious position financially. Yet it’s also clear from the current budget that the top two priorities of the Democrats who swept to power in the November 2019 elections have added to the budget’s bottom line.

[Continue Reading](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a823ba9aa5&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
### **2. [Renamed, rebranded, and reduced — What happened to the Philadelphia Museum of Art?](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c57f705fae&e=b7b3eddce2)**
_By Thom Nickels_ The Philadelphia Museum of Art, now the Art Museum of Philadelphia, has been in decline for a number of years. Director Timothy Rub attempted to hold the line and keep up appearances for a good while. To a large extent, he succeeded. Exhibition openings (for the press) were well-organized and still special events with speeches and tables of breakfast delicacies for journalists on assignment. The old guard at the museum took the press seriously, a tradition going back to Anne d’Harnoncourt’s tenure when press events were actual sit-down diners (white table cloths and wine), where a Philadelphia journalist might find him or herself seated beside someone from the _Wall Street Journal._ Enter the new CEO, Sasha Suda. She was from Canada. The sound of the name caused me to pause. Call it a vibe. I’m not xenophobic but I felt what I felt. The museum continued to have press exhibition openings, and I continued to attend but the new director always managed to be absent, or she managed to speak to journalists at the very beginning of the event before I got there and then retreated to her office. It was a full year or more before I actually saw her. About this time, museum press events were slowly changing. They seemed a little hurried and more abbreviated now: I could see that old guard PMA formality was slowly being eroded. The new boss liked things simple. **[Continue Reading](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=33685e8320&e=b7b3eddce2)**
---
|
---
|
# **3\. Lightning Round**
* **[A deep dive into Krasner’s third-term win](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=b429dd2942&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Seth Higgins: Reflections from a Pennsylvania veteran](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=de175552f4&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Gerard St. John: Joe Rosenthal’s Iwo Jima](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=33fa99c1b1&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Frank Speidel: Why they fought](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=8ed3b7fd73&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Christine Flowers: It’s hard to stomach what the residents of NYC just did](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=863022c81a&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Kyle Sammin: Eating the seed corn](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=2f3d1bf6bc&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Operation Clean Sweep scores a big hit on Philly-area drug trafficking](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c090865c9b&e=b7b3eddce2)**

---
|
---
|
# **4\. What we're reading**
The Supreme Court's October term will have many high-profile cases, but the most momentous of them is the recently argued matter of _Learning Resources, Inc. v. Trump_ , which challenges the president's claim that he can set tariff rates without any input from Congress. Over at the _Free Press,_ Harvard's Professor Jed Rubenfeld suggests that despite his other successes, the [outlook is not great for the president's arguments](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=3ac1f10b41&e=b7b3eddce2).

One big problem Rubenfeld notes: "As authority for the challenged tariffs, Trump has relied solely on the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA). But the word tariff never appears in that statute." There are other problems, as well. Tariffs could be Trump's signature achievement in his second term, but depending how the court rules, it might require doing it the old fashioned way: by working with Congress.

name: Amanda K. Edwards
subject: We can’t play politics with people’s lives
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Amanda Edwards for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ake_engen?refcode=11%2F12_e [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ake_engen?refcode=11%2F12_e]
Friend,
The government shutdown isn’t just politics, it’s people’s lives on the line.
Families are missing paychecks. SNAP benefits are running out. And food lines are growing longer by the day. While politicians argue in Washington, families in our community are wondering how they’ll put food on the table.
And now, to make matters worse, the deal the Senate has advanced fails to address a major crisis on the horizon: the expiration of Affordable Care Act (ACA) subsidies will more than double monthly premiums for millions of Americans.
I’m running because our community deserves leaders who will put people before politics and protect access to affordable health care. But I can’t do this alone. I need your help to keep fighting for those being left behind. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ake_engen?refcode=11%2F10_e&refcode2=f1c61667-f1bf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c]
Can you chip in today to help us stand up for working families and defend affordable health care? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ake_engen?refcode=11%2F10_e&refcode2=f1c61667-f1bf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c]
We need Democrats in Congress who will protect affordable healthcare.

name: Alliance Defending Freedom
subject: BREAKING: Babylon Bee WINS free speech case
body:
We just received BIG news—your free speech is SAFE against two draconian censorship laws out of California!

| | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/u/SZgqbRzRQErNd2drwesI7Jbsmcv_Qqg4M2XQjt0R7Xm6r0H-poRe-SM-wb2moBDUkCohw_uY65krNO59IGeuvbjueLH13Zr0k9gtjtKxEXC1rAeNC1M00IAUrXuP1pv_H4Xc_VBVZhupq58ifrB0iYy63M6GjxoWzsHUzSLNDBTdxokYend8bJjE/iKufmQXkPOrDcwXZjeZKm1qVoPBrFpkE/14)_

**[Free to Meme—Court Says California Can’t Censor Political Jokes](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/n5IDEz19-2DIS2HvVWvkBrqUrfiJOMt9e_voce96K1kisuLzU9IrnryA43F0AvOeCZiipzYl-G40Zzd1TyLOYngG8MsxkRYojQWsoD748uhqAXffNEveblgRO-rqJ277BsSpYSBR5jPFg1OzAKRlqKDte5YSg9cbCt4luKjATNlesXRLv9gxiEOi200guMS3dUIyvuXkNUIVBc0VnOw9WV3FUYqy1WSMjIpArt6mf91Ao-WwZJQQwIJob8TRDtCmHu5WyFK0JHukPOtxsD2nKhRW0oWbBqD8kbNNGWmhzgcx_mrm8-uKMIEbgnlW700i4aVcri_1TNLq6jr04VpKwXy6ikq6zviePuZB1QHC92athwXk-o22IQDtcg4AtHYl6fOnRkM0pqHdE1b6ZlqHklp1HdoDbNsFDTvFibDvCwZPwlhuR4CuIG2xo9ofYoMwel67OkDAorwiQeDgvzGdO7KcTKyHwdXCLihFDj7EAM9kS5qWuT0Y6RNtfekkiIZ1WA1e4ZGcIbPcjbAaUfMdH3zWuQAdq2i_-3T2Y71EYSspuYYei7lwlgfKBNfWu9aH0ZzE8uPJf03_OpDOgbctYAKR0uz4EPrmmRYuH5ZAUQds8JmXWAw8Ccuw4bPZFSFKOaFMJxXcI9ZnoHozpHMgPHpLg_AEq_fM_iGYctNatvo9fatQv4VaXBXjw5-Wvs7puefMNjpqzVoPnDSqA7W_/GM__zKkFB_vinh7C-Lv5HnkSnfm42KYA/14)** Hi Fellow American,

We just received BIG news--your free speech is SAFE against two draconian censorship laws out of California!

The laws censor political speech and even punish people for posting a simple, funny meme and then require online platforms to censor that speech--if the government deems the content "materially deceptive." So, The Babylon Bee, along with a California lawyer and video-sharing platform Rumble, challenged the laws for violating their free speech rights--**and today, a court agreed with them.**

This is an incredible win for free speech. And it's because friends like YOU stood by The Babylon Bee and helped them fight this egregious government overreach.

But it's not the end of the story. The Babylon Bee's battle to protect humor and free speech isn't over.

Their rights--and yours--are still under threat in Hawaii, thanks to a law that goes even further than California's.

Hawaii's law threatens fines, damages, and even _jail time_ if a person posts a political meme that government officials don't like. It's all part of a growing trend of censorship. Across the country and the globe, government officials and private actors alike are cracking down on speech in cases like this.

With your help, The Babylon Bee is fighting Hawaii's law--no one should face jail time because a politician doesn't get your joke.

This victory in California is promising, but your help is needed to keep up the fight for free speech. clean_body: We just received BIG news—your free speech is SAFE against two draconian censorship laws out of California! | | Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this | | | Hi Fellow American, We just received BIG news--your free speech is SAFE against two draconian censorship laws out of California! The laws censor political speech and even punish people for posting a simple, funny meme and then require online platforms to censor that speech--if the government deems the content "materially deceptive." So, The Babylon Bee, along with a California lawyer and video-sharing platform Rumble, challenged the laws for violating their free speech rights--and today, a court agreed with them. This is an incredible win for free speech. And it's because friends like YOU stood by The Babylon Bee and helped them fight this egregious government overreach. But it's not the end of the story. The Babylon Bee's battle to protect humor and free speech isn't over. Their rights--and yours--are still under threat in Hawaii, thanks to a law that goes even further than California's. Hawaii's law threatens fines, damages, and even jail time if a person posts a political meme that government officials don't like. It's all part of a growing trend of censorship. Across the country and the globe, government officials and private actors alike are cracking down on speech in cases like this. With your help, The Babylon Bee is fighting Hawaii's law--no one should face jail time because a politician doesn't get your joke. This victory in California is promising, but your help is needed to keep up the fight for free speech. For Freedom, Alliance Defending Freedom Alliance Defending Freedom 15100 N. 90th Street, Scottsdale, Arizona 85260

name: Adelita Grijalva
subject: Speaker Johnson’s snarky comment about me
body:
I know you’re likely getting a ton of emails from candidates and causes right now, Peter.
But I have to ask: Is there anything I can say to convince you to chip in $25 between Real Texas PAC and my Senate campaign in Ohio? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]
What if I told you that right after I joined this race, experts at The Cook Political Report increased our odds of winning?
What if I told you that CNN and The New York Times report that Ohio is “in play” – but that corporate special interests are gearing up to spend millions against me?
What if I told you that misleading attack ads have already hit the airwaves – and that whether we’re able to counter these smears and reach voters with the truth depends on people like you?
This grassroots, small-dollar donor movement is our best chance to flip Ohio’s Senate seat. But we’re facing an uphill battle, and I need folks like you by my side to win.
So please, split $25 or more between Real Texas PAC and me to power our campaign forward, win in Ohio, and keep up the fight for working people. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]

name: Dr. Kim Schrier
subject: When will Republicans start working?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
Mike Johnson carries on the the Republican tradition of attacking the Affordable Care Act but offering no alternative plan [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119151274/582112425/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODljMjk3Zi1mM2JmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7kCMg5suH1bMAEz5egXLdygkwupg6-_67INQHcHPvVU=&emci=7797b0ca-ddbf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=e89c297f-f3bf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=15820338)
---
Peter,

Mike Johnson has renewed the Republican attacks on the Affordable Care Act, saying he won't commit to holding a vote on whether to continue subsidies that help lower health insurance costs for Americans.

The shutdown may be coming to an end, but there’s still no guarantee that Republicans will negotiate in good faith to help people afford health insurance. They seem inexplicably committed to dismantling the ACA despite its overwhelming popularity with their own constituents.

This is after they voted on the Big, Ugly Bill that kicks millions off their health insurance, slashes Medicaid, and threatens the survival of rural hospitals and hospitals that serve a high number of Medicare and Medicaid patients.

We're already starting to see the effects of this Republican agenda–hospitals are closing, key medical staff are losing their jobs, and Americans are weighing whether they can afford health insurance or whether they should roll the dice and hope no one in their family gets sick or injured this year. It’s time for Members of Congress to stand up for constituents, not billionaires.

[**To protect and improve our healthcare, we need to take back the House majority next year. Before our mid-month deadline, will you chip in $10 or whatever you can to help me protect my seat so I can continue fighting for affordable, accessible healthcare in Congress?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119151275/582112429/1491818712?ms=em25_251112_mm1_fndd_gq_act___DRKS_ettx&refcode=em25_251112_mm1_fndd_gq_act___DRKS_ettx&source=em25_251112_mm1_fndd_gq_act___DRKS_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251112_mm1_fndd_gq_act___DRKS_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=drcLE4U71hBoF36FUywYRJ/kkden9kCWf99o8ybQBX6GPYsNZcbJ/sRpsdpWZ0EvcX+8YIUixV5QYvAxzXcLKkjltKzWXpcRrZSwWv7kpIbbdutH5U8iT7PnpPSNLKYbJoPnoP8ilJ24xvat+bHnJAPhXYnm02ICLlbk346OV1Q=&refcode2=e89c297f-f3bf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODljMjk3Zi1mM2JmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7kCMg5suH1bMAEz5egXLdygkwupg6-_67INQHcHPvVU=)

_This fight is personal to me, Peter._

As a pediatrician and person with Type 1 diabetes, I understand how life-changing the Affordable Care Act is, both for my patients and other people like me with pre-existing conditions. Tens of millions of people across this country who couldn’t buy insurance on their own before finally have the sense of security that comes with affordable access to insurance and medical care.

Before Trump first tried to repeal the ACA, I had never even considered running for office. But when Congressional Republicans tried to overturn the Affordable Care Act in 2017, I joined with three other doctors from my clinic and met with our former representative’s staff to explain all of the ways the ACA protects people in this district, and why it was so critical. Two days later, our congressman voted to overturn the ACA.

That was the final straw for me, and I decided to put my practice on hold to run against the person who’d just tried to take healthcare away from millions of Americans. I loved working with kids and parents, but I knew the stakes for their families, too.

Since the day I was sworn into Congress, I’ve worked to protect and improve the ACA, lower the cost of prescription drugs, and expand access to healthcare. But Republicans are so fixated on killing the Affordable Care Act that they were willing to withhold SNAP funding to get their way. It is disgusting.

I will always fight to ensure the people of WA-08, and Americans across the country, have access to the care they need, when they need it. But if we want to make more progress to improve our healthcare system, then we need to flip the House blue.

[**Before our mid-month deadline, will you please chip in to help defend this seat and retake the House majority in 2026?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119151276/582112437/1491818712?ms=em25_251112_mm1_fndd_gq_act___DRKS_ettx&refcode=em25_251112_mm1_fndd_gq_act___DRKS_ettx&source=em25_251112_mm1_fndd_gq_act___DRKS_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251112_mm1_fndd_gq_act___DRKS_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=drcLE4U71hBoF36FUywYRJ/kkden9kCWf99o8ybQBX6GPYsNZcbJ/sRpsdpWZ0EvcX+8YIUixV5QYvAxzXcLKkjltKzWXpcRrZSwWv7kpIbbdutH5U8iT7PnpPSNLKYbJoPnoP8ilJ24xvat+bHnJAPhXYnm02ICLlbk346OV1Q=&refcode2=e89c297f-f3bf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODljMjk3Zi1mM2JmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7kCMg5suH1bMAEz5egXLdygkwupg6-_67INQHcHPvVU=) clean_body: Mike Johnson carries on the the Republican tradition of attacking the Affordable Care Act but offering no alternative plan Peter, Mike Johnson has renewed the Republican attacks on the Affordable Care Act, saying he won't commit to holding a vote on whether to continue subsidies that help lower health insurance costs for Americans. The shutdown may be coming to an end, but there’s still no guarantee that Republicans will negotiate in good faith to help people afford health insurance. They seem inexplicably committed to dismantling the ACA despite its overwhelming popularity with their own constituents. This is after they voted on the Big, Ugly Bill that kicks millions off their health insurance, slashes Medicaid, and threatens the survival of rural hospitals and hospitals that serve a high number of Medicare and Medicaid patients. We're already starting to see the effects of this Republican agenda–hospitals are closing, key medical staff are losing their jobs, and Americans are weighing whether they can afford health insurance or whether they should roll the dice and hope no one in their family gets sick or injured this year. It’s time for Members of Congress to stand up for constituents, not billionaires. = This fight is personal to me, Peter. As a pediatrician and person with Type 1 diabetes, I understand how life-changing the Affordable Care Act is, both for my patients and other people like me with pre-existing conditions. Tens of millions of people across this country who couldn’t buy insurance on their own before finally have the sense of security that comes with affordable access to insurance and medical care. Before Trump first tried to repeal the ACA, I had never even considered running for office. But when Congressional Republicans tried to overturn the Affordable Care Act in 2017, I joined with three other doctors from my clinic and met with our former representative’s staff to explain all of the ways the ACA protects people in this district, and why it was so critical. Two days later, our congressman voted to overturn the ACA. That was the final straw for me, and I decided to put my practice on hold to run against the person who’d just tried to take healthcare away from millions of Americans. I loved working with kids and parents, but I knew the stakes for their families, too. Since the day I was sworn into Congress, I’ve worked to protect and improve the ACA, lower the cost of prescription drugs, and expand access to healthcare. But Republicans are so fixated on killing the Affordable Care Act that they were willing to withhold SNAP funding to get their way. It is disgusting. I will always fight to ensure the people of WA-08, and Americans across the country, have access to the care they need, when they need it. But if we want to make more progress to improve our healthcare system, then we need to flip the House blue. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you, Kim Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. Chip in now to help protect Kim's seat. = Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. name: Shawn Harris
subject: Marjorie Taylor Greene can be beaten

body:
ICE has said they are not taking visits from Members of Congress. I went anyway. |
---
Peter, Last week, I returned to ICE’s Eloy Detention Center here in Arizona for my third visit this year. Weeks into the longest shutdown in American history, ICE has said they are not taking visits from Members of Congress. Peter, oversight of these taxpayer-funded centers is literally my job — **so I went anyway.** The conditions at Eloy are horrific. Dozens of people who came to this country seeking asylum are being detained. They lack access to quality water and face abuse and racist treatment. One of my constituents, who goes by Yari, has been detained since February. Yari has leukemia, but ICE refused to allow her to see an oncologist until my office and the public pressured them to do so. Now, ICE is refusing to turn over her medical results, delaying the treatment she urgently needs. When I went back to Eloy to demand ICE give over her results, I heard excuse after excuse. It’s inhumane, and frankly, it’s bullshit. These are just some of the horrific stories of neglect and abuse in ICE detention centers in Arizona and across the country. **When human rights violations take place in our own country, it’s incumbent on all of us to stand up and speak out.** [I won’t back down from calling out this administration's authoritarianism. Will you chip in now to stand with me as I push back?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119151538/582113266/470246576?refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251112_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDFkYjA0NS1mNGJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LT1MsfMjOG1_UlHqErCseZuQMYfPonD6x2-jHL2jZnA=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119151541/582113268/470246576?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251112_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDFkYjA0NS1mNGJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LT1MsfMjOG1_UlHqErCseZuQMYfPonD6x2-jHL2jZnA=)

Peter, Last Tuesday’s elections delivered incredible wins all across the country. **Voters showed up to the polls to reject extremism and leadership that puts corporate profits over people.** Here in Georgia, we broke Republicans’ years of unanimous control of the Public Service Commission by electing Democrats Peter Hubbard and Dr. Alicia Johnson. And it wasn’t just Georgia — bellwether states like Virginia made it clear that **Americans are ready for Democratic leadership.** These victories proved something important: when we focus our efforts, organize strategically, and engage voters early on — we win. We’re taking these lessons and the momentum we’ve built into the 2026 elections. We’ve mapped our path to victory and we’re going all in. **With less than a year to go, we’ve set an ambitious goal of $50,000 by this Saturday to supercharge our organizing.** [Peter, will you chip in $10 now to help us hit our target and carry this momentum through 2026?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119151813/582114205/-2004472022?amount=10&source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_111225_MM1_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMDk5ODBlNC1mNGJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OHVWlq5dUutS27E5DEvVBJ0pSO3DpTCdq109VNlZ_-A=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

name: Dan Goldman
subject: We must restore trust in the Supreme Court.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York**
body:
Convicted felon and current president, Donald Trump, appealed his own conviction. |
#
---
Peter, **Convicted felon and current president Donald Trump just appealed his own criminal case to the same Supreme Court he stacked and that gave him near-absolute immunity last year.** Trump’s 34 felony convictions last summer in New York State court were affirmed by New York courts. But now, Trump is going back to his United States Supreme Court that effectively ruled that he is above the law. **Such a ruling upends a core tenet of American democracy — that the law applies equally to everyone, even the President.** And now, Trump is using that very ruling to settle his own legal matters and establish himself as a king who is bound by no law. Our Supreme Court should serve as the final interpreter of the Constitution. Yet they’ve used their conservative majority to bend to Trump’s will — allowing him to decimate our checks and balances and override our country’s democracy. **It’s never been clearer:_We must reform SCOTUS._** **[I’m taking a stand for the rule of law at every opportunity. Will you join me and sign on in support of my fight for SCOTUS reform today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119151913/582114314/150841983?refcode=AU_DSG_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20251112_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MDgzODRkMS1mNGJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zzHvc8sq_6VDFkXt8o4iBVOkIEhi67PIZsT3kq9n4kk=&emci=cc02310c-bbbd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=608384d1-f4bf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=13556823)** | [SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119151914/582114315/150841983?refcode=AU_DSG_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20251112_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MDgzODRkMS1mNGJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zzHvc8sq_6VDFkXt8o4iBVOkIEhi67PIZsT3kq9n4kk=&emci=cc02310c-bbbd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=608384d1-f4bf-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=13556823)

name: Team Pou
subject: We won’t repeat last year’s mistakes
body:
Trump won this district. The GOP is coming again.

| |
---
Hi friend, Last year, no one saw it coming. Republicans made huge gains. They made real inroads fueled by false promises, fear campaigns, and a flood of attack ads. Trump even won Nellie’s district. And now national Republicans are pouring millions back to do it again. They’re not subtle. They want to buy this seat, lie to our voters, and erase the progress we’ve made. But this year’s elections proved something else: When we invest in organizing, when we knock doors, when we talk to voters, and tell them our plan and vision... **we win.** We know what works. We just need the resources to keep doing it. **[That’s why today we’re announcing our November mid-month fundraising goal. We need to raise $2,000 over the next few days. ### About Nellie Pou

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: Can you help feed another family?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
We just need another $2,600 to hit our goal and provide 3,600 meals to families in need.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20251112-5620-footer&t=1&akid=5620%2E420207%2EOX6Gy4)
Hi Peter. Pramila here with a quick but important update on our partnership with White Center Food Bank, where I had the chance to volunteer this week and see firsthand the impact your support is making.
[](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251112-whitecenter.n1h3wo&refcode=em20251112-5620&t=2&akid=5620%2E420207%2EOX6Gy4)
Just days ago, we surpassed our original goal and set a new one — to raise $9,000 and provide 3,600 meals for families in need before Thanksgiving. And as always, this team showed up.
**We’ve now raised more than $6,400 for the White Center Food Bank, enough to provide over 2,500 meals to families in need.**
But our work isn’t done yet. Food banks here in Seattle and across the country are still overwhelmed. And even if the shutdown ends tonight, Republicans have slashed long-term SNAP funding. The need won’t disappear — and neither can we.
**That’s why we need one last push from this team to raise the final $2,600 needed to hit our $9,000 goal and provide 3,600 meals before Thanksgiving.**
[**Will you chip in right now to help us get there? Every dollar you donate to this email will be used to fund meals at the White Center Food Bank here in Seattle.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251112-whitecenter.n1h3wo&refcode=em20251112-5620&amount=3&t=3&akid=5620%2E420207%2EOX6Gy4)
**[Contribute](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251112-whitecenter.n1h3wo&refcode=em20251112-5620&t=4&akid=5620%2E420207%2EOX6Gy4)**
This is what our movement is about: showing up with compassion and urgency when our government fails to. While Trump and MAGA Republicans continue to play games with people’s lives, **_you_** are helping deliver something real in the face of cruelty and abandonment — nourishment, dignity, and hope.
For families facing hunger, each meal represents one less thing to worry about, one more day with food on the table, and a little more security in a time of deep uncertainty. That’s the difference your support is making.
So thank you for being a part of this work and helping us meet this moment. Again and again, you remind me why I believe so deeply in people-powered change — and in the truth that compassion and community are stronger than cruelty and indifference.
In solidarity,
Pramila Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20251112-5620-footer&t=5&refcode2=5620_420207_OX6Gy4&akid=5620%2E420207%2EOX6Gy4)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.

# Blue States are hemorrhaging BILLIONS as residents flee
New York and New Jersey **[hemorrhaged nearly $690 billion](https://substack.com/redirect/74fcfc53-e015-448d-98b8-e313d29a35a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in resident income over the past decade as 2.1 million high-earners fled soaring taxes and urban decay for sun-soaked havens like Florida and Texas, according to a stark new Census Bureau analysis.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/74fcfc53-e015-448d-98b8-e313d29a35a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Cumulative Losses Mount** : From 2013 to 2022, New York shed $517.5 billion in incomes while New Jersey dropped $170.1 billion, ranking them among America's top four net losers alongside California and Illinois.
* **Population Surge Southward** : Florida scooped up $1 trillion in gains and 1.59 million new residents, with Texas close behind at $290 billion and 1.27 million, dwarfing other states' inflows.
* **Domestic Migration Drains** : Over 2011-2022, New York lost 1.76 million residents net, and New Jersey 350,000, fueling a historic wealth transfer that economist Steve Moore calls a "regional bloodletting."
* **Economic Power Shifts** : The exodus has turbocharged Southern economies while hollowing out Northeast powerhouses, highlighting how low-tax policies lure affluent workers and reshape national prosperity maps.

### **Coinbase DITCHES Delaware for business friendly Texas**
In another black eye for blue states **[Crypto giant Coinbase](https://substack.com/redirect/44907568-2182-43fd-8020-27ad3efdf5cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** announced Wednesday it has reincorporated in Texas, abandoning Delaware amid frustration with its unpredictable courts, following Elon Musk's lead with Tesla and SpaceX. The shift aims to shield executives from shareholder lawsuits in a more business-friendly legal landscape.
* **Delaware Courts Unpredictable Rulings** : Coinbase cites recent Chancery Court decisions, including Tesla's $56 billion Musk pay package rescission, as eroding the state's once-reliable consistency for corporate governance.
* **Texas Shields Corporate Insiders** : A new Texas law empowers firms to restrict shareholder suits against executives for fiduciary breaches, providing stronger protections than Delaware's framework.
* **Echoing Musk 's Anti-Delaware Campaign**: Elon Musk urged companies to flee Delaware in a 2024 X post. Coinbase's exit now joins Dropbox, TripAdvisor, and investor Andreessen Horowitz in the exodus from the blue state.

Citadel CEO Ken Griffin confirms a brutal truth: "We're definitely on a sugar high in the US economy right now," warning that we are in a "very pro-inflationary environment."
In 2008, the S&P 500 plummeted more than 38%, wiping out trillions in retirement savings overnight.[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/73ed6da9-c3a6-40fc-b75d-6a36349a191a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[For those nearing retirement, the losses were catastrophic but they pale in comparison to what's on the horizon.](https://substack.com/redirect/73ed6da9-c3a6-40fc-b75d-6a36349a191a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
This isn't speculation. This view is shared by some of the world's most wealthy and influential.
Look at the latest news from Goldman Sachs and JP Morgan. With the US Dollar's devaluation and "smart money" demand soaring, their 2026 gold price forecast has surged to $4,500-$4,900 per ounce. The "smart money" isn't waiting for the inevitable collapse.

# Trump's Wall Street Titan dinner is tonight
President Donald Trump will host a high-stakes White House dinner Wednesday night with JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon and Nasdaq's top executive, forging closer ties to corporate titans amid **[drives to bolster U.S. capital markets](https://substack.com/redirect/966253e3-d1e3-48ae-b3ee-bdd02f2950f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and secure vital supply chains for national defense.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/966253e3-d1e3-48ae-b3ee-bdd02f2950f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Private Guest List Confirmed** : Administration officials have verified attendance for some key finance leaders, including Dimon, though the full roster remains undisclosed despite media inquiries.
* **JPMorgan 's Massive Pledge**: JPM has already announced a $1.5 trillion, 10-year investment push into manufacturing, defense, energy, and tech, with $10 billion earmarked for equity in security-critical firms.
* **Strategic Economic Focus** : Discussions are expected to target reshoring industries, expanding domestic production, and channeling private funds into high-tech and energy infrastructure for growth.
* **Series of Elite Engagements** : The vent extends Trump's pattern of discreet business summits, emphasizing private-sector roles in rebuilding American economic resilience.

* * *
* * *
# AMD surges on profit forecasts as CEO claims AI is "The right gamble"
Advanced Micro Devices shares **[skyrocketed 10% Wednesday](https://substack.com/redirect/78466cfd-f06e-4669-a28c-dc4325172d94?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after CEO Lisa Su projected a 60% surge in data center revenue over three to five years, fueled by AI expansion and deals with OpenAI and Oracle, positioning the chipmaker to challenge Nvidia's dominance.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/78466cfd-f06e-4669-a28c-dc4325172d94?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Data Center Dominance Projected** : Su dismissed fears about AI spending saying that _**" It's the right gamble"**_ expects $16 billion in 2025 data center sales to balloon 60% amid a $1 trillion AI market opportunity, including GPUs, CPUs, and networking gear.

[](https://substack.com/redirect/8874c613-dad6-46f0-b0f2-3670caaa9a58?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Overall Revenue Acceleration** : Forecasts project 35% company-wide growth from $34 billion base in 2025, with data centers comprising the bulk, plus 10% uptick in client PC and gaming chips.
* **Margin Expansion Ahead** : AMD is targeting 55-58% gross margins and over 35% operating margins in 3-5 years, bolstering profitability as AI investments pay off.
* **Strategic Partnerships Sealed** : The deals that AMD has locked in includes a 6-gigawatt OpenAI pact and a 50,000-chip Oracle supply deal starting in 2026. AMD is eyeing gigawatt-scale wins with MI450 and MI500 processors across multiple "hyperscalers" thanks to it's chip design.

* * *
* * *
# Coreweave CEO dodges questions over data center delays tied to failed acquisition
In a CNBC interview Tuesday, CoreWeave's CEO Mike Intrator **[dodged questions](https://substack.com/redirect/391e58cc-f932-45e3-8c4a-0f98a6838e4a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** on whether the failed attempt to acquire Core Scientific triggered crippling delays at multiple AI data centers, sending shares of both companies plunging--CoreWeave down 16% and Core Scientific 10%--as revised 2025 revenue forecasts fell short of Wall Street hopes.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/6e26d6cc-8ebf-440b-9ae3-4304a082d570?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Q3 Revenue Skyrockets Dramatically** : CoreWeave posted $1.36 billion in third-quarter sales, a 134% surge from last year, fueled by booming AI infrastructure demand despite isolated setbacks.
* **Failed Acquisition Fuels Suspicion** : The earlier $9 billion bid to buy Core Scientific collapsed in a shareholder vote, leaving lingering ties that now spotlight the bitcoin miner's role in multi-state site delays.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/65acc109-6ae0-400f-b91b-6dad08a435f2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Mega-Deals Anchor Backlog Strength** : Coreweave's $14.2 billion Meta pact and $22.4 billion OpenAI expansion remain untouched by hiccups, bolstering a robust pipeline across 41 global data centers.
* **Intense Remediation Hopes To Accelerates Progress** : CoreWeave dispatched on-site teams for daily collaboration with contractors, vowing swift facility activations without eroding contract values or long-term growth trajectory.

* * *
* * *

name: Jim MacEachern
subject: D.C. money is coming for New Hampshire
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee, Chairman Jim MacEachern
body:
Connecticut Senator Chris Murphy is in New Hampshire, and he’s not here to admire the view.

| | | | | [|
---](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=8cb7ac9076&e=f3aa6419cd)
| | | | | |

Friend,Connecticut Senator Chris Murphy is in New Hampshire, and he’s not here to admire the view.**Murphy and the Washington Democrats see New Hampshire as their next target. He’s here to raise money, funnel D.C. dollars into our elections, and push his far-left agenda that New Hampshire voters have already rejected.**
---
| [FIGHT BACK >>>](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=60de8a0fab&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| |

This is the same senator who called for riots to defend criminal illegal aliens and voted more than a dozen times to keep the government shut down while families went hungry. Now he wants to buy influence and help elect Democrats who’ll turn our state into the next Massachusetts or Connecticut._We can’t let that happen._**Every dollar he raises for Democrats will go toward flipping our legislature, our cities, and our state — and undoing everything Republicans have built.** That’s why we need your help [**right now.**](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=9bf191aeb2&e=f3aa6419cd)Your contribution ensures Republicans have the resources to fight back, defend our majority, and keep New Hampshire safe, prosperous, and free.[**Chip in $25, $50, or $100 HERE today**](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=3d4bc66dff&e=f3aa6419cd) to stop Washington Democrats from buying our elections and remind them: New Hampshire isn’t for sale.
---
| [DONATE NOW](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=c98adfa6b6&e=f3aa6419cd)

For New Hampshire,Chairman Jim MacEachern

**But these big losses mean Republicans are turning their attention to the midterms – and they will spend whatever it takes to hold onto power, no matter how unpopular their agenda is with voters.**

And the President and his allies know as well as we do that there’s no Senate majority for Democrats without Virginia.

clean_body: let’s talk about it. | | | = Derek, Last week, Americans spoke: They do not like Donald Trump’s policies that are skyrocketing prices and making health care less accessible. And they are fed up with the administration’s chaos, corruption, and incompetence. Virginians and folks in states across the nation took to the polls to deliver Democrats big wins. Here in the Commonwealth, we flipped the Governorship and expanded our razor-thin majority in the House of Delegates by 13 seats. But these big losses mean Republicans are turning their attention to the midterms – and they will spend whatever it takes to hold onto power, no matter how unpopular their agenda is with voters. And the President and his allies know as well as we do that there’s no Senate majority for Democrats without Virginia. So we need to be ready for anything. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately: = = = = = = Thank you, Team Warner = * | | Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | Click here to . | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

name: Sarah McBride
subject: The only thing that Congressional Republicans are more loyal to than Donald Trump is cutting healthcare for the American people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
This week, the Republican-controlled Senate passed a budget that fails to address the health care crisis facing Americans.
#
Hi, Sarah McBride here.
This week, the Republican-controlled Senate passed a budget that fails to address the healthcare crisis facing Americans.
**The only thing that Congressional Republicans are more loyal to than Donald Trump is cutting healthcare for the American people.**
Delawareans deserve so much better. We can reopen the government and tackle the healthcare crisis at the same time — but Republicans’ cruelty has been standing in our way for months.
**Today and every day, I will fight to defend your healthcare. It’s why I ran for office, and it’s why I’ll be voting no.**
[**Your support is what keeps me in this fight, so if it makes sense for you right now, will you please pitch in $10 or $20 to help me keep fighting?**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251112-1.BcuPIH&refcode=em20251112-2496&t=1&akid=2496%2E21716%2ErJm0_h)

Thank you,
Sarah

# Trump’s Reset Can Give Birth To
# America’s Greatest Era Yet
# A 90-Year cycle may end soon, creating real wealth for early adopters
In 1933, Executive Order 6102 forced everyday Americans to hand over their gold at a fixed rate. Everyday citizens lost a sizable amount of their hard earned wealth at the stroke of FDR’s pen. Now, 92 years later, [President Trump has focused his energy on making things right. ](https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=959e81ab988f5c3fd0d290dfbc3354f28b21f8125859c25b615fff51aa84abbd352d770ced752ca4e0bc2e9f1cb1779aca1db407c2a503c8baf7a5018e510131) His next move has the power to trigger a financial reset that could shift trillions of dollars into the hands of the people. A provision buried in the U.S. Code Title 31, Section 5117 allows the U.S. Treasury to revalue America’s gold reserves from an outdated $42 per ounce to today’s market price. That’s a 72x increase!
---
If activated, it could
* **Reinforce America’s financial dominance**
* Reignite trust in value-backed money, making the dollar valuable again
* **Spark a modern day gold rush once the public understand their choices**

---
And the best part... Over 60 Million Americans are eligible to become a first wave benefactor in Trump’s Gold Reset. However, only those who download a copy of our 2025 Wealth Protection Guide will know the simple steps needed to take part in this historic wealth reset. [Claim Your FREE Guide Now](https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=959e81ab988f5c3fd0d290dfbc3354f28b21f8125859c25b615fff51aa84abbd352d770ced752ca4e0bc2e9f1cb1779aca1db407c2a503c8baf7a5018e510131) and discover how to position yourself for this golden opportunity.

name: Jahana Hayes
subject: SNAP was the difference between putting my kids to bed hungry and not
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
| | Hi there — I was one of the millions of Americans who relied on SNAP. When I was a college student working two jobs, I qualified for the program. SNAP benefits were the difference between putting my kids to bed hungry and not. My colleagues on the other side of the aisle may not believe it, but I know that there are SNAP parents doing everything they can to provide for their families, and it still is not enough to make ends meet. Millions of families have a similar story — seniors, veterans, people who need a helping hand putting healthy food on the table. But instead of standing up for these families, Republicans are weaponizing and attacking this critical safety net to satisfy an agenda that is designed to benefit the wealthiest people in this country. **[No more. We need a House majority that will put people over politics and fight to ensure working families have what they need to thrive. Pitch in right now to join this fight and support our work.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119153202/582118812/-1546761245?refcode=AU_JH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251112_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNTdkNGJjMi1mOGJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QgQIKEOygYinKfB7MhE-pWlCTpHwQrI-Vq8lrOcVsu4=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [$10 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119153203/582118813/-1546761245?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_JH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251112_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNTdkNGJjMi1mOGJmLWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QgQIKEOygYinKfB7MhE-pWlCTpHwQrI-Vq8lrOcVsu4=)

clean_body: | | Hi there — I was one of the millions of Americans who relied on SNAP. When I was a college student working two jobs, I qualified for the program. SNAP benefits were the difference between putting my kids to bed hungry and not. My colleagues on the other side of the aisle may not believe it, but I know that there are SNAP parents doing everything they can to provide for their families, and it still is not enough to make ends meet. Millions of families have a similar story — seniors, veterans, people who need a helping hand putting healthy food on the table. But instead of standing up for these families, Republicans are weaponizing and attacking this critical safety net to satisfy an agenda that is designed to benefit the wealthiest people in this country. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | = | = | = I will always fight for our kids and for Connecticut families. name: Adam Schiff
subject: I voted no
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9177%2E5257348%2EwxMp-0)
Derek —
Sunday night, I voted no on the Republican funding bill to reopen the government without addressing the healthcare crisis.
The Republicans’ funding bill contained nothing to help people afford their health insurance.
The bill had nothing to bring down the costs of care or extend healthcare tax credits.
The bill had nothing in it to help make sure people with preexisting conditions can afford their health coverage.
The bill had none of that.
When this debate began, I said, I'm not supporting a government funding bill that doesn't have money for health care in it.
This one didn’t, **so I voted no.**
Derek, we owe our constituents better than this.
The American people deserve a healthcare system they can afford.
We need a system that allows people to join Medicare and receive high-quality care.
And we certainly can’t afford to make things worse.
Especially after the GOP already broke the system when they passed the Big Ugly Bill, which took billions and billions of dollars out of Medicaid to give tax cuts for wealthy people and corporations.
This is about fighting for lower healthcare costs — and only one party is taking up the mantle for the millions of Americans who are facing a cost-of-living crisis.
But this fight is also about our democracy. About whether we will stand up to a corrupt and lawless president hellbent on destroying our democracy and hurting citizens.
This is a president who went to the Supreme Court to stop food assistance from going to hungry families and children. I mean, who does that?
Derek, I will not stop working to lower costs, make health care more affordable and accessible, and to stand up to the bully in the White House.
Much more soon,
Adam

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Fighting for your healthcare (find resources to access lower cost care)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
I’m fighting for affordable healthcare, including extending Affordable Care Act subsidies. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWiN20yoLwgLqf5pUnc6j1npmbynu9UsoicRQRqUdwUSDNbTDp5ExBuUT5k0rrnj9TCqPxdcbrIjxWrXih71Eu8sNpsw65KQ_7DaIIfM4tDn2dO1HhFGqFnmLB4UmkMvlTYdTElVgyvS_jxxVIhLdrmDYX_8bhsPS_lk2vi7EpVfFEA5_s0k1swcs10sOiv614Cx5e9ZKo7NFNYKh49Y129yqOfac_pqhN_hy141uOoCKcyKHjg5NkVHxrckg6Bezh/4lj/8bl19ZHPQHWe2r0H6LMwUg/h0/h001.qbyWGqK4ny5oY9u0Z5dOGXLrsVZG_Dhbf81N3kHkRaw)
---
Friend, During the longest government shutdown in U.S. history, Republicans have cancelled votes and refused to show up to work, avoiding necessary negotiations to reopen the government. But this week, **the Senate passed a deal to end the government shutdown** , which is heading to the House of Representatives for a vote today. **Unfortunately, this bill fails to prevent health insurance costs from skyrocketing for over 20 million Americans** —including over half a million people in my state of Michigan. If Congress lets the Affordable Care Act subsidies expire at the end of 2025, millions of Americans will be unable to afford health insurance, and many will die. I’ve been hearing from constituents about how this disastrous move would impact them, including people who’ve said: _“We are living paycheck to paycheck... I cannot afford to pay any increase in premiums,”_ and _“I had to file bankruptcy due to medical bills. I just had two transplants and fear losing my healthcare.”_ **I will keep fighting to renew these essential subsidies, reverse MAGA’s healthcare cuts, and ensure universal healthcare for all Americans.** In the meantime, if you’re unable to afford the healthcare you need, **I want to share resources to help you find more affordable healthcare** , which you can also share with loved ones in need:
* Type in your zip code at [https://findhelp.org/](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.RNKlYhK-gnABEpCnH88uwMux-X6DDnpMotLPP-uxUWE8RwzRtB2df5zpZdQ3vMq-UWTdeYJ85vuSgVgIT7fcs-WqOe_dbZ7jIKWUUANYI7Er14TSVSgP2GIoNnIxixQ_ZWOy64MnHhzCbhm3PEPkKKmsooN8DJVpMyPUKZ2xakPzGVS01q1WWR-muQG5wcLTibR7L01iQO9bD56Z2I6JQtG0JOYWVJSrPFGH5TguOSoty9TQ_R28RC4WAFG4HIGSE9JChIcUnonYarEF7ASoPCq7lmJDimsbj1kAwUwvcqjda-PCTLmJeR_dFpa6c_ZH3SX13CIJY2tn6JqDlUVkOA3rMOZNINY6yFLGS6WvuEVxfdjomnTWMPB8_TgreZqF/4lj/8bl19ZHPQHWe2r0H6LMwUg/h1/h001.-WOajNVLmcd_X86RtCMvZHZM1CngBRVNR6tnjWMoqJ4) to find low-cost healthcare near you and potential assistance with medical bills.

**Additionally, I want to share resources for food and other essential needs**. Although SNAP and WIC payments should return when the government re-opens, Trump’s Big Budget Betrayal from earlier this year made huge cuts to SNAP that will continue impacting families across the country. clean_body: I’m fighting for affordable healthcare, including extending Affordable Care Act subsidies. | | | Friend, During the longest government shutdown in U.S. history, Republicans have cancelled votes and refused to show up to work, avoiding necessary negotiations to reopen the government. But this week, the Senate passed a deal to end the government shutdown , which is heading to the House of Representatives for a vote today. Unfortunately, this bill fails to prevent health insurance costs from skyrocketing for over 20 million Americans —including over half a million people in my state of Michigan. If Congress lets the Affordable Care Act subsidies expire at the end of 2025, millions of Americans will be unable to afford health insurance, and many will die. I’ve been hearing from constituents about how this disastrous move would impact them, including people who’ve said: “We are living paycheck to paycheck... I cannot afford to pay any increase in premiums,” and “I had to file bankruptcy due to medical bills. I just had two transplants and fear losing my healthcare.” I will keep fighting to renew these essential subsidies, reverse MAGA’s healthcare cuts, and ensure universal healthcare for all Americans. In the meantime, if you’re unable to afford the healthcare you need, I want to share resources to help you find more affordable healthcare , which you can also share with loved ones in need: Type in your zip code at to find low-cost healthcare near you and potential assistance with medical bills. Check out the National Association of Free & Charitable Clinics’ list of . Look up that provide free or income-based care. Call 211 or go to for assistance with healthcare, housing, food, and other expenses. See the for support paying for prescriptions and co-pays. See if there are mobile pop-up clinics near you offering free medical, dental, and vision care at . Use coupons for discounts on prescriptions. You can also get a free trial to check out GoodRx’s membership program with lower cost telehealth visits . Check out the to find doctors near you who offer the option to pay them a set monthly fee for access to them and their services. A may offer sliding-scale preventative care such as annual exams, vaccines, and screenings for cancer or STIs. Find free or low-cost eye exams and support with prescription glasses at . You can also find lower cost glasses at websites such as or . You can also find eye care or glasses at Sam’s Club and Costco, and you may find discounted dental or vision services at nearby dental schools or student eye institutes. Additionally, I want to share resources for food and other essential needs. Although SNAP and WIC payments should return when the government re-opens, Trump’s Big Budget Betrayal from earlier this year made huge cuts to SNAP that will continue impacting families across the country. You may find these links helpful, or perhaps you know someone you can share this with: Type your zip code into for a map of local food pantries and resources to help pay for transit, housing, and healthcare. Find a diaper bank providing free diapers near you: . Access or donate baby formula at . Find a nearby or that’s accessible to all. Buy low-cost surplus food from local grocery stores and restaurants through the , the (see for info about SNAP recipients getting $50 of free groceries), and the . And if you’re over 65 or disabled on Medicaid or Medicare, you may be eligible for free home-delivered meals with . See if you qualify . We’ll keep fighting together to guarantee food, healthcare, and housing as human rights. Always serving you, Rashida * Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Rashida Tlaib for Congress, please .

name: JB Pritzker
subject: This race could decide the Senate, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
Let me cut to the chase.
#
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97570?refcode=em20251112-9701&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9701%2E614464%2E2xWAKM)
Peter — JB Pritzker here.
Let me cut to the chase: **New Hampshire’s Senate race could decide whether Democrats take back the majority — _and Republicans know it._**
Donald Trump and the MAGA GOP see this seat as one of their best shots to expand their power.
They’ve already got a Republican governor in New Hampshire, and they’re betting they can flip the Senate seat next. If they win here, our chances of taking back the Senate drop to zero.
_We can’t let that happen._
Chris Pappas is a proven fighter for working families — taking on Big Pharma, defending reproductive freedom, and making sure our government actually works for the people it serves.
I’ve seen what happens when leaders like Chris have the platform and the power to make change — and _I want him in the Senate._
I know Chris can win, but there’s no way around it: **_This isn’t a safe blue seat, folks._**
The GOP is ready to flood New Hampshire with attack ads and dark money. The only way to beat them is with early, grassroots support. That’s where you come in, Peter:
**[If you can, please split a $15 donation right now between Chris Pappas and State Democrats Committee to put Democrats on the path to a Senate majority.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97571?refcode=em20251112-9701&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=2&akid=9701%2E614464%2E2xWAKM)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Contribute $5](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97571?refcode=em20251112-9701&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=5&t=3&akid=9701%2E614464%2E2xWAKM) [Contribute $15](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97571?refcode=em20251112-9701&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=4&akid=9701%2E614464%2E2xWAKM) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97571?refcode=em20251112-9701&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=5&akid=9701%2E614464%2E2xWAKM) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97571?refcode=em20251112-9701&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=6&akid=9701%2E614464%2E2xWAKM) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97571?refcode=em20251112-9701&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=7&akid=9701%2E614464%2E2xWAKM) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97571?refcode=em20251112-9701&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=8&akid=9701%2E614464%2E2xWAKM) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97570?refcode=em20251112-9701&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=9&akid=9701%2E614464%2E2xWAKM)

body:
In a bold move aimed at tightening the seams of America’s election security, Senator Bill Hagerty (R-TN) has introduced legislation that election integrity experts are calling a long-overdue response to a serious and largely overlooked vulnerability in U.S. federal law: the ability of foreign nationals to influence domestic political outcomes through indirect campaign activities and [...] | [](http://trk.libertyledger._ZD01In0/STpjEu8N8UZKmvU2rKYgmw)

---
In a bold move aimed at tightening the seams of America’s election security, Senator Bill Hagerty (R-TN) has introduced legislation that election integrity experts are calling a long-overdue response to a serious and largely overlooked vulnerability in U.S. federal law: the ability of foreign nationals to influence domestic political outcomes through indirect campaign activities and [...]

# **[Schumer Gets Tough Reality Check From CNN's Enten](http://trk.libertyledger._snkA)**
---
Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer is facing a full-blown revolt from within his own party — and the numbers are unforgiving. On CNN News Central Tuesday, chief data analyst Harry Enten delivered a brutal assessment: Chuck Schumer is now the least popular Democratic Senate leader on record — dating back to 1985. Drawing from Pew [...]
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger._snkA)
[](http://trk.libertyledger.)

[](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9In0/qLsV1l0oxx6_WgSE-1RsOw)
# **[U.S.-Colombia Intel Cooperation Paused After Drug Fallout](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9In0/qLsV1l0oxx6_WgSE-1RsOw)**
---
Colombian President Gustavo Petro — a far-left ideologue and staunch advocate for drug legalization — has officially suspended all intelligence cooperation with the United States, escalating an already fraught relationship over the U.S.’s intensified anti-narcotics operations in the Caribbean. Petro made the dramatic announcement Tuesday on his official Twitter account, directly citing a CNN report [...]
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9In0/qLsV1l0oxx6_WgSE-1RsOw)
[](http://trk.libertyledger._FGbKTEUUjA)
# **[Travelers Brace for More Disruptions in the Skies](http://trk.libertyledger._FGbKTEUUjA)**
---
As Congress inches toward a deal to end the government shutdown, the American public is facing an unfortunate reality: even if the government reopens tomorrow, the chaos in the skies and in shipping hubs will likely linger well into the holiday season. Already, thousands of flights have been delayed or canceled during the shutdown, and [...]
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger._FGbKTEUUjA)
[](http://trk.libertyledger.)
# **[Legal Storm Brews Between LSU and Former Coach Kelly](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
The fallout from LSU’s firing of Brian Kelly has now escalated into the legal realm, with the former head football coach threatening to sue the university if he does not receive the full $54 million buyout he claims he is owed. The dispute, already mired in controversy and political fallout, now stands as one of [...]
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.)
[](http://trk.libertyledger.-QyAgA)
# **[Trump Pushes New Use for Tariffs That's Sparking Debate](http://trk.libertyledger.-QyAgA)**
---
In a bold and characteristically Trumpian move, President Donald Trump announced on Monday a sweeping new economic proposal: use U.S. tariff revenue to send $2,000 dividend checks to the vast majority of Americans — and apply the remainder to reduce the national debt. The idea, laid out in a series of posts on Truth Social, [...]
